### vkiernan — 2012-09-04T14:53:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-49327>

Not really posting as not much is new. Still doing the progesterone and that seems to be going well. I am still getting warm when I eat BAB. Got to be related to the progesterone. Wasn't happening prior to. Went fishing yesterday with my bro and we did ok. We each caught about 7 gills. Not great, but didn't get skunked either.

Holy sleep last night. Fell asleep on the couch around 8, went to bed at 9 and woke at 5. WOW! If I could do that every night, I think I would get better. Seems like I sleep better thru the night the earlier I go to bed.

Today starts my few week's from hell at work. Fiscal year end and there is sooooo much work to be done. I just have to buck up and focus some how.

I see the doc in a 1.5 weeks. I will have to print out the hormone 101 blog and give it to her to explain why I am not taking the t4 with the Armour. Although, she does know that stress creates rt3 so I don't know why she would want me to take it knowing my stress level. I am going to talk to her about my thyroid and see if we can do something different. Also thinking it is about time to check my ASI. I believe it is a whole lot better. I wake up naturally every morning. I never use an alarm clock and I am tired at night. Seems like if I could do something to give me energy in the afternoon, I would be on a roll. I am wondering if it is time to do a little exercise? That really did help the last time I was exercising seriously. Perhaps this time, I do yoga or something like that. This visit will tell me if I need to check out Dr. Stanger (sp?) or not. She is still on my list of maybes. Maybe I need both.

I have been feeling better again lately. Seems like if my hormones are in better shape, I am not nearly as stressed as the little things don't bother me. Things flow much better at work and at home. I'm sure dh notices too.

The one thing I have to figure out is how do get my body to not get that whacked out feeling when I am soooo hungry? It usually happens in the evening if I don't get dinner on time. I absolutely hate it. It is this "got to eat" feeling and it makes me crabby if I don't eat. I don't understand why after all this time, I still have it?????

### 2bls — 2012-09-04T20:33:35+0300

vkiernan, I just popped in to say hi and thanks for mentioning OAT axis imbalance in your OP. My LR since May has uncovered a severe adrenal issue and since doing reading on OAT axis imbalance on DrLam.com I thought I would come to the forums here to see if anyone else has experience with OAT axis imbalance. I still have to get the saliva testing but my history of health issues from childhood could be a textbook history of OAT axis imbalance. I think you might be the only person on the boards here that even knows what that is. Just wanted to say thanks for your thoughts and experiences, I hope to start my road to recovery very soon. All the best

### vkiernan — 2012-09-04T20:47:02+0300

You sound like my twin. I wish you the best too. Please pop in and let me now what and how you are doing. Seems like my biggest helper has been the sun in the early am and CT'ing. Now that my estrogen has been reduced, progesterone is actually working. Just a thought for you, Jack says AF is usually a brain problem more so than an adrenal problem. I have been getting the early am sun as much as possible and using my sunglasses or whatever to assist in the evenings to keep my rhythm going. After I really thought about what he said, I realized when I went totally in the tank and what helped me get there and it made a lot of sense. I had adrenal issues prior but a few things really threw me over the edge. One thing was working my 2nd job and getting new software that I had to spend all my nights working on it to get the acctg. portion set up. Literally, every night after my primary job (sitting in front of a computer) I would go there and spend the whole evening glaring in front of a computer until 10 or so for a few months. This was also just after I got divorced. That is when my world got real bad.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-09T00:18:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50324>

Well after my consult today the news is not so good. Celiac and hashimotos. Got to get another test to confirm. I failed to mention to Jack that I have the gene for celiac. Or, at least that's what was told to me last Oct. when I had the stomach thing. Now I really have to find a good doc to help me with my gut and hormone balancing. SOMEDAYS AREN'T WORTH GETTING OUT OF BED! I am really bummed as I had tests in the past for celiac but found that they were worthless when you aren't eating the stuff. DH said we need to do the other tests so I will be doing a little more research to get them asap. I really thought I did not have hashis either. If you want to live in a dream, don't get the GI Effects test done. It will tell you everything you don't want to know and then some!!! Ok, off my rant.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-09T00:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50328>

Hey V, sorry to hear. Lyndra taught me how to handle these moments...... knowledge is power. Now you know what you need to do to get well. And I know it sucks. A LOT. Some days it's most comforting to stay in bed or go back to bad... nothing wrong with that. But I do think it was worth you getting out of bed today.... b/c now you know and can make a plan.

### Lyndra — 2012-09-09T02:15:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50344>

I know about the roller-coaster effect (as I call it) - feeling up, then feeling down, getting good news, then hearing bad. Rinse and repeat. It's so frustrating and demoralizing. But as a wise man used to tell me at work when I had a problem, you've got two choices - deal with it or leave. Not quite as applicable here, but not only do I think knowledge is power, I know that doing \*something\* towards your goal is better than doing nothing. Take a brief bit of time to grieve, then regroup and then get back on track. Finding out now seems to be a lot better than not knowing until you're too sick to get better. Hang in there! You've already made such great progress and you have the knowledge and strength to move forward.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-09T03:52:45+0300

### vkiernan — 2012-09-09T04:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50404>

> **Cavemam;50930 wrote:** So sorry! It IS hard to deal with. Your initial reaction is appropriate - but like most things, thought abought how awful it is - they are worse than the reality.
>
>
>
> Would you mind sharing what it was in your labs and symptoms that made him think hashi's? I have my GI test hat ready to go when i can get the family out of the house - so i would love to hear more about what this told you!

Ok, don't quote me but anyone else correct me if I am wrong. I think it was because my Anti-gliadin sigA was high. This is the marker for celiac and I think the two of them can go hand in hand. I do have to research this more. I was not able to record my skype session and half of everything he said had no volume and I sometimes I had to read his lips. I was bummed it ended up this way. This would have been the best consult to record. I tried to take a lot of notes.

The test is easy. Only takes a few minutes in the bathroom. The hardest part is not peeing in the dish with the poo. This test is great as it does tell you a lot. Told me I have a fat absorption issue and all the good fat from my food (can we say dha) is not going where it is supposed to be going. My brain is not getting what it needs. I def. caught that part. My large colon stinks but I think he said the rest wasn't too bad. He does think I am making some progress though with the gut and this really is my biggest problem along with my pg/e2 ratio which plays a roll in the gut as he mentioned somewhere just not too long ago. That was a biggie when I heard it since my est has been so high and pg so low and our gut needs it. He was also able to tell me why I have not gained a lot of weight and that was due to my adiposity index which is the firmicutes and bacteroidetes. I do need some work there though too.

### Lyndra — 2012-09-09T05:04:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50420>

Gratitude is a good thing to focus on right now. And thinking about what is still available to you, not what you have to give up (all things I am telling myself). I've also ditched eggs & dairy, at least for a while, and I know it gets old to constantly keep giving things up. But remember, it is all a choice, something you choose to do, for yourself, and your health. Nothing is being imposed, it's all within your control. Those are the thoughts that help me and I hope may help you.

### Patty Cakes — 2012-09-09T05:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50430>

> **vkiernan;50970 wrote:** Thanks Patty!!! For hashis, are eggs out? I have given dairy and grains up for the most part but I was not 100%. Now I HAVE to be and I realize that. I did have just a little cheese every now and then and now that is out. Guess I have to focus on what I can have and be grateful things are not worse.

Yes eggs are out but life's really not that bad without eggs. It took me a few months to adjust my thoughts on eggs. Now I just don't even think about it. I hope someday I will be able to eat them again. Nuts are supposed to be out too but that is one thing I'm having a hard time giving up..not sure why either. Cheese was a toughie too. The only cheese we have in the house is sheep and a little goat. I have it for DH and now after 9 months I rarely think about it. Eating more fish, as weird as this may sound, has made me not really think about cheese or eggs at all. I usually pan fry the fish with a lower flame so I don't think about cooking it with eggs or having cheese with it.

I think the eggs, nuts, dairy, nightshades, etc all are inflammatory which is bad for any autoimmune disease. Maybe there was more to it but the reality is I'm pretty strict with everything except the darn nuts. But actually I am getting better and eating less lately. So someday I will get there.

Being grateful is a good place to be, like Lyndra said. Remember one step at a time and you'll get there! You just keep getting better!

Patty, This helps for sure and I am so glad I have people like you out here to help. Sucks that we have to live this way but at least we are not alone. Any other way of looking at things is beneficial. It is tough living in the land of dairy and not being able to partake. My dh is a dairy freak. I will really have to work on substitutions.

Last night was interesting, we went to a seminar and they had food (of course) and my dh said to me "you aren't eating, they have shrimp;" yes, they had shrimp but it had some sauce all over it and god only knows what is in the sauce. I guess I have to continually remind him.

I don't remember, what are you doing for thyroid meds since you have hashis?

### Lyndra — 2012-09-09T19:06:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50513>

> **Dali Dula;51028 wrote:** The sauces are evil especially if they are shiny. Shiny sauces are sugar and/or cornstarch. Run.

If not cornstarch, then gluten to thicken up the sauce, not to mention the bad oils in the mayo base. I've gotten to the point that I don't think of that stuff as "food."

On a tangent, I've been told that duck eggs are less problematic than chicken eggs. My plan was to stay off eggs & dairy for at least a couple of weeks, then add back in duck eggs and see if I can detect any difference in how I feel. I will say my joints are feeling better after almost 2 weeks without eggs & butter and I may continue down this road for a while longer to see if I improve further. I'm too young to not have functional hands .

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-09-09T19:53:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50522>

V, when I quit eggs, I was a different person in 3 days! You have to try it. I know it's hard, but so worth it. It made all the difference in how I feel and on energy level. Jennifer posted once that the problem with eggs is in the white: there is something toxic in it in order to protect the yolk. For most of people,it won't have any bad effects, but for those with AI condition, it's highly inflamatory.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-10T05:06:43+0300

Posting for later review since I have this problem.

> Some one asked a good question today at the blog and I want to share it here: From the Hormone 101 blog post key point:
>
> Dr. Mitch Moore asksJack so I re=read the H101 post. I read it to say high stress causes HIGH CORTISOL which, due to pregnenolone steal, causes decreased T3, sex hormones, sarcopenia and osteopenia.
>
> But you said my cortisol was LOW..and you wanted it higher
>
> So low cortisol could be a symptom also of pregnenelone steal â€“ right? And increasing T3 and vit D is the key to making more pregnenelone and other hormones as well â€“ right? Why is low Vitamin D levels an epidemic?
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> Answer: @Mitch when the effect is CHRONIC the decision in the cell is always onâ€¦..meaning all the pregnenolone shunts to cortisol to hep you survive. When we measure it in the plasma it is lowâ€¦..that is a sign the PVN nucelus is working over time and this is sign your oxidizing your cells. The result is all the hormones going the other way in the hormone synthesis chain are very lowâ€¦â€¦..that is the reduction path. Re Read BG 11. Low cortisol= low melatonin = epithelial cancers= LR. Oxidation =LR. Low cortisol is not a good thing. When the process first beginsâ€¦â€¦ACUTELY you will have hyper cortisolism for a time until you fatigue your PVN nucleus output chemicals in the hypothalamus. Do it long enough and you oxidizes (age) your body, your sleep declines and your body fails with your body composition.
>
>
>
> The critical equation of life = LDL + T3 = pregnenolone. If free T3 is low you cant make pregnenolone. If you live a life in the modern world you are in survival mode (living in the cortisol pathway of oxidation) shunting the already low amount of pregnenolone your cells can make to cortisol at the sake of making progesterone. Progesterone is the base hormone for the testosterone, estrogen, DHEA, etc (the hormones of fertility)â€¦â€¦..The more we shunt to survival mode hormones the less fertility ones we make. More survival path activation of cortisol makes the cell more oxidized the faster the cell agesâ€¦â€¦.Moreover, the more oxidized the cell becomes the more Vitamin D has to be used to offset this increased oxidation by the immune system so Vitamin D levels also falls with oxidation. I hope this clarifies it. This is why modern humans are infertile (1 in 7 couples) because to have a child you must be more reduced than oxidized because it favors a higher level of the pro- GESTATION hormone called progesterone. Most modern humans are starved for progesterone because modern life keeps them in the survival pathway of cortisol. The cell always has to chose between survival or reproduction and inflammation is the traffic cop for the decision. Hence why we see upside down PG/E2 ratios in all modern neolithic diseases. when this happens the lab panel show increased HS CRP, increased E2, and LR, with LOW free T3 levels. Lowered Vitamin D levels are an epidemic because modern life forces us to live in a constant state of oxidation of the survival pathway (cortisol). I hope this answers it clearly now.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-10T14:56:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-50681>

Monday, Sept. 10th.

Today is my first day w/o eggs. Ate a lot of pork loin and some cauli. I lived! I also am out of Kombucha until this 2nd ferment is ready. We will see how my stomach fairs. I'm thinking the komb might be causing my bloat. Take more things out and the stomach bloats more. Doesn't make sense.

Will be seeing the doc this Thursday and will see if she has the cyrex tests or if I need to call my chiro. I'm pretty sure he can get them. I am excited about getting these done. I need to know how bad things really are. I am amazed at how much info is in the gi test. I will be doing that one again way down the road to see how well I am progressing. You can think you are better, but testing really tells all. I don't know how some of us ever thought we could get better w/out testing. Jack is so right! There is no way I could ever really get better without having done some of the tests I have. Now I clearly understand why some of us become LS and some of us don't.

I am a little confused though that I might have hashis as my 2 tests that have been done a couple of times have been low. But, I may have hit the good times and not the bad and since it waxes and wanes. I also know that 2 weeks after my very first thyroid test, my results were so much better than the first and I had only been on t4 for 1.5 weeks. From what I understand, this should not work that way. So, hashis? Probably so!

Hopefully my ox bile salts will be here today. I only ordered them over a week ago. It's taking a long time to get here! I want my fat to be absorbed; my poor brain needs it. What am I really going to feel like when I get all this stuff fixed???? Jack says most of my problems are in my gut and my hormones. I CAN find docs to help me with both of these! I will make that first call today. Pretty sure my current doc won't be giving me t3 only but I will certainly plead my case! She is open somewhat so gotta ask no matter what. I think I also need to ask to have my pg/e2 ratio measured. I am thinking I need more progesterone. What is interesting is that someone wrote somewhere that milk thistle or Dim (thinking it was milk thistle though) created breast tenderness. Since I quit taking it a few weeks now, mine is gone for now. We'll see in a week or so.

Off to get ready for work now. Have a very full week ahead of me.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-14T15:09:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-51630>

It is time I do a recap from from my last consult and where I am today. I have put bits and pieces out here and more in one recap would be more beneficial for me to look back on.

Where I am today vs. 1 year ago plus 5 months when my true journey started (it actually started in 2009 but not much progress happened). I was waking up almost every day with really dark moods. Please don't smile at me cuz I ain't smiling back! Then I got tired of not being able to breathe thru my nose and decided to have my deviated septum fixed. That was last spring. When he got in there he also made my holes bigger and did some reconstructive as it was crushing down on itself. After that, my recovery was not quick. I couldn't figure it out. Why was it taking so long to feel better after "nose" surgery. I knew at that moment that I had a problem. A lot of things happened to me the prior year and I couldn't figure that out either. I found out that this doc is also involved with the thyroid (actually he only cuts them out but that was good enough for me). So when I went back for a checkup I asked for a thyroid test and he gladly obliged. In a few days, he called and I knew what he was going to say; yes, I have a thyroid problem and I should go see my doc. Well, my doc at that time didn't stay my doc for long. I started the t4 that he gave me and then started my journey with JK at MDA. Life changer.

Long story short, where am I today? Today I no longer wake up with dark clouds hanging over my head almost every day. Being fairly diligent about everything and trying so many things and not having success has made me spend some money and finally figure out what the hell is going on. So far, it has been the best money I have ever spent. I wish I would have really understood last summer why testing is sooo important. I kept waiting for my leptin to reset when the whole time nothing was going to reset anytime soon. I needed testing and finally got some and got some more and then more and I'm still not done. This battle isn't over but I have clues now that I would have NEVER had if I wouldn't have found Jack and read my brains out.

The biggest tests for me were the ASI with hormone panel and the GI Effects test. Next will be the gluten and food test from EnteroLab. Need to confirm the gluten issue and know what the hell I can eat.

So let's recap what the tests revealed: Adrenal Fatigue - stage 7 (very last stage where cortisol and dhea are completely tanked); Gluten issue whether celiac or not; Thyroid problem - still questioning if it is Hashis at this point(Jack thinks so); Fat malabsorption; trashed colon; PG/E2 upside down ratio; and I know the gi e test showed a few more things but I don't completely understand them yet.

So the list seems a little overwhelming but on a positive note, I wasn't diagnosed with them all at once. I have had time to get adjusted to them little by little. Also, when you are working on something and not really getting better, this tells you more is wrong.

What I am doing today to solve my problems:

I am looking at the sun when it comes up to help my adrenals and if I watch tv, glasses on for sure.

I am now switching to t3 only.

I am taking ox bile for the fat malabsorption.

I am adding probiotics to my meals for my gut like kombucha and sauerkraut (Jack gave me a list of things to eat to assist but the only one I caught was kimchi). His voice kept cutting out. I am bummed over that missed opportunity.

I have cleaned up my diet and removed my beloved eggs and no more little dairy cheats but I am also trying to add some veggies back (of course non-starchy).

I eat sardines every day for lunch to ensure I get some fish and I add turmeric, mustard and mac nut oil to it. I do have to eat more real fish too though. I should really think about this for BAB.

I am using progesterone cream and working on balancing my ratio.

I take enzymes before most meals.

I am running the air in my truck at 60 where ever I go

I am going to get back into CT'ing. It has been a month since last one. I think this would really help balance my pg/e2 ratio since it clearly detoxes the estrogen from my body.

I have started progesterone to fix my ratio.

I go to bed before 10 every night and get up around 6 every morning.

I take specific supplements like dgl licorice, d3, k2 & a, etc.

I am still a work in process for sure. My success will be when I am happy & energetic thruout the day. I am still tired a lot in the afternoon and evening. I have to think though if I keep working on the gut, keep my diet clean, get my pg/e2 ratio in line that things will start to look up for me. None of this can be fixed over night and I know it. Since I finally have some answers it makes staying the course so much easier. I am looking forward to my future now that I think I have one.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-14T20:33:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-51667>

The ox bile is apparently working. The big D 3 am's in a row. That part isn't good, even cut back on it already. But, it didn't float today so I am happy about that. I am happier today - maybe cuz it is Friday! I'll take it whatever. Could be from removing eggs from the diet too. I am not even really thinking about them when I make BAB. Kinda strange, I see them in the fridge and the thought does not go there to take them out and cook them.

I wonder when my body starts absorbing the fat if my skin will get even better! I am thinking I might actually be happier as my brain starts getting what it needs too. Another thought is, how many nutrients are fat soluble and couldn't benefit me. Sure hope this does the trick.

Guess we have to look at our journey and realize it truly is a journey and lots of things are going to change; I really want to relish in the good things that happen from the changes. I don't want the little things to just pass me by. We are all working so hard to fix our brokens and every step of progress is important.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-17T20:40:25+0300

Monday, Sept. 17th

Doing ok today, not happy and not sad. Ordered my Enterolab test for gluten and other offending foods. Added fat malabsorption to it to see how badly things are damaged inside even though I know I have it. I will know what is triggering things in me. Backed off the oxbile.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-19T03:43:01+0300

I had the GI test done and Dr K said I might have celiacs and Hashis might be lurking in the back ground as well. Thanks! - and yea - I just gave up eggs three days ago and I think I do feel better........

Hi Meg. Glad you are out here with us. Jack is the path to optimal. It isn't an easy one but it will get you there if you persevere. Yeah for dropping eggs. I have already given up so much that it was very tough for me to do. But, within a day or two, I never gave it another thought. Also, good that you are not low on all levels with the AF. How is your DHEA? That tells a lot. If you start on ox bile, go very slooow. I found out the hard way with the runs. Had no idea when Jack told me to go slow and titrate that that's what he meant. Get a low dose and build from there. As our gut heals, this is supposed to go away and we shouldn't need it as long as we have our gallbladder.

For the celiac, I am just doing another stool test from Enterolabs that measures the Anti-gliadin slgA. This does not confirm celiac but rather that I have a gluten intolerance. I already know I do have gi and the gene for celiac but I want another measure and this test also will tell me if I can eat eggs or dairy, etc. I need to know what is causing my imflammation and I'm sure it is probably due to those. I'm just hoping I can add 1 thing back. I have also added the fat malabsorption to it as this one supposedly measures how much damage has been done to the small intestine by how high your result goes. Don't quote me on that cuz my memory is bad but that is the jist of it. You can go out to enterolab.com and read all about it. It talks about celiac and gluten intolerance. This test is pretty expensive but I want to know!!! Over the long run, it is peanuts as my health is worth more. I'm not saying if I can eat yogurt that I'm going to do it all the time but every now and then would be fantastic.

As for glucose issues, I have always had very low bs like around 70 for quite a few years and only more recently has it climbed. I can tell you that sometimes if I don't eat when I am hungry that it can hit me quite hard and make me very cranky. I am hoping as I get things fixed, that will go to.

What is interesting for me is that all of my tests for Hashi's has been negative. So I don't know if I have it or not but I am going to eat by what this test results tell me.

Crazy hormones is right! I have had enough of them already. Working on that though and my new doc is doing her best to help me.

Please stop by and let me know how things are going. I would be glad to help however I can.

I don't know if there is a better route for testing. Biopsy perhaps. Just don't count on the results if you have not been eating grains. Enterolabs does a pretty good job of explaining it all. If testing for celiac is going to cost you money then I'm sure you want to go there. We should ask Jack but I think the only thing it will tell you is if your villi is damaged or not. If we are gluten intolerant, we should never eat grains ever anyway is what I have concluded. I think one of my son's has it too. He just has to come up with the money for testing. He didn't work enough hours this year to get insurance so he is pretty stuck right now. It is either celiac or a really trashed gut from the time he was a baby. He was antib. all the time for pnuemonia and bronchitis. I had no idea I was doing that to him.

My LCFA was 11.8 and total fat was 15. I think the ox bile is starting to work. They sunk twice now. I am going to add a dose in a dinner now but still go slow. I'm laughing right now as I never thought I would be discussing my pooh on a forum. My anti-gliadin was 7.1 so not so out of range but out none-the-less. Also, the anti-g will continue to remain high for 1-2 years according to enterolab after you quit grains. You should read out there, it will really shed some light for you.

On a positive note, I had candida as far as I know and the gi e test said none at all. I'm pretty happy about that. Not sure if I ever had it in the first place but I would guess I did the way I loved sugar. Sugar is "finally" not getting the better of me. We always have chocolate in the dish here at work and it is actually becoming far less attractive. I look at it like complete junk. Pretty amazing for me. What are you doing for the Candida?

If you have adrenal insufficiency, you'll be low in aldosterone which along with ADH controls body hydration levels. Some symptoms you may have include being lightheaded when you stand up and being incredibly thirsty or craving salt. In my case, I was getting up 2-3 times every night despite not drinking more than 4 oz of water after 6 pm. Now that my adrenals are coming back, I'm only getting up once and someday hope to make that zero.

Tuesday, Sept. 25th

Been on T3 only since Sunday. Going ok as far as I can tell although sleep has not been too good the last 2 On the bright side, this is the first pms week that wasn't! OMG, I can't believe I was able to be in control the whole week. Even one event that should have ticked me off didn't. I was able to explain my side and walk away ok. Huge win for sure. Got to be that I finally got rid of enough estrogen to make that change. I will be working on getting rid of more too. Plan to take my DIM with my CT. Yes, I have to get back at it. I am still cold and that makes it so tough.

I have decided that since she won't give me more t3 I will load up on D3 and silymarin and try to knock out the rt3 as best as I can. I see her in 3 weeks anyway and we'll see how it goes.

Fluke or? Within days of starting the ox bile, I got the big D for days in a row. Backed way down and then finally off and a few things happened. Went from D to C to D and stomach pain and C and D some more. Starting to think my gut flora might be changing. No oxbile since last Thursday and D today after C. Will have to wait on Enterolab test until things settle down. Perhaps the progesterone is assisting me there too. Stopped the enzymes too. I think I need some w/out the HCL in them even though a small amount.

Still taking normal supps. No eggs in about 1.5 weeks or so. No coffee. Doing tea only. Very small amount of 99% dark chocolate but plan on stopping that completely asap. ### vkiernan — 2012-10-01T20:51:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-55632>

Monday, Oct. 1st

Doubled my t3 on Saturday but didn't notice any effect. Worked on Sat. & Sun. outside on the house all day and did get a lot done. Was a beautiful weekend. So glad I spent it outside rather than inside reading all of this stuff. I did a lot of shoveling and moving of pavers. DH and I work really well on projects. We seem to balance each other and come up with great solutions. I did notice I wasn't sore from this weekend so that has to be a good thing. Last weekend I hemmed 6 prs of dh's pants. Feels like I am getting a few things done. This weekend I also make some veggies for the week, bottled my kombucha, made some sausage and froze it up for BAB's.

Think it is about time to put the laptop aside and start living my life. Perhaps some of my stress will go down if I quit worrying about it all the damn time. Not much is changing anyway. I keep doing and not much changes. Tells me there is more to do or change but what? Getting frustrated with the whole scene. MHS are not on my list either. I eat sardines usually everyday for lunch and fish or shrimp quite a few times a week. Maybe not enough for my lacking brain but more than before. I have thought about eating fish for BAB but would have to eat a lot to tide me over until noon. I should bake up some cod since I really like it.

I actually watched a movie with the dh last night. First time in a while. Wore my light blocking glasses. Somehow I was able to stay up til 10pm. This is pretty much unheard of in my life. By 9 I am usually DONE! Even slept unitl 5 and then dozed a little more before getting up at 6. Maybe the t3? Who knows!

Sent off my Enterolab test this am. Forgot to put it into the ziploc bag so who knows what will happen. Might be a very yucky mess by the time it arrives there. I really hope it makes it ok as I don't want to repeat this one too. This will be a long couple of weeks to wait for the results. I am hoping for good results.

Been having little headaches lately. Thinking it is from the lack of t4. Will have to watch it. I'm not convinced yet that t3 only is for me. I just started to get a little happy before switching and it isn't back yet. I hope I am not setting myself back instead of moving forward.

Not sure if doc is going to give me more t3 or not. She is a little nervous about the whole thing but has never used that protocol either. Can't say that I blame her. She knows I am looking for something to clear my rt3 so who knows what she might come up with. I do have to get more silymarin and heavily dose that while doing this as that is supposed to help clear it. That just might speed up my progress. I am also slamming the d3 and k2.

It sure doesn't take much to fill me up for lunch. A little kraut and a can of sardines and I am FULL! Hell, I was full after the kraut. I did drink a little kombucha too though.

I don't know what is up but I have been dreaming about babies. Not really sure if they are mine or not. This has happened at least 5 or 6 times in the last couple of weeks. Really weird. I dream about nursing them, trying to hold them, clean up their messes, etc.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-01T21:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-55639>

> **vkiernan;56788 wrote:**
>
> I don't know what is up but I have been dreaming about babies. Not really sure if they are mine or not. This has happened at least 5 or 6 times in the last couple of weeks. Really weird. I dream about nursing them, trying to hold them, clean up their messes, etc.

Maybe you feel like you're finally starting to take care of your vulnerable self . Sounds like you are.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-02T02:11:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-55710>

Lauren, Interesting take. Let's hope so. It sure has made me wonder what is up with it. My son recently got married and I want to call ask him if she is pg!!!

### vkiernan — 2012-10-03T20:54:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56100>

Jack says in response to a newbie:

> When we have people who are obese or LR this is the best time to use protein for a short period (12 months) to control cravings and slowly ramp up fats as the protein falls......as one approaches optimal......how best to do this naturally? Start the epi paleo template as soon as cravings for carbs are controlled. How do you accelerate weight loss with the template change? You add CT as you go........it is layered appraoch that takes finesse.......and discipline but it is not hard to do when you understand why.

Ok, I have been doing the leptin reset for 16 months. Does this mean I am doing harm by still eating all this protein which I know from my consult that I am not assimilating it anyway? I have never read anything about doing it for 12 months or ramping up the fat and dropping some protein after that. So -wth???? As for reaching optimal, no way am I even close. And, I have been pounding the enzymes and sauerkraut and a little kombucha and I still have veggie particles coming out whole. I already dosed out on HCL and know I don't need anymore of that unless something changed recently and I need to attempt it again. This is sooo FRUSTRATING to have to figure this out on my own not to mention the rules keep changing.

Stuff is obviously not working for me.... Was reading a lot about Hashi's and I can't figure out why I have never gone hyper. Or, is this a rt3 problem. Might need to ask Jack.

I had my ultrasound yesterday. Have no idea what to expect. My doc is reading about dosing with T3 so that is favorable so far.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-06T23:19:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56628>

> **vkiernan;56788 wrote:**
>
> Been having little headaches lately. Thinking it is from the lack of t4. Will have to watch it. I'm not convinced yet that t3 only is for me. I just started to get a little happy before switching and it isn't back yet. I hope I am not setting myself back instead of moving forward.

Sorry to hear about the headaches. I had to quit cytomel because it gave me a mild but constant headache. I switched to Armour and the headaches went away. Good luck getting everything sorted out.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-07T15:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56692>

Hi Lyndra, thanks for stopping by and giving me some feedback; always appreciated! The headaches seem to be a little better now.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-07T15:55:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56696>

Sunday, Oct. 7th

What an ending to a week. I thought I was going to lose my Dad; already lost my Mom. He has been losing blood for over a year and keeps getting sick with infections of the blood and bladder so they couldn't scope him. Well, it finally came down they had to scope him or he was not going to make it. All started with him seeing his doc on Wednesday and he said he didn't look good so take him to the emergency room. Went downhill from there. He got there at 11 am and they didn't give him any water or anything until 8:30 that night. In the meantime, he slowly became unresponsive and they put him up in ICU. He stayed unresponsive until Friday morning when he was able to answer just a few questions. They had to give him a lot of plasma and blood along with everything else they were putting into him. What they found was severe gastritis and a tear where the esophagus meets the stomach. They said the tear just happened and probably was from vomiting up the blood that was pooling. They also said that the blood he has been losing for the past year was from the gastritis. Wow, I sure hope this fixes him. He was actually sitting up last night and his wife was feeding him. That was huge for us. No one stills knows why he became unresponsive. He told me last night that he thought he was a gonner. Really hard to hear. Maybe later this week he can elaborate when he is not so tired.

My Dad is one of these guys who is usually always happy and has great stories to tell. His stories are never about how bad they had it but more about everything he did in his life whether it be how fun and exciting or not so fun in some cases. I love my Dad and it would have been a very sad day for sure. Kinda woke me up a bit. I realize I had better forget about my own woes and visit my Dad more just because I don't know what tomorrow will bring. It is very tough for me talk with him as I have this voice problem and he has a hearing problem so between the 2 of us we struggle. I told him the other day that he can't leave me, I want more stories. When he is over for dinner, the usual happens. I cook like crazy and then we eat and then it is time for clean up and I miss it all. Gotta change the method a bit here and maybe leave clean up till the next day.

I'm pretty exhausted from the whole thing but slept great the last 2 nights. I have a lot of work to do today including my job. I have the worker's comp auditor and the financial auditors coming tomorrow and I am now behind and not prepared. A little stressed for sure. I need to get laundry done, buy and cook some food so I have something to eat this week and clean the house up a bit. So much to do and so little time to do it. I should go see my pop too today.

Was able to start the ox bile again as I completed my Enterolabl test last weekend. Things are still floating which means no absoption yet and I have been on it all week. Must not work overnight. I'm afraid to up it and take more than 1 in the am but looks like I might have too. I need to understand how this works and if I have to take it with every fatty meal or if once a day is enough. No results on test and am patiently waiting. Also, thru all of this my doc called and I missed it. No call log either. I'm guessing it is about my ultrasound and I'm just a bit nervous that they didn't say a thing but to call her back. They took a ton of pics and she was highlighting a lot of areas. Hoping this is normal. Don't need another thing to add to my list! My doc also didn't respond yet about the t3 upping. This makes me think something is up unless that is why the nurse was calling me. I know I shouldn't stress over this until I know but my voice has been bad for so long along with the sore throats that it makes me think the worst. I saw a small glimmer of hope a while back with my voice but it didn't last but for a very short time.

Decided to have coffee this am and I think it is not for me. I am having just a little heart fluttering going on and that is drinking it with my BAB and lots of fat. I love my coffee but seems like tea might be the better choice.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T15:58:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56699>

> **vkiernan;57351 wrote:** Jack says in response to a newbie:
>
>
>
>
>
> Ok, I have been doing the leptin reset for 16 months. Does this mean I am doing harm by still eating all this protein which I know from my consult that I am not assimilating it anyway? I have never read anything about doing it for 12 months or ramping up the fat and dropping some protein after that. So -wth???? As for reaching optimal, no way am I even close. And, I have been pounding the enzymes and sauerkraut and a little kombucha and I still have veggie particles coming out whole. I already dosed out on HCL and know I don't need anymore of that unless something changed recently and I need to attempt it again. This is sooo FRUSTRATING to have to figure this out on my own not to mention the rules keep changing.
>
>
>
> Stuff is obviously not working for me.... Was reading a lot about Hashi's and I can't figure out why I have never gone hyper. Or, is this a rt3 problem. Might need to ask Jack.
>
>
>
> I had my ultrasound yesterday. Have no idea what to expect. My doc is reading about dosing with T3 so that is favorable so far.

I completely commiserate here with you. It brings me back to thinking about Cavemam's comment that she would wait until next year's optimal is released to continue!! I think Jack had great results, because he put all the pieces together, knew which labs to watch carefully, and got dramatic results quickly. We all started with LR, hoping for good results. Then added in the CT, hoping to up the game. Meanwhile, it became clear that hormones need to be pretty damn good to get results too. Then the seafood (but not with CO!), and the iodine, and now the light. To be fair, all of these factors were laid out earlier, but we were not focussing on them as a group yet. Since a lot of us are struggling (well, at least me), to keep up with all the biology, our assimilation is limited, and it's easy to focus on the current talk of the town, rather than re-reading old stuff for more tidbits about what's to come (eating the placentas from your birth month was Cavemam's next hypothesis!! Still makes me smile!). So now, we've been trying to fix ourselves for a very long time (not to mention all the time before finding JK), achieving limited results at best, and we're starting to get frustrated (maybe I'm just speaking for myself here though) that it's never "enough." To be clear, I'm not upset with JK. He has presented soo much information for "free". I have no complaints whatsoever in that regard, only gratitude. This is more an inward complaint with myself. I find myself waiting to look around the next corner, or the one after that. That will make this whole thing come together. Realistically, it probably won't. It hasn't yet. I need to better understand my own unique problem to better create a solution for it (I'm heading towards improving hormones, and then gutting my microbiota at the moment).

Also, I've never any symptoms of hyper-thyroid either, and I've been Hashi-ing away for 13 years.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T16:03:57+0300

### vkiernan — 2012-10-07T16:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56703>

BKW, actually I have been on board since day 1 although I waited a few weeks to actually start the reset and this was 7-3-11. I believe the first blog was written in June and I started reading then. It wasn't all laid out and the rules have changed. I'm sure he had a good reason and I, too, am extremely grateful for him. I get very frustrated though that it isn't working for me; or perhaps it is and I just don't know it. My moods are not as bad and I sometimes get a ray of happines so something I guess. Not quitting as I know I can't cuz I am too broken. I would have ate a lot more fish back then but that wasn't the plan; pastured was the way too go. CT and the circadian cycle wasn't brought up until this year and that was probably a good thing cuz I don't know if I could have grasped it all. I have been on the reset for a long time and if we are not supposed to eat the BAB for this long I want to know that and also know what I am supposed to do. We can't question what we haven't a clue about and I am trying my damdest to follow the rules. Any you can't question everything cuz no one else gets it like Jack!!!!!

I feel like I have let a WHOLE YEAR go by and what have I accomplished?!! Yes, I am finally figuring out what is wrong but can't get better unless I have a good doc on board to help me. This has been a baby step thing for sure and I guess I am a little upset that Jack didn't choose me to come down there. I have been begging him for a year to come see him and then to not be chosen is a little disheartening. They said first come first serve; I e-mailed him asap after the webinar when it was announced but I guess I was too late. I need to be fixed by someone who gets it and then I would have a clue to what I truly need; then I could train someone here to help me finish the task. Now I am stuck again. Lots of people out here can retain what he writes; I cannot and feel like I am being punished. I read and do my homework but cannot retain this stuff so therefore I am left behind and "ditzy". I need him to be my doc and help me get there so I can retain. What he is not thinking about it is those of us who are broken in our brains and will not get better until some fixing gets done by him. We desperately need his help. We are in this vicous little catch 22 cycle and can't seem to break it. Sort of like the "rich get richer" and the "sick stay sick." I sincerely hope who he choose is a lot more broken than I am and need his help more than I do and that is how I am going to look at this.

Don't get me wrong, I owe my life to Jack for all his is doing; problem is without him as my doc, I am stuck with my poor brain ways. I just really wanted him for my doc and was willing to spend any money I had to!!!!

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T17:25:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56718>

I was on "the list" too, to become a patient. When the email about the details came out, I thought long and hard about it. I sent the message to my parents to ask their opinion (they know what I've been going through). My parents pointed out a lot of issues/drawbacks/concerns about the program as it was described. I think their concerns were valid, although I can also see that I would have to enter into this kind of a program with a certain amount of "good faith." In the end, I decided not to respond to be considered a candidate. I received plenty of advice from my consult, and according to Jack, these kinds of changes take time to show a difference. Becoming a patient would make it all a lot more streamlined, (getting the right labs, getting the right scripts, getting the right interpretation) that's for sure.

I can't remember - how long have you had Hashi's? Do you have positive antibody tests? Jack made a comment once that he thinks I have delayed onset type 1 diabetes. Well, he may be right, but so far no labs back the claim (and I've been tested). I'm starting to realize I'm on an unsustainable path (optimal or not). My marriage is feeling the heat, my friends think I'm crazy, I'm losing out on enjoyable time with my kids. I've becomed consumed by my (lacking, non-optimal) health. I spent so much time with low FT3, ketogenic, and low energy, eating my seafood and waiting for things to turn around. Then comes the iodine blog, explaining that when carbs are low, you have to compensate with iodine! Ack! Meanwhile, an apple or 2 a day would have made me a much better mother/wife (although not optimal!). I am not good at balance...

### vkiernan — 2012-10-07T18:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56724>

> **bigknitwit;58019 wrote:** I was on "the list" too, to become a patient. When the email about the details came out, I thought long and hard about it. I sent the message to my parents to ask their opinion (they know what I've been going through). My parents pointed out a lot of issues/drawbacks/concerns about the program as it was described. I think their concerns were valid, although I can also see that I would have to enter into this kind of a program with a certain amount of "good faith." In the end, I decided not to respond to be considered a candidate. I received plenty of advice from my consult, and according to Jack, these kinds of changes take time to show a difference. Becoming a patient would make it all a lot more streamlined, (getting the right labs, getting the right scripts, getting the right interpretation) that's for sure.
>
>
>
> I can't remember - how long have you had Hashi's? Do you have positive antibody tests? Jack made a comment once that he thinks I have delayed onset type 1 diabetes. Well, he may be right, but so far no labs back the claim (and I've been tested). I'm starting to realize I'm on an unsustainable path (optimal or not). My marriage is feeling the heat, my friends think I'm crazy, I'm losing out on enjoyable time with my kids. I've becomed consumed by my (lacking, non-optimal) health. I spent so much time with low FT3, ketogenic, and low energy, eating my seafood and waiting for things to turn around. Then comes the iodine blog, explaining that when carbs are low, you have to compensate with iodine! Ack! Meanwhile, an apple or 2 a day would have made me a much better mother/wife (although not optimal!). I am not good at balance...

Still not sure I have it and thus the further testing. Jack just told me about a month ago in my last consult. My antibodies were never out of range but I now realize that doesn't have to be so. I guess part of my problem is I am still in the phases of anger, etc. from the diagnosis since I "thought" I was lucky and had escaped it. I couldn't keep up with it either and decided to add a little squash, sweet potatoes and carrots to my diet. Don't eat too much but a little to add variety and not feel so desparate about what I cannot eat. Probably not optimal either but something had to give.

Sounds like we are on the same path. I know it is hard on my DH cuz we just can't go eat anywhere now unless I say screw it and take the hit. He sort of gets it but doesn't really realize it is slowly killing me when I cheat. As much as I tell him it doesn't seem to matter although he says it does. When you ask him what he wants to eat and he says Pizza Villa which is "our" most favorite pizza, I get that he doesn't understand. Pizza has everything I am not supposed to eat and he should know by now that it is off my list and should never say to go there. They have nothing I can eat. Burgers are cooked on the same grill as buns are toasted etc. Surely a no go. I told DH that he has to be there for my next consult. I am going to ask Jack to tell me what he told me in the last one so DH might get it. He has sort of insinuated that I am making this up and saying it myself. Surely not the case.

After 1.5 years of trying it on my own and not really getting much further, Jack was my ticket. Yes, I would have to follow all the rules and I was prepared to do so.

I wouldn't say you aren't good at balance. Look what you all have to balance. Gosh, my kids are grown and I couldn't imagine doing what you are doing with little kids. Course when mine were little, I had no energy then either and life wasn't much fun. Matter-of-fact, the last time I had energy was about middle school. All pretty much down hill after that. On the bright side, I now wake up with a little energy and that has never happened for as long as I can remember. I guess that is progress even if it took me a year to get there. What do you think you are going to do in regard to "unsustainable path?" Do you want to share how it is affecting your marriage? Not necessary, up to you if you want to vent.

Maybe we can help each other. I need support too. My sister who was always my favorite has become my not so favorite which is sad. We had a huge blowout in the waiting room. So not appropriate. I am so sick of her not getting this and thinking I am just a bxxxx!!! She should know me by now and realize I have huge health problems and none of this is by choice except the fact that I have eaten SAD most of my life but she has too and continues to along with smoking. She doesn't have these problems although she is starting to bloat and a few other things but her mood never tanked. I realize I have to discuss this with her in more detail but I am not ready yet. I am still to0 upset with her for this. I have never been so upset that my heart raced like it did and I was really scared when it happened. It all started as they were talking about my other brother and being crabby. I said it wasn't his fault and they needed to understand that fact and that got me rolling about how she feels about me and it totally escalated. We cannot help it when our moods tank. I don't care what anybody else says either. I have lived with this for years and I feel compassion for those now that are crabby and try to enlighten those that are complaining. Pretty sure they have issues and they don't choose to be that way.

I guess I need to up my fish intake to more than once a day and see where things go. I sure hope that by adding a little squash that I am not setting myself further behind. I know I need to start CT'ing again but I have been miserably cold for the last 2 months and just can't get there yet.

Type 1 DM would be very scary to have and would surely make life more difficult. Know that I am thinking about you.

Don't know what is up with the long posts today. Sorry, must be all this thinking about my Dad. Guess he joked with nurses today and that is a great sign. He is quite the joker and makes everyone laugh. Great trait to have!

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-07T18:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56729>

> **bigknitwit;58019 wrote:** I was on "the list" too, to become a patient. When the email about the details came out, I thought long and hard about it. I sent the message to my parents to ask their opinion (they know what I've been going through). My parents pointed out a lot of issues/drawbacks/concerns about the program as it was described. I think their concerns were valid, although I can also see that I would have to enter into this kind of a program with a certain amount of "good faith." In the end, I decided not to respond to be considered a candidate. I received plenty of advice from my consult, and according to Jack, these kinds of changes take time to show a difference. Becoming a patient would make it all a lot more streamlined, (getting the right labs, getting the right scripts, getting the right interpretation) that's for sure.

Same thing........... family advised me not to b/c of lacking defined info. I was ready to go yet after the email I wasn't sure that my problems would be much closer to fixed after the investment. So I never applied. I would have taken the risk had it been cheaper.

Vicki, it wasn't first come first serve. It was Jack's pick amongst those who did send in their info. I also believe that life gives you what you need in general. Maybe look for why life gave you this..... I think we all need to learn how to create optimal for ourselves and optimal will not look the same for everyone. Instead, I think optimal is a very unique thing..... I love reading and learning on JK's blog. And some things.... I rather leave up to nature. I need to be in unity with myself and what I do. The more I learn to listen to my intuition with the added information the closer I get to my personal optimal.

You got to live in the Now. You might never see tomorrow. My decisions need to be balanced with that fact. Making myself miserable today so I can live more happy some day in the future is not making sense.

>
>
> Vicki, it wasn't first come first serve. It was Jack's pick amongst those who did send in their info. I also believe that life gives you what you need in general. Maybe look for why life gave you this..... I think we all need to learn how to create optimal for ourselves and optimal will not look the same for everyone. Instead, I think optimal is a very unique thing..... I love reading and learning on JK's blog. And some things.... I rather leave up to nature. I need to be in unity with myself and what I do. The more I learn to listen to my intuition with the added information the closer I get to my personal optimal.
>
>
>
> You got to live in the Now. You might never see tomorrow. My decisions need to be balanced with that fact. Making myself miserable today so I can live more happy some day in the future is not making sense.

Thanks Soul, I'm sure you are right but I would swear Misty said it was first come first serve in the webinar. I understand what you are saying about living in the now but I think we all deal with things differently and have to come to terms in our own time. Some things take more time for some than others and I am working on not worrying about it but I must just not be quite there yet. I have to internalize everything, that is the way I am. Always thinking, never stops and sometimes I can't stand it. Sometimes, I don't want to think, plan, organize etc. but my brain doesn't always cooperate and be at peace and just rest.

I also wonder if I missed an e-mail cuz I don't remember anything about sending any info other than wanting to be considered. But, I don't remember a lot so....

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-07T18:55:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56732>

> I need him to be my doc and help me get there so I can retain. What he is not thinking about it is those of us who are broken in our brains and will not get better until some fixing gets done by him. We desperately need his help.

You are giving a heck of a lot of power to one person here. Claim that power for yourself and you will be successful. Dr K can be an assistant on your way to your personal goals...... but he can't do it for you and he can't tell you what those goals are either. I'd advise you to get out of the helpless spot, and find a place where you can use the information he gives you for your own unique journey to health. Use him and the information he provides as roadsigns when you need it. But don't give up yourself while doing so.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-07T20:00:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56755>

> **vkiernan;58026 wrote:**
>
>
>
> What do you think you are going to do in regard to "unsustainable path?" Do you want to share how it is affecting your marriage? Not necessary, up to you if you want to vent.
>
>
>
> Type 1 DM would be very scary to have and would surely make life more difficult. Know that I am thinking about you.

I don't seem to be one of these people like Kiwilauren and Soul who can keep things running smooth and calm, meanwhile know my body is a wreck and needs further research and tuning! I admire them! I have a problem, and I plunge in wanting to \*fix it,\* and there's no stopping me... I take the rest of the household with me to a certain extent (tried to get the lights out here after dark. That didn't go well, and I took a little heat when I posted about it). The cereal is gone from the pantry, the kids all eat fish for breakfast every morning, and love 100% chocolate in their lunches, which are pretty much grain free. I'm really happy about all that, because it happened transparently, and those are real changes. It is not perfect here, though. And I think I have to embrace it for the time being, and keep looking for easy additions, rather than difficult subtractions (like dairy, lighting).

Dh doesn't want to hear what Jack has to say. I should say that, because he loves innovative thinkers, even when he dislikes the person. He doesn't really know much of Jack's arguments, except what he hears coming from me. It's also different for him though, because he is focussed on his career the way I am focussed on health. Me talking about more changes and concerns really drives him crazy. He says it's important to keep some overal perspective in mind, when looking at lab markers. That's a doctor's job - to balance the labs with the symptoms of the individual. He is not interested in altering his lifestyle. I know it's because his issues are not bad enough (yet). Quite frankly, I think this approach has merit. I'm running around saying the sky is falling! Quick! Duck! Cover! Eat more oysters! Turn off the lights! And he's the devil's advocate, poking holes and casting doubt, eating "wheat" pizza (although not at home. Our house is gluten free because it has to be). And to make matters worse, a good portion of my issues this past summer were self-induced. I was trying to optimize my thyroid hormone profile, and while the labs kept looking better, I kept feeling worse. Clearly I couldn't tolerate the doses I needed for "optimal." Fine. But my family suffered in the mean time. Now I am taking synthetic T4, and synthetic T3 (needs to go up!), and while I am not fixed, I am functional again (gaining weight though, 12 pounds eating meats, seafood and veggies no less - ugh!!!). It's become clear to me that my weight setpoint has clear correlations to my FT3 levels. So maybe, in the mean time, I need to put away the size 6s and 8s that used to be effortless to maintain (and yes, I have a sister too. She eats SAD, and still looks fabulous, regaining her pre-pregnancy size 4 -6 figure, etc. Just like me until baby #4), and embrace the 10s and 12s... My kids won't remember my clothes sizes, nor do they care. Neither does dh.

I will also say that my Hashi's issues really only took a turn for the worse 2 years ago. Before that, there were definitely symptoms, that in hindsight were related to loss of hormone production/control, but I did manage to have 4 babies and literally not sleep through the night for 10 years, all the while on synthroid. My grandfather had a thyroidectomy and lived until 92 without issue (although I think undiagnosed celiac that probably started a couple years before his death was what did him in).

I think I need to find a way to keep "working on" my health, but put it on the back burner, so that I can focus onto something new. Maybe I will even need to go out shopping for size 14 clothes... And I don't think I have type 1 DM. The intermittent numbness in the fingers I'm pretty confident was due to the cycloset I tried taking, probably because it was overdriving something when my thyroid hormones were all well too low to handle it.

I also took a 2 month CT break. I did a bath yesterday though. Now I'm posting novels lol!!

### diane — 2012-10-07T20:37:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56768>

> **vkiernan;58032 wrote:** I have to internalize everything, that is the way I am. Always thinking, never stops and sometimes I can't stand it. Sometimes, I don't want to think, plan, organize etc. but my brain doesn't always cooperate and be at peace and just rest.
>
>
>
> I also wonder if I missed an e-mail cuz I don't remember anything about sending any info other than wanting to be considered. But, I don't remember a lot so....

I tend to do the same thing - and wonder if that's not a large part of the problem. Whether we can do all the right things, but when the mind isn't there, the issues can't heal. I keep thinking of Jack's comment "change your brain to change your DNA" - and I think that's what I'm having the hardest part figuring out how to do. But I think it's a big key to change....

When I was home a couple of weeks ago, I was getting stressed from my mom. For the first time, I actually felt the pain in my stomach, and realize that my leaky gut probably comes in large part from how I deal with things. Over the past 2 years, I worked at changing much of what I consider health, and now am starting to get help in changing how I deal with things as well. So I wonder if no matter what we do from the outside (eating, observing light cycles), our thinking can be counter productive and not move us forward.

I also think that we have become such a quick fix society that we get so impatient when we don't see progress quickly. I see this in so many things - and not everything changes overnight. I've heard from so many people that change can take up to 2 years with the issues that I have - but I really haven't accepted that yet either. I know you've been at it a long time, and it can sure get frustrating.

I wonder what Jack went through as he worked through his changes. I imagine there was lots of trial and error, frustration, and joy when he started to get on the right track. Would love to hear about that some day.

I didn't get an email about the trial - would have loved to have seen it! Although I'm in Canada, so probably wouldn't have been able to participate anyway.

I believe what Soul says - everything happens for a reason. Sometimes it would just be easier if someone would spell things out and point us in the right direction - but there is so much more power in figuring it out for ourselves. I really don't have a head for science (but do for so many other things lol), but am so proud that there's things I'm starting to understand. I suspect this is what Jack is trying to teach us...

Hang in there, and enjoy the journey. A journey forward no matter how fast or slow is so much more rewarding than the alternative.

And yes, everything happens for a reason. I sometimes forget that! I, too, would love to hear about his trial and errors! Would be a great read.

On the bright side, I can still consult with him and he can keep pointing the flashlight for me and he has already pretty much pointed the way. I do know most of what is wrong. Can't be too many more surprises!
>

Got that right. I am worrying about my eating habits and not so much anymore those of my DH. He is a big boy and can take care of himself. Really trying to take everyone's advice to heart and learn from it.

**bigknitwit;58057 wrote:** I don't seem to be one of these people like Kiwilauren and Soul who can keep things running smooth and calm, meanwhile know my body is a wreck and needs further research and tuning! I admire them! I have a problem, and I plunge in wanting to \*fix it,\* and there's no stopping me... And I think I have to embrace it for the time being, and keep looking for easy additions, rather than difficult subtractions (like dairy, lighting).

Dh doesn't want to hear what Jack has to say. I should say that, because he loves innovative thinkers, even when he dislikes the person. Me talking about more changes and concerns really drives him crazy. He says it's important to keep some overal perspective in mind, when looking at lab markers. That's a doctor's job - to balance the labs with the symptoms of the individual. He is not interested in altering his lifestyle. I know it's because his issues are not bad enough (yet). Quite frankly, I think this approach has merit. I'm running around saying the sky is falling! Quick! Duck! Cover! Eat more oysters! Turn off the lights! And he's the devil's advocate, poking holes and casting doubt, eating "wheat" pizza (although not at home. Our house is gluten free because it has to be). And to make matters worse, a good portion of my issues this past summer were self-induced. I was trying to optimize my thyroid hormone profile, and while the labs kept looking better, I kept feeling worse. Clearly I couldn't tolerate the doses I needed for "optimal." Fine. But my family suffered in the mean time. It's become clear to me that my weight setpoint has clear correlations to my FT3 levels. So maybe, in the mean time, I need to put away the size 6s and 8s that used to be effortless to maintain (and yes, I have a sister too. She eats SAD, and still looks fabulous, regaining her pre-pregnancy size 4 -6 figure, etc. Just like me until baby #4), and embrace the 10s and 12s... My kids won't remember my clothes sizes, nor do they care. Neither does dh.

I will also say that my Hashi's issues really only took a turn for the worse 2 years ago. Before that, there were definitely symptoms, that in hindsight were related to loss of hormone production/control, but I did manage to have 4 babies and literally not sleep through the night for 10 years, all the while on synthroid. My grandfather had a thyroidectomy and lived until 92 without issue (although I think undiagnosed celiac that probably started a couple years before his death was what did him in).

I think I need to find a way to keep "working on" my health, but put it on the back burner, so that I can focus onto something new. Maybe I will even need to go out shopping for size 14 clothes... And I don't think I have type 1 DM. The intermittent numbness in the fingers I'm pretty confident was due to the cycloset I tried taking, probably because it was overdriving something when my thyroid hormones were all well too low to handle it.

\>I also took a 2 month CT break. I did a bath yesterday though. Now I'm posting novels lol!!

Seems like you are an all or nothing kinda gal. You take it and run with it and that is a good quality. But, I think you also started this way later which means you got soooo much at once and have been trying to implement it all at once. No wonder he thinks you are crazy (kidding)! Us older ones got bits and pieces and therefore where able to slowly introduce things to the other half. My DH tells me he doesn't think I'm crazy but I still wonder. I think if they had health issues and were worried and have followed the path that we are that they would think different. Necessity always comes out of need. They don't need anything but pizza!LOL. Sounds like your DH is a lot like mine in his thinking and he owns his own business so his focus is business not health too; and he tries to balance things out with me with labs and what is real and what is maybe questionable. Isn't that the point of the other half, to try to balance us out. Perhaps if we didn't have them to keep us in line a little, we might think we are going to die tomorrow and who knows what we might do. Even Jack's wife isn't like him or at least the way I read some of his posts.

I think you nailed it when you said you need to worry about easy additions instead of difficult subtractions. Everyone will be happier and there will be a lot more wins for all. Congrats on the progress you have made so far with your family. Not an easy task and you should feel proud. None of us are perfect so if you are doing all that you can, why stress that you aren't doing enough or have a handle on things. To me, you are rockin' it. Just keep forging ahead and before you know it, you will have success and your kids will be a lot healthier for it as well as yourself.

Woohoo on CT'ing. You are sooo right, your kiddos will care less about your clothing size; what is important is the time you spend with them. They will remember what mommy did with them. I certainly remember my Dad taking me ice skating and how good those times were.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-08T00:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56815>

> **bigknitwit;58057 wrote:** I don't seem to be one of these people like Kiwilauren and Soul who can keep things running smooth and calm, meanwhile know my body is a wreck and needs further research and tuning! I admire them! I have a problem, and I plunge in wanting to \*fix it,\* and there's no stopping me... I take the rest of the household with me to a certain extent (tried to get the lights out here after dark. That didn't go well, and I took a little heat when I posted about it). The cereal is gone from the pantry, the kids all eat fish for breakfast every morning, and love 100% chocolate in their lunches, which are pretty much grain free. I'm really happy about all that, because it happened transparently, and those are real changes. It is not perfect here, though. And I think I have to embrace it for the time being, and keep looking for easy additions, rather than difficult subtractions (like dairy, lighting).

I just want to apologise if any of my posts gave the impression that any of this is (or should be) super easy and straightforward. Or if I've made anyone feel less-than for the way things are going for them. Things often do NOT run smoothly at my house. However, I will admit that this was also the case well before I discovered Dr Kruse!  My motto these days is to do what I can to improve my health AND to remember that my health includes my relationships, how much I laugh, how much I can enjoy each and every day, etc. My perspective is a bit different, maybe because I've had cancer. I'm not sure. The only thing that helps me keep things as smooth as they can be is to make sure my decisions come from a place of belief, not fear. When I can do that, I can feel good regardless of how smoothly or roughly things go that day. My wish for everyone (including myself) is not that they become some 'perfect' CT/epi-paleo/circadian being, but that they embrace the principles of optimal health while appreciating every day they have. We're all on different paths, but we're all in this together.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-08T00:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56817>

Lauren, no need to apologize to anyone. It was well said I'm sure I am speaking for all, we wish you the very best. You have had to fight a very difficult battle and I believe you are on your way to becoming optimal. You have your brain in the right place. We can all take lessons from you! We gotta have some of each of us out here to put this all together as we all bring something different to the table.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-08T20:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56916>

I'm afraid to write this cuz it always changes the minute I post it but I have slept fantastic the last 3 nights. DH has even gotten out of bed and not woke me. I also felt pretty good yesterday and today. I'll take it for sure! Falling asleep on the couch by 9:15, going to bed around 10 and then not waking until 5:45 or so. That is a good night's sleep for me!

Got my thyroid ultrasound results back and I do have a nodule but it is less than 1 cm so they don't worry about it for now. Will retest in 6 months and see where things are at. I think I really need to get my iodine from some sea veggies and see if that helps. Now, which sea veggies and how to prepare them. I haven't a clue.

Had a little sweet potato and don't know what is up but I really don't like them very much anymore. I used to love them sooo much. What changed and why? Maybe it is for the better, less starchy carbs.

Even though my t3 hasn't been raised I am doing ok and have a little energy. Got enough done at home yesterday to keep things flowing and 1 auditor couldn't make it in today so life is good.

### LinD — 2012-10-08T20:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56925>

Same here! I don't recall the size of my nodule, but they said they will look again a year later.... Maybe it is smaller or this office less cautious.

I did purchase some roasted nori and ate it with fish this morning. Not sure what/how others are using it. The 'roasted' is probably not as optimal as raw.... I don't know. We'll learn together?

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-08T21:51:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-56936>

I bought some seaweed, but then I started getting nervous about it, because it was harvested in Japan. I'm going to look elsewhere to see if I can source it away from a nuclear fall-out area. Meanwhile, I am taking the ever unpopular iodoral...
