### vkiernan — 2012-10-11T04:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-57510>

Finally got the ok from my doc today on T3 and titrating up slowly. She read what I gave her for info and is on board. She said I have to take my temp and pulse twice a day. I am all for that. Should be doing it anyway just to see where things are at. She has never done this before but is open to it and even asked me if it is compounded or Cytomel. I choose compounded so the ins. company couldn't force a generic on me. This will hopefully be a fairly short-lived experiment anyway. Hoping to clear the t3 and switch back to Armour. Who knows where it will go though.

Work was very mind draining today and yesterday. Really exhausts me. I'm ready for bed now and just might go there very soon. House is cold. No heat on yet and it was 50 outside today. I keep telling myself it is good for me and to just buck up and be cold. Pretty hard when one is already cold in the first place.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-11T20:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-57633>

A few days ago I posted how great my sleep was and that I probably shouldn't be bringing it up; well I was right and it has been crap ever since. I seem to be quite restless and really dreaming. Not staying awake but just not rock hard like I had been. I woke up today not feeling any happiness. Didn't like the feeling at all and wonder where it comes from if nothing is changing or at least that I know of.

Work has been stressful and maybe that is part of it. Dealing with auditors yet today and tomorrow and then I should be able to breathe for a few days at least before we start a major project here. Actually, 2 majors projects might be starting. Sure hoping the t3 does its thing cuz I am gonna need a lot of energy to get thru these. Very excited about them though.

Not much new otherwise. My pop is still doing very well. Visited him the other night and he had a great appetite. Yeah Dad!

### vkiernan — 2012-10-15T15:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58288>

Got my reg. t3 yesterday to start titrating. Thank you Doc for letting me try this. They gave me a lot so I am free to do this. Got to buy that thermometer to take my temp and my pulse as she requested. I "will" follow the rules. Don't want to blow a good thing.

I spent the day with my granddaughters yesterday. It was good day but still exhausting. They didn't fight too much and I was very happy about that.

I am still feeling a little happy and like I want to do something. So not like me. Have added a little coffee back in and mood is still holding. Don't want to push it though. Need to buy more green tea and keep drinking that. Not sure what is doing what, but I had removed eggs for a couple of weeks and then removed coffee and it was within 3 days that things started looking up. Thinking I might have an egg problem and it took a bit for things to subside inside. Will know pretty soon when I get my Enterolab result back. I WANT to know what is all wrong.

I am curious about flour and if one is gluten intolerant, what is the impact? If you are around someone who is baking, are you potentially ingesting it due to it flying around in the air? I think the answer is yes. Never really gave this much thought but now I think I have too. If I have the gene for celiac, my daughter has celiac, what does this mean for my granddaughters? Especially the one with epilepsy? Would removing all grains from her diet help her potentially? She does complain of stomach aches. I think we need to have her tested.

Sleep is still not as good as it was. I am waking up and going right back to sleep but I don't like waking up. Not sure if my body goes right back to where it was or if I stop all good things from happening. I would like to ask Jack that question.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-15T20:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58374>

> **vkiernan;59800 wrote:**
>
> I am curious about flour and if one is gluten intolerant, what is the impact? If you are around someone who is baking, are you potentially ingesting it due to it flying around in the air? I think the answer is yes. Never really gave this much thought but now I think I have too. If I have the gene for celiac, my daughter has celiac, what does this mean for my granddaughters? Especially the one with epilepsy? Would removing all grains from her diet help her potentially? She does complain of stomach aches. I think we need to have her tested.

A friend of mine has celiac. She was at her sister's house in the basement while the sis and neice were upstairs baking. My friend got sick just from being in the house when they were using flour upstairs. So I'd say if you are very sensitive, you need to avoid being around someone baking. Given the genetic history in your family, I would think getting your granddaughter tested makes sense. And even without testing, avoiding all gluten (including from shampoo & other products) is necessary.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-16T21:00:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58575>

Things are still floating and I can't figure out why. I am taking Jarrow ox bile and it is strong stuff. Taking 1 with BAB and now 1 with supper and it is not working. Def. making things flow better but no sinky. What is up? If I take more, it will just be the big D.

I also am wondering if the progesterone is making me gain weight. Can't be pushing my E as high as it was when I wasn't gaining. Matter-of-fact, I actually hadn't lost or gained in several years. This is sort of ticking me off! I am NOT going to buy new pants. Will have to some how keep getting in the cold tub. My poor cold body says Noooooo, don't do that to me but I don't know what else to do. If, the p is making me have better moods then I need to keep it going despite the weight, but this sucks.

So let's see, swollen eyes, weight gain, a little gum bleed (never have this either). Hmmm, what else is going on???? Cold, not a whole lot of energy. Mostly all energy is in the am and then by evening I am tanked. All sort of points to thyroid.

I could try eating a very small supper of protein/fat only and see if that does anything. But, that would mean calories in/calories out matter.

ok, back to work now.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-17T02:26:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58626>

> **vkiernan;60105 wrote:** So let's see, swollen eyes, weight gain, a little gum bleed (never have this either). Hmmm, what else is going on????

Interesting. These are all my usual symptoms when I'd eat something that I shouldn't. Last March when I was figuring out the food issues, I remember eating chili (low-carb) for dinner one night, and eyes being so swollen the next morning that they felt very uncomfortable - looked like I'd been crying all night long. And also the weight gain (water), and my teeth would often bug me a bit - they'd get more sensitive for a few days. My left wrist is also a good barometer for me for inflammation. It hurts when I've eaten something I shouldn't. When I remove the culprit food, it takes a couple of days for the reaction to drop off, and about 5 days for the weight to drop away again. Just something to think about with regards to your foods (although I think you're pretty limited and AI too, aren't you?). I know the squashes give me a light reaction, I think sweet potatoes do too  Also, consider the ingredients in your T3 since it's also new.

### Hope — 2012-10-17T02:59:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58632>

There you have it, Adrenal fatigue in the making. The above are the "highlights" that I believe caused a big part of my health issues along with my really crappy diet. Who knew sugar and high 06's could kill you or at least make you very sick. I just thought I might get fat. Even though I had been low carb for years (ever since following Atkins), I believe the damage was already done. I also believe I have had estrogen dominance most of my life as well due to really bad TOM's forever. It is too bad I didn't take that nutritionist more serious and have this really looked into. I might have been better years ago. Funny how Dr. K came into the picture when he did.

This is amazing..you have been through a lot! And how great to see your improvement..I see myself where you were in the beginning of your journey. It's so encouraging to read your progress....thanks for sharing all this. Love the pic! How I miss the Pacific!

And, would you mind sharing your cocoa/coconut bark recipe? Never heard of it. Sounds yummy.....

Will keep reading...

### vkiernan — 2012-10-17T03:30:08+0300

I have lived a rough life but certainly not as bad as some. It wasn't all bad; I do have 3 beautiful kids and 2 granddaughters who are the love of my life.

Others out here posted recipes and I think everyone tweaks it a bit. I do it two different ways but chocolate is pretty much out for now. I melt coconut manna, coconut oil and sometimes a little ghee along with 99% dark chocolate and a little stevia. I was adding brazil nuts to get my selenim but they are out for now too. But, I really love this stuff w/out the chocolate and nuts and I put a little english toffee flavored stevia in it. It is truly dessert. I always ate it after lunch but had to stop that too cuz that is when I eat my seafood and coconut oil isn't allowed with seafood. Anyway, I use a mix of about 50/50 oil to manna and guess at the rest. You gotta play with it and see what you like. You can also add coconut flakes if you like. Your mind is the limit.

### Hope — 2012-10-18T16:32:29+0300

You are amazing! You will get through this, and find understanding about the test, and you will find and eat all the foods you need to get well.

I wish I could give you a big hug right now, so I am hugging you virtually.

Also, I'm sorry about your hubby. Mine doesn't get any of my stuff either, really though at times he tries....I think maybe it is because when you have not been ill, or suffered physically, or paid enough attention to how food actually affects you (most people don't even though they DO have issues, and I would bet he does, even not as bad as yours - unless he's optimized himself with Dr K's program or his intuition!), you just don't get it. I can't tell you how many people - including family and in-laws - don't get it...about anything, or poo-poo when I say I won't eat any gluten, period etc. OF course it's harder with hubby.....I still have to struggle with mine to keep off the lights (especially the hallway by our bedroom - I have replaced as many bulbs as I can but the ceiling is super high). Anyway.

I am so glad you vented here. We are here to support you.....and we get it.

How is your hubby's health? Is he energetic and able to think straight most of the time because he's exuding energy? You said he smokes....is he stimulating himself to make it through the day/night? Perhaps his brain is broken too....from modern life.

### Hope — 2012-10-18T16:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58916>

As nuts and seeds are a very healthful source of vegetarian protein and heart-protective oils and minerals, rather than avoiding all nuts and seeds, you can render nuts and seeds less antigenic, more digestible, and more easily tolerated by choosing the few that you seem to best tolerate overall, soaking a one-day supply in a glass jar filled with clean water for 4-8 hours (or for ease, overnight), and pouring off the water and rinsing before eating. The resultant soaked nuts or seeds can be eaten as is (alone or with fresh or dried fruit), blended into nut butters (by adding some water), or added to Ã¬smoothies.Ã®

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-18T21:01:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-58992>

Well, they say knowledge is power... Now you need to figure out how to use this new knowledge that you have. I can only imagine this is not the result you were hoping for, but keep in mind that you are the same you. Nothing has changed about you, you have only learned more about your body. Dr K. says people with AI should not eat specific foods, regardless of the test's results - fine. I am curious about the prognosis for the others - like beef and tuna and pork (have trouble with pork, a little bit of trouble with chicken, and possibly beef - especially when any of these meats are in their ground form). Is tuna representative of most seafood protein? So then it comes down to, how does one eat who is reactive to most all foods, including seafood? I have a friend who fears this is where her highly overtly allergic son is. She says the tests would not be useful, because they would show him reacting to _everything._ In your case, the reactions are obviously at a lower level (it's not like you're breaking out in hives all the time or need to be on perpetual anti-histamines, or anything). So, do we still move toward better health by continuing to eat some of these reactive foods (I'm implicating that you may be reacting to more seafood choices than just the tuna), and that better health will still come?

i am also curious, which markers did you have previously that led Dr. K to believe you had Hashi's?

### Hope — 2012-10-19T02:48:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59089>

If you have any kind of leaky gut, you would be more reactive to foods. That is what I have had in the past, and probably do now, LOL. I remember reading in a book by Dr Christiane Northrup that beef is/can be inflammatory in her view...because of Arachidonic Acid and what it does with prostaglandin production and other stuff. But we need it....it's one of those things I guess. I wonder what beef they used to test....grass fed, pasture-raised or terrorized, chemical laden stuff.

Here is a rather lengthy article from someone I know from Weston A Price. It's very long and you can ignore the beans and grains stuff...

[http://westonaprice.org/food-features/living-with-phytic-acid?qh=YTo3OntpOjA7czo3OiJzb2FraW5nIjtpOjE7czo0OiJzb2FrIjtpOjI7czo2OiJzb2FrZWQiO2k6MztzOjU6InNvYWtzIjtpOjQ7czo0OiJudXRzIjtpOjU7czozOiJudXQiO2k6NjtzOjEyOiJzb2FraW5nIG51dHMiO30%3D](http://http://westonaprice.org/food-features/living-with-phytic-acid?qh=YTo3OntpOjA7czo3OiJzb2FraW5nIjtpOjE7czo0OiJzb2FrIjtpOjI7czo2OiJzb2FrZWQiO2k6MztzOjU6InNvYWtzIjtpOjQ7czo0OiJudXRzIjtpOjU7czozOiJudXQiO2k6NjtzOjEyOiJzb2FraW5nIG51dHMiO30%3D)

I would love to hear what u learn in the consults...

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T16:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59628>

Hmmm, what is all of this mean? Ct'd last Sunday, got a rash on Monday and still have it. Had achey joints Friday, Saturday and yesterday ended up with awful headache, really bad achey joints and a fever. I never get sick but am sick now and staying home from work. A little better today but wiped out and I don't want to push it.

Could 1 ct session really bring that on or does the rash have something to do with it? Although, our house and work has been cold for several weeks so does any of that add a little ct to things?

I decided, thanks to Patty, that I am going to get the book on the other t3 dosing protocol and see if I can help my adrenals with it too. At this point, what do I have to lose - nothing!

I am finally realizing that a lot of my problem probably stems from inflammation. Don't know why I couldn't realize this before. The more I CT, the more I will reduce it and things will start to right themselves. This should have been so obvious that everything stems from inflammation. I think my insides are a mess.

I also realized that I have to find a good doc for my bios like Jack has said. Without righting the hormone status, how can anything else work properly. I know my current doc isn't on the same page as me. Although she is willing for some things, other things she is adament about. One example is that progesterone doesn't push estrogen. I believe I am living proof. Why else would I gain 5 lbs. in a month? I really need to ask Jack this question and get his thoughts on it. A few of us out here have this problem too so I know it isn't just me.

The other thing I have realized is that stress might just be worse than drinking or smoking. I say this because I never really drank much and never smoked but I am more broken than a lot of people who do these things regularly. I think being happy plays a much bigger role than I ever thought. Even my eating hasn't been that bad over the course of the last 15 years compared to some. If it were, I'm sure I would have gained a ton a weight, but instead held my weight pretty steady. My mom and my dad were both pretty heavy. The one thing that I haven't been is happy. Pretty crappy first marriage so for me it makes a lot of sense. I even used to exercise and that didn't change it for me either. Got to be the stress factor. I realize the light factor plays a role here too but we always kept the lights down due to a miser of a husband. He was always turning the lights off. What I want to know is, is why I am so bad since I was at most 20 lbs over after my 3rd baby? This isn't really that much. Have I had hashi's ever since? Is it possible to have it for almost 25 years and really not know it?

I'm reading the book "Recovering with T3" right now - almost done it. I think I'm also going to give it a try, even with the minimal quantity of T3 I have (I'll be asking for more next week). I will probably only start with 5mg at 5am. The difficult part for me will be getting back to sleep. I am hoping to simply "train" myself to do this...

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-22T20:14:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59676>

I completley hear you on the stress/happiness angle. How do we fix this? How do we move towards a place of happiness/acceptance with our lives? ### Inger — 2012-10-22T21:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59690>

I see you both on the stress issue.

Me too destroyed myself with stress. For many, many years. That is the thing why I am so broken too. I am not allowing it anymore. My health is so much worth.

What I am doing is priorizing myself more, to have just more time, work less. Less consumption, less money. I have given up a lot. But guess what... I am happy now. I live so much more in the moment, I enjoy every day so much. I have given up what family and friends think about me too. That can be a huge stressor and was for me. Paradoxal is that I have very good connections now, better than before.

I am giving me at least one year to just heal. No other plans. I will plan when I am healthy. I need zero stress now. Working a little, and then just have it dark and cool and boring. With some MHS throwed in and other crazy healthy foods.. It all feels so very healing, I have started to crave it. I need it after all these damaging years. I need it so much. It all makes me feel more and more alive, lighter and lighter.

So to me the answer is

Super healthy foods, light management and CT (I do quite gentle CT)

Reducing work and having more time

"Reducing life" in general, giving up.. enjoying the presense, sunrise and sunset, silence...

### vkiernan — 2012-10-22T21:56:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59693>

BKW,

For myself, I have to do some of the things that Inger does and that is slow down and enjoy life. I am remarried and happy. My health is why my mood has tanked and made me a not-so-happy person. I never used to be like this. I must have been on a complete high when I met my dh cuz I felt very loved and happy and haven't actually ever felt like that with my ex. I know I had a problem looking back but I had been this way for so long that it didn't occur to me until things settled down in the relationship with my new dh that my health took a turn for the worse and my mood went totally in the toilet. I am starting to feel happier and that change happened when I dropped eggs, coffee and started progesterone cream. Not sure which made the change but I sure liked feeling that way. Yes, I still work 2 jobs and I know I shouldn't but the 2nd job is only 1 or 2 short nights a week and a Saturday here or there. One of these days I will quit one of them. In the mean time, my dh never pushes me to clean house or anything. I do what I can and leave the rest. Keeping a very tidy house is just not worth the stress to me.

For yourself, since I don't really know your whole story, you can work on fixing yourself slowly and surely. Once you get there, the answer might be right in front of you! You can also talk to someone about things. Just make sure they are neutral and can help you see things as they really are and not a one-sided deal like a man-hater might be. But always make sure your intuition is also telling you or you will make some unwanted decisions that might bite you in xxx later. I have realized that my mood tanking makes me think or say a lot of things I do NOT mean.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-23T20:58:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-59884>

Yes, and yes lol!! Eating this way is somewhat stressful for me (I am still thinking about the foods I am missing, rather than the good foods I can eat). And, I think my attitude (or personality) creates a lot of unnecesary stress for me. Lately I have felt like I am chasing a "cure," or resolution, for my current health issues, and those of my children. In trying so hard to find and implement that "cure," I think I have created a certain amount of stress for myself that wasn't necessary, and possibly set me back further than all my forward moves (with diet, and cold, and supplements, etc). It seems, in thinking about it, that cold baths and seafood, and living in harmony with my environment should really be viewed as tools to help my brain heal, but that I need to actively practice what I am trying to learn and achieve (inner calm, feeling well, enjoying life, happiness, etc), no matter how damaged I currently am! Does this make any sense at all?

> **vkiernan;61263 wrote:** I just wrote something but I think I misinterpreted you. Are you saying that by having to eat this way you are stressed or your life in general? Not really sure what you are getting at.

> **bigknitwit;61459 wrote:** Yes, and yes lol!! Eating this way is somewhat stressful for me (I am still thinking about the foods I am missing, rather than the good foods I can eat). Yes it all make complete sense. I am the same except my kiddos are grown. I am still trying to help them be healthy though. You never quit being a MOM! I think you and I have the same situation though were we are doing this all by ourselves and frankly it is tough. I, too, have lived this way for so long and only want to be fixed today not tomorrow. But, it ain't gonna happen on our terms; and until we come to terms and just start living our life for today and not tomorrow it will be a very long process.

I think I am still shocked at how the body reacts to everything in life and how "I" ended up this way! How can I not be able to eat all those things without harming myself further is a crazy thought. I would have never guessed that could happen. I knew some about grains and the harm they do and a little about lactose intolerance but not about how your body will fight itself and actually do damage. Heck, you eat too much sugar and carbs and you get fat, not auto-immune diseases. I wonder all the time now how many people are slowly killing themselves and they haven't a clue! Once again, thank God for Jack!

You keep enjoying those few mornings a week that you have to yourself. You do deserve it and are worth every second you take. What do you have to lose if you a little counseling too. I believe all you are trying to do is find peace within yourself. And if someone can help you sort it out, then go for it. Besides, if Momma is happy and healthy, everyone else will be happy and healthy!!!

If you come up with any really good AI recipes, please let me know. I have trouble since I'm not supposed to use crushed red pepper - my favorite spice. Ummm um! I frankly don't know how to cook anymore.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-25T20:41:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-60179>

Finally got thru to the new doc. and I will see her next Wed. as they had a cancellation this am actually. I am pretty excited. Still gonna keep the old though too for now.

On a positive note, something is changing for the better. Today is tom and no crankiness. I had 1 semi-bad day last week but I also got sick last week and that was it. Got to be the prog. and maybe cutting out the eggs and coffee. Not sure but going to stay the course. This is the easiest one in so long I can't even remember. I actually feel good and happy. Maybe the t3 is doing something. Been taking the first dose in the middle of the night this whole week and upped it this am to 12.5. I am still dosing 6.25 mcg every 4 hours thruout the day and the sr 40 mcg before I leave for work. Will stay there for a bit. To have basically no pms is a miracle in itself.

I am thinking about going to a Halloween party and my work x-mas party. Haven't wanted to do these things in a long while. What is up with life and wanting to be a part of it???? I truly hope this attitude stays around for long while. It is a whole lot more enjoyable!

As for the t3 and waking up to take it, yes, I am tired and don't like it but doing ok for sure. I am desperate to feel good again. I will do almost anything at this point. I am sure I will adjust once I figure out the time I need to take it. Somehow I am pretty good at programming my wake up times. I can go on vaca and know what time to wake up. Pretty strange.

I was also able to stay up until 10pm. I did have to work until 8 though. Probably the lights but could be the extra t3 too. Usually when I put my glasses on at night, I am OUT if I even think about putting my head down even a little. I tried 2 times the other night to listen to Underground wellness on Devil in the Milk and fell asleep twice instantly. Finally woke at 10 and decided I had better crawl in bed.

Devil in the Milk is an excellent read. Not what I expected but he lays the facts out pretty clearly. Looking for A2 milk in this great dairy state and can't find any YET! I would love for my dh to drink that instead of his reg. milk which I know is A1.

My Recovering with t3 book is on its way. Looking forward to reading it. Read enough on STTM to get me started. My thermometer is complete crap. Couldn't find the old fashioned kind. Got to do more searching for a good one.

I am still warm in the am. Always am. If I am going to ct, this is the time that works best for me. It is in the afternoon and evening that I get cold. Hoping this changes soon. 1 night a few nights ago I was warm and still don't know why. Strange feeling to be sitting on the couch and not have frozen hands and toes and be all bundled up. Ok, enough babbling for one day.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-27T17:09:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-60441>

Getting stoked! Was doing more reading on the new doc and she seems like a gold mine. She is an MD with func. med background along with anti-aging. Everything I need. Her std. thyroid panel is Ft3, Ft4, Rt3, tsh and the hashi's tests. She even deals with the labs that have done my gi effects test so she should know how to interpret them. Why did I listen to DH and stay in network and waste more of my precious time?? I was going to see her a while back. Oh well, on my way so won't dwell any longer. I do have to get stronger with him and my boss who wastes my time too. I have to get better about not letting them run me over. They also do neurotransmitter testing along with a host of other things. I finally feel like I might be getting somewhere. Her fees are not outrageous either.

Did my CT yesterday and practically shivered the whole 30 min. Been cold again even though I have upped my t3. Not sure what is going on.

Got a headache today. Hormones, stayed in bed too late??? Who knows. Hope it goes soon though. Got lots of rock work outside to do. DH and I are building rock pillars in our front. It is going to be beautiful! It is cold today, only a high of 45 but the sun is out and shining brightly so that will be nice to get some sunshine in. Will probably need a nap this afternoon but that's good too.

Had yesterday afternoon off, have to burn vacation time. I will be taking every Friday off now until New Years. That makes me very happy but somehow I have to keep up at work and that is a little stressful. Year end always throws me behind and I have to play catch up. One should be able to take vaca and not worry about their work. Doesn't happen for me though. No one else to do it. Ok, enough whining!

Had a little heart palps going on yesterday. Not bad, just a little. Will keep the course. I have not been very hungry lately and seem to get full on just a little. Nice change. I wasn't even hungry at noon yesterday which is not normal. No cravings either. Gotta roll with it and enjoy it. CT used to make me really hungry. Hope that doesn't start again. Had nauseated stomach yesterday. Thinking it was from CT'ing. That is ok though; I know it is a "good" thing. I suppose I had better get rolling outside if we are going to make progress today.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-28T15:08:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-60539>

Still getting the little bit of heart palps. Wondering which way to go. Thinking I might need to back off the early morning dose and see how things feel. I have been really tired the last couple of days. I'm still not sure this t3 thing is for me but I have to press on especially for the adrenal results. I am no longer waking up feeling good like I was. I feel all drugged out and pretty worthless. No energy and sort of light headed. Got dizzy yesterday morning and haven't felt that in a while. Perhaps something else is going on. Sort of feel hyper inside but have no energy. Weird!

I read of everyone's success. Why can't I find success? My gosh, I have certainly given up about everything I can. I "think" I am doing everything I need to except some major CT'ing which I am working back into. Yes, the glasses are on at night and I get my morning sun in the am. What am I not doing or what is still wrong that has not been uncovered??? I should be feeling like a million bucks by now but I am not. I don't get it. I'm hoping my new doc will sort some of this out for me. I have read a little about LDN lately and am wondering if I might see results with it. Almost seems to good to be true!!! If it could reduce some of my inflammation, then I might be on the right path. Got to install the skype recorder and have that consult with Jack to get some of my questions answered.

Be careful, read the labels, think.

I am looking at tin with oysters.

Oysters are good, actually the best, DHA content and all else, DHA-->Omega3 (the best kind)

but

those oysters are in cottonseed oil.

Cottonseed oil is a bad news in this context, loads of omega6

drain completely the oil before eating oysters.

From this pov, only water and olive oil are edible. All else should be drained off.

.....

We probably should slow down on calling all omega3 as good fatty acids.

Have to be even more selective.

With current practice we are ending up with too much EPA.

.....

### vkiernan — 2012-10-30T13:32:27+0200

Really love waking up to the sun. I love seeing the sunrise and I look for the sunset too since Jack has made me aware of the few that we actually get. I try to point them out to my dh too.

This is a very busy week. Got per. end at my day job and month end at my night job. Will have to stay focused today and not let my boss interrupt me all the time. Gosh that is soooo annoying. No more than get into something and he is over asking silly questions or talking about silly stuff that really doesn't matter at the time. Started drinking coffee again (don't know why) and I think I need to ditch it and go back to tea.

Ahhhh, on a very important note, my granddaughter has had no seizures and will start backing off her meds asap. I am sooo excited for my little babe. Sure hope she is outgrowing it and it is not all the med keeping them at bay.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-01T13:47:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-61252>

Saw my "new" doc yesterday. Very nice to have someone who can read and understand my lab results. She is doing a lot of things for me right now only after 1 visit. 1. Treating me for parasites, 2. Testing me for heavy metals, 3. Doing a 4x salivary test along with a neurotransmitter test, 4. Retesting the gut test after I do the parasite thing, 5. Helping me figure out what I can eat and where I might find these foods, 6. Helping me with my pg/e2 ratio and will work with me on my thyroid if I choose. I will be transistioning all over to here in the near future I'm pretty sure.

Thanks Jack for leading me down this path!!!! This isn't what I had hoped for my future to have to take out so many foods but at least I hopefully won't have to suffer like my Mom did for years. Had I not found you, I would be so bad by now that they probably would have me on anti-depressents, etc. by now. But, because I found you, I won't have to take a bunch of bad meds to make me feel good. These are the things I need to keep in the forefront of my mind. The positives of what the right foods will bring me.

Sleep has been real crap lately but I am feeling ok today. Took my 6.25 mcg T3 at 3:30 and I think that is working out. Maybe this weekend after the stress of this week is over I will start sleeping better. Last night was a late night for me. DH decided I should upgrade my vehicle and put a deposit on a vehicle 2 hours away. So we left work and zoomed right down there. Unfortunately, they could not agree on a net price with a trade-in. My current vehicle has 25,000 less miles and gets better mileage but does not have some of these really great bells and whistles and is a year older. I love my truck so I really have to think long and hard before doing this.

I have to note that my appetite has really gone down. This has been over the last 2 weeks I believe. I will have to read as I think I noted it earlier. Don't know what is changing but I think it is good. I ate lunch at 2 yesterday and wasn't even hungry for dinner. Went to bed at 10:30 ish and did not have hunger pains. This would really help me if I only had to worry about 2 meals a day instead of 3. It is tough enough figuring food out right now.

Wanted to note that I don't know what changed between yesterday and today but I feel like I did a 180. This past week has not been good. ### vkiernan — 2012-11-07T14:54:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-62504>

What to do, what to do??? I am exhausted by 7 pm and just want to go to bed. I know though, if I do, that I will wake very, very early and lie there til it's time to get up. So, I end up falling asleep on the couch and go thru a wake up, sleep, wake up, sleep and it is very irritating. I believe this is making me more exhausted. I am also trying to wake up to take my t3, then wake up again 1 hr before rising to take my parasite cleanse as this needs to be on an empty stomach. I feel like just going to bed early and then getting up early. My first wake up is around 2:30 cuz it was 3:30 before the time change. This is, unless dh wakes me up to put his arm under me which absolutely drives me crazy cuz I am soooo fricken tired and hate to be woken for that. Not only that, it gives me a neckache. Maybe I should ask my new doc if I can double my dose of prometrium to see if it helps.

My mood is still holding fairly well. I am very pleased about that. I really like not being a cranky, irritated bxxxx! Life is a whole lot more fun for sure.

I need a break from work and all it's hassles. I need some down time to get back on track and caught up. My boss needs to stop the new projects for just a bit so I can catch my breath. I also need my people to be there and do there jobs so I don't have to. Summer thru x-mas sucks for me. No time to slow down.

Reread and TOOK notes on Jack's latest blog about adrenal fatigue. OMG, it is crazy how much one can get out of it if you take notes and not just read. It is packed full of info and each sentence has to be dissected and absorbed before moving to next one. I definitely have a PVN problem which is nice to know that it is not my adrenals. I have to come up with a plan to incorporate the things Jack talks about for healing it. Maybe I move lunch to later in the day and that be my last meal. Then I can go to sleep earlier and still have the right amt. of hours before going to sleep and this will not allow a lot of evening light if I am in bed sleeping. Eat a LOT of seafood for lunch so I get my 1 meal in at least. This would certainly help with figuring what to eat since a lot of normal protein is off the list. My doc told me a place to go shopping for the foods that I can try eating. I will go this Friday on my day off. I have quit eating the meats that are on the list. It's hell to have nothing to eat. I am going to drink a lot more coconut milk for the fat too. I noticed yesterday the my BAB did not have much fat and I was hungry early. Actually, I was hungry after my lunch too.

Oops, time is flying. Got a dentist appt. this am and need to get going.

I saw your hs-crp was very good. Congrats on that; low inflammation is a very good thing. Mine is not so good but working on it. NT is neurotransmitters.

The poor kiddos, they both had bad stomach flu. Her little sister went back to school today.

I hope you have a good night's sleep too. My sleep was good for awhile and now it is downhill. Just don't understand quite why it changes.

I am going to get ostrich, bison, lamb, maybe goat yogurt and goat cheese just for fun, will look for duck or quail eggs. Not sure about the egg thing though or where to find them. My chiro suggested the quail eggs. Said his brother's family eats them cuz they all have food issues and they seem to do fine with them. I really need to get some variety into my diet or I think I will crack soon. Not only that, I've learned if I don't mix it up, it will only get worse in regard to antibodies against them. I did eat shrimp for lunch the last 2 days and I put cajun spice on them and they were good. Next time a squirt of lime will be even better; I have to eat more though since they have no fat and I get hungry sooner. Although tonight I did very well with the hunger issue. My chiro told me tonight that the local grocery store in his town carries the ostrich and bison and a few others. He says the ostrich is really good. I am actually kind of excited to try this stuff. I am also going to look for pheasant as it is supposed to be very good - and especially good as soup. If you have any ideas, I am open to them in regard to meat/seafood. I would love to try ceviche. That sounds like something I would really love.

I have this seafood cookbook that I need to bring up from the basement and go thru. It should give me a lot of great ideas and make all of this seafood eating a lot more pleasurable.

My son is going to get me a deer and when I take it in to have it processed, I will talk with them about my food issues and see what we can come up with for a breakfast sausage. I would really like to try duck fat. I wonder if I found some if they would use that in the sausage? I have to think outside the box so that is what I am trying to do. I can't come up with another fat that might work since beef and pork are out.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-10T14:30:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-63344>

Went to 1 store yesterday to buy my "new" food that I can eat. Well, $170 later and only 2 meat sources was a total swallow, choke, wth????? Went home and was frustrated beyond belief. Even more so after trying a smidgen of organic raw cheddar goat cheese when I got home and not being able to swallow the stuff very well. Hope the dh likes it. Wonder, does one adapt to those sorts of tastes over time if you keep trying them? Or, is it ridiculous to eat the stuff in the first place and just go on without it?

Have to report - TMI alert -the gut microbe stuff must be doing something because I am not taking enzymes and hardly any mag citrate and the outcomes are sinking and looking way more normal this whole past week. I might be on the path. That gut microbe stuff about makes me gag trying to swallow but if it works then it is all worth it.

I also have to report that I am lasting longer until the next meal time and not going crazy if I don't/can't eat right away. This is even after eating a fish/seafood meal that usually makes me hungry. This has been going on for a couple of weeks and excites me as it tells me something is better inside.

Slept much better the last 2 nights. Yesterday I completely felt like crap but today, so far, am feeling quite a bit better and I might even have some energy.

Kiddo gave me some venison and I got that wrapped up and froze. Kinda strange to get large chunks of meat, that I haven't a clue what they are, and try to figure out what to do with them. I sliced them in not quite 1 inch slabs and figure I can grill them or throw them in the crock pot. I did know what the tenderloin was though and am pretty excited over that. We love venison tenderloin wrapped in bacon and grilled. That is one tasty meal.

Still don't think the t3 thing is working for me yet. Not sure that I am doing it correctly though either. Have to spend more time with the book to get it figured out. Not giving up.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-11T00:57:37+0200

### vkiernan — 2012-11-11T16:32:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-63603>

Started rereading the Recovering with T3 book. Lots of good info. The 2 main points that I get are 1.) If you take t3 4 hrs before wakeup, you make more cortisol than if you took it 2 hrs before wakeup. The more time you have t3 in the range when you make cortisol to wake up, the more cortisol you make. Problem is, if you can't tolerate that much cortisol than you need to have less time between taking it and wake up until you can tolerate that much. 2.) You might need to take the biggest dose for your early am to "jump start" your cells with t3. A small dose might "not" give you enough right out of the gate and then subsequent doses might be able to be smaller. If you start small and it does nothing, then up the dose and hold for a few days and continue the process until you feel something. Also, start later rather than earlier to see what you can tolerate. This really is an individualized thing. Got to play around with it and see what works best for you. I upped this am to 18.75 mcg from 12.5 mcg the whole last week. I also take it around 3:30 to 4 am and I get up around 6. This is 2 to 2.5 hrs. before waking. Will stay there for a few days and see how I fare.

Slept pretty good again last night. Woke up a few times but went right back to sleep. Can't complain too much. Feel good again today except have a small sore throat. This one is real and not thyroid related. Actually, now that I think of it, my throat has been pretty good lately overall. Maybe the t3 is doing something that I was not aware of.

Did my CT yesterday and didn't shiver until it was time to get out 30 min. later. That is really good for me considering I normally shiver for the first 5 min. or so and then after 20-25 min in. It always energizes me so I plan my house work around it. Guess it's time to go CT and get on with my day and get something done while I'm "feeling" it.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-12T14:36:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-63798>

Slept fairly well again. Whew! I really needed some catch up. Mood is still holding and for those of you who never have mood issues, I cannot tell you how great this is. My life sucked before sooo bad. Always cranky, couldn't even stand myself. My poor dh. Don't know how he survived living with me. I am wondering if eggs and/or dairy were my biggest offenders; well, along with grains that is; but, I haven't eaten a lot of grains in years though unlike the eggs and cheese that were part of my daily staples.

Snowmobile season is fast approaching and I am thinking I should start doing some exercise now that I am starting to feel better. Got to have some muscle to really enjoy the ride. Starting small of course. I could either do it in the early am or come home for lunch 2days a week which would be more optimal. I could do it right after work and that might even help me stay awake until 9 if I'm lucky and I might even sleep better.

Dh and I went grocery shopping again last night as he needed some things and the store had a huge shrimp section and only 1 tiny little place with wild caught. Why are they so hard to find? I was very happy to have found those though. We also bought some scallops. I am pretty set for a bit food wise. Cool thing is, seafood cooks very fast even from a frozen state. Makes dinner time easy when I am too tired to really think about cooking much. I also bought limes for making ceviche. This will be interesting as I have never had it. Loved herring as a kid so I wonder if the fish texture is similar?

Don't know why but the oral prometrium seems to be doing a much better job with me than the prog. cream. I am taking a very small dose unlike the cream. Sleeping fairly well and no breast tenderness so far at all. We will see what next week brings. Of course I am getting my iodine now but it hasn't been that long to stock up on it. My overall bloat/gain has gone down a bit too. Still 6 to 7 lbs over my ideal weight but my good mood is worth it if this is how it is going to be. I do believe though that if I continue my CT'ing, I will lose the weight. I did it again yesterday and didn't shiver until out and could still put my makeup on (I am taking a risk though as mascara and shaking don't go well together. Course I only stayed in for 25 cuz I am trying to see how I fare w/out shivering in the tub so much. If it helps me get in it more then that is a win. I did notice yesterday that I gasped with the cold. I have never done that and the temp has been upper 40's and low 50's. Better than shivering though.

Ok, got to get ready for work and make it a good day.

### Lyndra — 2012-11-12T19:11:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-63835>

It's great to hear your mood has been good. Life is much more pleasant when you're not cranky all the time ;-). Nice going with the CT, too. Sounds like you are tolerating that quite well.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-12T19:21:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-63840>

Bad mood? Honey I was the undisputed QUEEN of bitchiness. If DH hadn't been working for the railroad and gone most of the time I'm sure we'd have been divorced ages ago.

I slid slowly into that hell because of about 25 years worth of insomnia. It was insidious in that I didn't realize what the source of the problem was. I just kept losing bits of sleep every night and over time it amounted to a HUGE deficit.

Adding OTC progesterone cream helped but only so far. Going on the Wiley Protocol BHRT in July of last year gave me a huge boost out that hole. Adding the leptin Rx and making myself EAT helped more. But the what really sealed the deal for me was adding the blu blockers at sundown. It took time but now (about 99% of the time) my sleep is just incredible. Out like a light and sleep like the dead until it gets light out. Now that winter is closing in I keep one window uncovered for just that purpose. Nowadays when it DOES get light (about 7or so) I grab my sleep mask and sleep until 9 or so. It. Is. Wonderful.

It has taken a long time. And sometimes I regress. An hour or two of missed sleep and I am the Queen of Crab again. But these days I know what the problem is and am able to rein it in a bit. Those moments are coming further and further apart.

You'll get there.

I don't know what all you're dealing with so I'm not going to even try and tell you what might help but I WILL say that from my perspective fixing your sleep is paramount. But it takes time. Keep at it.

Hi V,

What a challenge... Are you starting to see some payoffs while doing it? I may try to get this test run for myself in the new year. I think I can get out-of-network coverage for it if I can show no in-network lab with a similar test (there isn't). What is this gut microbe stuff which you mentioned? It appears, at least to me, that some things are starting to fall into place for you. You've got T3 to work with, you've got answers about your reactive foods, your appetite is settling, your mood is stabilizing in a more positive light, and you are sleeping a little better! I hope as you continue these things continue to improve for you!

I hope as you continue these things continue to improve for you!

Hi BKW. You recapped things very nicely and what a challenge it is. I am finding answers - may be not what I am looking for but an answer none-the-less. As for my diet, I have eliminated everything except pork and beef. I will continue with beef (I tested the lowest w/beef and it is grass fed) but after this am's BAB, the pork is now gone. I will try to not eat the beef everyday though. Since removing eggs and dairy my mood has been a lot more stable. Really got to wonder if there is a connection or if it is something else.

Did have a blip last night with dinner out. Asked the server if I could swap out the noodles with brocolli due to having a gluten problem. He said for an upcharge you can so I said sure. First he brings my salad with croutons, then I started eating and got thinking that my sauce should have been runnier cuz it was supposed to be butter, wine and garlic. This was with shrimps, scallops and mussels. So my dh asks the server to check it out and of course it had gluten. But, they were so apologetic and kind. Even gave me a gluten free menu and a list of what items has what allergens in it like dairy etc. Our server was new so I can't really blame him. He sure got a lesson though and so did I. Hopefully, they were easy on him and trained him properly now that they know he wasn't or at least they said that. I will certainly be more informed the next time.

I hope you can get the test. Just be prepared for the worst. I cannot express enough that I would never have dreamed my results would end up the way they did. I keep waiting for this bad dream to be over with. I am trying to be ok with it. Last week was tough until I went shopping and found some things. Sure wish I could source some A2 cows locally. Goat is, well, kinda goaty. Leaves a baaaad aftertaste so that isn't setting too well with me. It's not like I am trying to eat a lot of this stuff, but looking for options to mix it up.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-13T20:22:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64080>

Got up and ate my BAB then got my squishy buns in the tub. Didn't gasp today but got the shivers around 20 min and stayed about 25 or so. That's all I could do. I did notice my legs got warm really quick today. Normally, I would run the cold to clear out any warm in the pipes and then I get in and fill it up. Croak method. Now, I am starting to fill it up and then get in. I do go in up to my shoulders so all of me except the top of my shoulders are in but I pour the cold over them and down my throat for my thyroid. If I am going to do this, I am going all in. Now let's see if I can continue and really see some changes. 3 of the last 4 days. Yeehah!

I also got a little smarter and put my makeup on before my ct. No more mascara messes. I am peeing a lot today. Sometimes that happens. Also, I still feel good. Happy! Got pretty good sleep again last night too.

Oh, btw, I got my first taste of Obamacare. I work for a wonderful company that provides us with excellent benefits and thanks to Obama, my company will now be penalized for being a great employer and will not be allowed to provide us with what they have been. We also employee about 400 people. How sad is that. Obama has made it clear that no one can have better benefits than anyone else. Gee, they call that what???? Sorry if you are reading this and am an Obama supporter, I am not. When you take away from the top, you always take away from the bottom too in many disguised ways. I don't know why he doesn't get that part.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-14T14:34:08+0200

Wed. Nov. 14th

I did sleep like a rock from 9 to 4 but I am kinda tired today; but, only cuz I fall asleep on the couch R E A L L Y early and then wake up at 4. Got to stop this. I want to exercise in the early evening but I come home pretty exhausted and don't feel like doing a damn thing ever! I know if I would just start, I would feel better. We will see what tonight brings. I am home alone again so no one else to worry about but me. Not a bad situation which I have learned to enjoy!

I am going to CT again this am. That will be 4/5 days. Keepin' it simple. The more I stress over it, the less I will do it. If 20-25 is all I can do, then so be it.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-14T19:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64297>

> **RiverNeighbor;66251 wrote:** [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10383602](http://http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10383602)
>
> Milk cross reactivity........
> Thanks for the thoughts. I really have a hard time understanding what they are saying. Are they saying that the reaction was happening from other animal's milk too?
>
>
>
> RESULTS: IgEs from children allergic to cow's milk are capable of recognizing most part of milk proteins from mammals bred in European countries (ewe, goat, buffalo), while no serum used in this study contains IgEs reacting with camel's milk proteins. Camel's milk was also not recognized from circulating IgEs from a child specifically allergic to ewe's milk. Specific antibovine monoclonal antibodies cross-reacted with proteins from other mammalian species, apart from those of camel.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-14T20:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64306>

Exactly, their results showed that children in European countries who were allergic to cow's milk also reacted to the milk proteins from other european mammalian milk (ewe, goat, buffalo).

### vkiernan — 2012-11-15T01:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64361>

> **RiverNeighbor;66300 wrote:** Exactly, their results showed that children in European countries who were allergic to cow's milk also reacted to the milk proteins from other european mammalian milk (ewe, goat, buffalo).

Thanks RN. Wonder though since I looked at IgA's how that compares to IgE's? I know they are different but now exactly how. Are we talking A1 or A2 milk? European would be more likely A2 wouldn't it? So not sure if we are comparing Apples to apples or oranges. I really need more info. Will have to do some digging.

### RiverNeighbor — 2012-11-15T06:01:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64417>

V, I'm pretty sure that I've also read that there is strong correlation between IGA,IGE, and IGG ( responses in children who have milk sensitivity/allergy - meaning if they have a response in one category they are very likely to have the other immune responses.

The other thing that popped into my thoughts tonight are all the other proteins that make up milk.... there's at least casein, casamorphin, and butyrophilin. A quick search revealed that they are all of concern for someone with AI. I think the casein and casomorphin differ between cow,goat,sheep, and A1 vs A2 but the butyrophilin is either the same or so similar in the milks that it doesn't matter.

So I'm thinking the bottom line is that A2 is a better choice for someone who doesn't have a gluten sensitivity or AI, but if you have AI already.... stay away from milk. At that point A1 vs A2 isn't the issue, the issue is reactivity to any/all of the different proteins in milk - any kind of milk.

Okay, If I'm totally going overboard in your journal feel free to kick me out

I "always" welcome other's opinions. Sometimes it opens my eyes to things I cannot see or didn't understand. You are always welcome to come here and HELP me cuz that is how I view it. I am very appreciative for you or anyone taking your time to respond to my issues.

What you said about the "other" proteins makes sense. I never thought about it that way. I will discuss this with my doc and see if there is more testing that I can do. I don't want to go back to where I was just to be eating a little goat dairy which I don't even like very much. Trying to find variety.

When my enterolab came back with no pork, chicken, tuna (salmon too cuz the cross reactivity to tuna) and even beef is on the "no" list, I about lost it. What the hell can I eat? And, more importantly, since my diet is so limited and I still have any leaky gut going on, then the new foods will leak thru and pretty soon I won't be able to eat those things either. I am trying to figure out how I can eat a lot of fish and seafood and not get the same issue. I already can't do tuna and salmon. What makes cod or shrimp or anything else sea worthy ok??? I am hoping I am healing my leaky gut as I have done a lot and continue to work on it but how do I know? I drown my BAB in turmeric and black pepper every morning. I drink kombucha and eat homemade sauerkraut every day. I did a LOT of L-glutamine. Essentially, I have been working in it since March 2012.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-15T14:31:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64460>

Thurs. Nov. 15th

Forced myself to watch a movie (with my glasses on) to stay awake until nine. It worked. I went to bed shortly after nine and woke at 3 to take my t3 and then went right back to sleep until 5:15 ish. Got up at 5:45 and makin' it a day. I call that a pretty good night's sleep and will take as many of those as I can get. I did take 2 prometrium (100 mg) before bed instead of the 1. Perhaps CT'ing is helping. I did it yesterday and will do it this morning. That's 5/6 days. On a roll and gonna keep it going. Making sure I take silymarin and DIM before too. Got to get the big guns out for this journey.

Had my first gf bison burger today. Not gamey at all. Not my gf burger of course, but certainly eatable. Which the stuff wasn't $12 a lb. though. This is going to get expensive at this rate.

Have tomorrow off and the boss is taking all of us to a play (Scrooge) at the Fireside (known for plays) today. People come all over to see them by bus. They serve up a great buffet and then you watch the play. Really wonderful actors and actresses. This will be a very nice relaxing day and a great way to kick off the holiday season. I am pretty excited for the holidays this year. Got to be that I am starting to get a little better. I am going to 3 x-mas parties in the first week of Dec. too. Haven't wanted to do this in a long, long time. I know he menu for 2 of them and I am ok as beef is served. Not completely optimal for me but at the bottom of the list for reactivity. The 3rd I will probably eat beforehand and not worry about it.

Ok, gotta roll in get in that cold tub and get to work.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-11-15T15:43:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64469>

Hi V! You seem to be doing pretty good, keep it up!

I didn't do any testing about food, but I know I don't feel that good when I have beef (regular one, that is, because here it's really difficult to find gf stuff). I sometimes have game meet or horse meet. The later is really cheap (cheaper than regular beef for certain cuts), but you have to find a good place to get it from. Horses are not raised for meet, and aren't usually given any antibiotics. It's really good too. I was having that every morning, but now it's only once in a while or if I'm not home (much easier than briging my shrimps).

Now I stick to shripms in the morning, and I add scallops every other day. I buy both from Costco. They are farmed, but that's ok, and that fits my budget. I also eat a lot of salmon, it really is a bummer that you can't have any. But you still can have trout, cod, sole, sardines, haddock, lobster, crab, snails, squid (those are real cheap too!), oysters, mussels. I prefer to stick with fish and seafood rather than paying the big price for gf meet. You just need to find a good place where you have variety, and you buy what's in special. And you are very lucky in the US because you can order online. It has always have amazed me: food by mail  But you probably can find good deal. Don't see it as a problem. Yes, you are limited, but you'll feel better at the end.

As for knowing is you gut is healed, I think you have to look at your stool (Bristol stool chart) and you should be on level 4. I'm not sure about that though....

Have a nice day!

> **vkiernan;66036 wrote:** Got up and ate my BAB then got my squishy buns in the tub. Didn't gasp today but got the shivers around 20 min and stayed about 25 or so. That's all I could do. I did notice my legs got warm really quick today. Normally, I would run the cold to clear out any warm in the pipes and then I get in and fill it up. Croak method. Now, I am starting to fill it up and then get in. I do go in up to my shoulders so all of me except the top of my shoulders are in but I pour the cold over them and down my throat for my thyroid. If I am going to do this, I am going all in. Now let's see if I can continue and really see some changes. 3 of the last 4 days. Yeehah!
>
>
>
> I also got a little smarter and put my makeup on before my ct. No more mascara messes. I am peeing a lot today. Sometimes that happens. Also, I still feel good. Happy! Got pretty good sleep again last night too.
>
>
>
> Oh, btw, I got my first taste of Obamacare. I work for a wonderful company that provides us with excellent benefits and thanks to Obama, my company will now be penalized for being a great employer and will not be allowed to provide us with what they have been. We also employee about 400 people. How sad is that. Obama has made it clear that no one can have better benefits than anyone else. Gee, they call that what???? Sorry if you are reading this and am an Obama supporter, I am not. When you take away from the top, you always take away from the bottom too in many disguised ways. I don't know why he doesn't get that part.

And I don't know why people continue to put this all on Obama. It is properly called the Affordable Health Care act. Which hasn't even gone into full effct. If greedy business owners are going to screw their employees you can't lay the blame on the President.

Now then, I come in here to read about health issues not politics. So I'm not saying any more.

Ummm, I'm in the same place as you with the Hashi's thing except Jack says I have it and the last 2 docs said I don't. Anyway, got to go with Jack and the worst scenario no matter what to do myself the best.

As for Cyrex, I am going to get further testing. Man, this has been an expensive out-of-pocket year. I have spent $1,800 minimum between docs and tests that my great insurance won't cover and I'm not done yet. I have 3 more tests sitting on my coffee table and the one I looked at last night is $258. I feel I have to do the Cyrex too. I will probably do it after Jan. 1 so I can include it in my flex ben. I have not come this far to not complete my mission. I could be eating the one thing that keeps me from optimal. I really don't want to do that at this point. I am wondering how much better I will really feel when I get all of this right.

Good for you for rotating your meats. Who would have thought that the very thing that was supposed to do right by you was actually causing the harm. Well, not due to itself of course but rather the leaky gut issue and what happens when food passes thru. I have said this many times before, but I still wonder where I would be at right now had I not found Jack and all his knowledge.

As for yeast, I would let Jack guide you. Even with Hashi's, he said to drink kombucha, kimchi, sauerkraut, etc. to help the gut. My gut is bad so it does make sense to treat with things that might cause other issues. Seems like what the value is vs. the negative. I'm sure that is why he always says to keep it in context and to only yours and not others.

Since my new doc put me on the gut microbe stuff, things are getting better in the gut dept.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-17T14:37:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-64920>

Ct'd again yesterday even though I said I was not going to do it. I did shake getting in and could only last about 20 mins. as I was COLD! What I am noticing though is my stomach is going down and almost appears as if I might have lost a pound, dare I say that of course as it always backfires. Had pretty good energy yesterday. Ct is always good for giving me that. I"m also curious, when I get in the tub my stomach is usually pretty full feeling but when I get out, it feels great. Why happens inside to make it feel like I didn't eat but I'm not hungry either. I think I have to ask Jack. He has probably already said, but maybe he can shine his flashlight to where.

Had some 85% chocolate yesterday and woke up with really achey hip joints. Haven't had those in a while and haven't really had chocolate either. Thinking there might be a connection. Not really sure what else it would be.

Slept pretty damn good last night all things considered. Of course, fell asleep on the couch by 8. Woke at 9 and had to make the bed first, urrrggh. Went to bed and only woke 2 times before rising at 5:30 from achey joints. Feel pretty good overall.

Still no breast tenderness. Really liking the prometrium vs. the prog. cream. No swelling of anything going on at least from an outward appearance. Can't say about the inside though....

One thing I have noticed this past week is my sniffer is sooo much more sensitive. I was putting my makeup on the other day and I smelled the brush hair and I have NEVER smelled the brush before (I have used these brushes for years). Ick, it did not have a good smell. Even with my dh and his smoking (yes, he is thinking about quitting - a start at least) I can smell it all the time now and it is gross.

Had our dept. outing to see Scrooge on Thursday and it was fantastic. Ebenezer couldn't have been more perfect. They totally accommodated "ALL' of my eating requirements too. I had fish. They gave me 2 cod fillets and 1 came home with me. Cool thing was, everyone was eating their chocolate cake and ice cream and I just sat there; but it wasn't like I was drooling or anything. I was really ok with not eating it. Sure, I knew it was delicious but it ended there. Seems like the better I get feeling the more I care about what goes in my body. After seeing all of the older people there, afternoon matinee, makes me want to do better all the more. Have of them could barely walk on their own. That is their reality but that is not going to be for me! Like Jack says, when you know better, you do better. I do know better and have no excuse now!

I took 4 t3 at 2:30 am and will hold there for a bit. Trying to find the sweet spot to where I really feel like I've taken something.

Sun is coming up and the sky is glowing red, grey, and purple. Really beautiful right now!!! WOW, getting more beautiful by the minute. It is glowing.

DH has big plans outside this weekend. We have to cut down a tree, split it and get it up to the house. We also have to do another layer on our rock pillars. He threw his back out 2 weekends ago so that put a damper on it. My wrist has been aching so I hope I can hold up today. Even been getting it into the cold water to try to help. Not sure what is going on.

I also have to make my sauerkraut and bottle my kombucha. Maybe I can sneak that in this am before he wants to get going.

Kinda cool that he owns a computer business (well some of the time). He had the guys set me up with a Lenovo Think Pad Twist. Brand new technology. I am actually getting something new before he does. It turns into a tablet or a laptop and is a lot lighter than my current dinasaur. Can run windows old time or by icon. We brought it home last night so I haven't had any time on it Yet... Later today for sure. Kinda funny that he was talking to a guy yesterday that he bought some company coats from and he wants to buy my laptop. So it is all working out.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-18T16:02:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65120>

Sunday, Nov. 18th

First things first, my sissies b-day, got to call her today. Did my CT yesterday and didn't shiver so much but only lasted 25 min. Ok though, some is better than none. Gave me good energy again. Took a nap yesterday as we were going to dinner with friends later and I knew I wouldn't be able to stay up otherwise. Man did I zonk! Ended up going to a new Mexican restaurant in town and I know the owner which is fun. I had this great shrimp dish with spinach, mushrooms, tomatoes and green onions. I also had a side of freshly made quac. It was soo good and pretty much all ok to eat. Yes, I did have a margarita and it was good too. Won't be having another one for a long time. I did really enjoy it! I now know a place locally where dh and I can go out to eat and I can still win. They have a 5 different shrimp items that fit the bill. I might even have them concoct that dish with a little spice next time.

I am feeling good today. A little tired is all. Gut is down and yes I do believe a lb. is gone. Got to be from CT'ing. Sure encourages me to do more. I have these great new magazines with all these dairy, grain free Thanksgiving recipes that has been really fun to read while CT'ing. I also put my x-mas music on which makes the time a lot more enjoyable. I am an x-mas music junkie! I love this time of year.

Love my new Lenovo Twist. It has a touch screen which I absolutely love. Saves on the wrist for sure.

Yeah, a really short work week this week and it is casual week all 3 days. Another beautiful day today. Sunny and about 53. I'll take it. Yesterday was very nice too.

### LinD — 2012-11-18T17:33:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65142>

Good morning, again. Sorry about the Badgers.... (college football). It was a fun football day while DH unpacked/arranged more in the new house. We are about 80% settled. Besides the sudden/unexpected TOM, I suspect a UTI. Ugh. I should have had dr test me when I was there on Thursday but wasn't sure, plus was distracted with all the rest (finding out about horned levels). Now she is out all week long.

Happy birthday to your sis. Your food choices sound harmless and quite yummy. I haven't had a Margarita since last year (before June), do 1.5 years. Margs were my favorite besides Chardonnay or Merlot or Cabs... However, I have coconut tequila shots when I'm in a mood. Lol. The sugar in the traditional Margarita is off my list.

I have a short week, too. Yay. Only work on Monday. Will drive 2.5/3 hours away for Thanksgiving day at DH's step-mom's house. DH father passed two August ago...

Good for you on the CT. I must start again. (surely showers with some cold, plus nighttime cold pack is not enough.)

> Anyway, have a great week. May I ask, what do you do?

I was just thinking about you and the iodine issue. So, you feel better with it? How long have you been taking it? I have been feeling better since I've been taking it. Maybe that is the catalyst that is making things finally work. My antibodies aren't very high either as far as I know(maybe that doesn't matter?). I will ask my doc to retest when I go the end of this month. I will have been on the iodine for a month or two by then. Jack likes iodine from real food and I believe he said he is good with it with people with Hashi's. Trying to work into that.

You "almost" bought a duck egg? Why didin't/couldn't you? I would think they taste just like a chicken egg. Too bad my neighbor doesn't sell them anymore. I believe I was told I could buy them at Whole Foods. It is a drive for me so I have to plan to go there. Maybe this Friday. I will certainly let you know what they are like; unless of course you tell me first. Nothing better than venison tenderloin and a couple of fried eggs for BAB!!! I do wonder though if they are really much different than a chicken egg in regards to their effect on my body.

Scallops are delish. They have a slightly sweet taste and I love them. Not seafoody at all. I bought some elk, bison/buffalo and ostrich patties today. Elk is thawing in the fridge. I also bought some ground lamb. No idea what I will do with it but am going to give it a shot. I also have scallops, shrimp, crab legs and cod. No complaints about nothing to eat from me for a while.

I hope you have a great week too!

### vkiernan — 2012-11-19T14:36:39+0200

Monday, Nov. 19th.

S/be pms week and Thanksgiving s/be D day. We will see what the week brings since mood is still holding and I'm only 3 days out. Last month was good too. This will tell me truly how things are. Got a little headache today. Going to CT and hope that takes it away. Woke up at 2:30 last night and took my t3 but then couldn't fall asleep for about an hour and a half. This is going to be a long day for me but at least the boss is gone so it will be relaxing. I can just do my own thing and not have him bugging me all day.

I did not CT yesterday but I'm ok with that. Gotta take a little break for toxins to clear anyway. Might be why I have the headache. Had just a little bit the last couple of days too.

Had my first elk burger today. Not bad at all but kinda tough. Maybe ground too large? Still very eatable. That is all I could do this am along with a sliver of sweet potato. Putting the rest away for lunch.

Ok, gotta go get COLD! Have a great day everyone!

### vkiernan — 2012-11-19T20:28:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65393>

> Jack Â· 66 weeks ago
>
> @Que....only 60% of serotonin is in the gut. And yes diet soda is huge blocker of serotonin because of maltodextrin in diet soda. It completes with tryptophan. Maltodextrin gets changed to glucose eventually so even though the govt says it has no calories.....you need to avoid it in my view. Just more processed crap to avoid. The vagus nerve is an amazing structure and will get it's own blog post soon. The vagus is parasympathetic and uses acetylcholine as it's transmitter but it controls and modulates the enteric neuronal system and all it's hormones.

Just read an old blog on NT and this was a little snippet worth remembering. I knew to never buy stevia with maltodextin but I never knew why it was bad and now I know and can pass this along to those who don't.
