### vkiernan — 2012-11-23T15:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65456>

Had a nice post and lost it Ithink. Might be here but I can't see it if it is. Oh well, let's try again. Thanksgiving Day just happened to be a great day for me. If I understand the dynamics correctly, my rt3 cleared and t3 finally got into my cells. I went very hyper, at least from my norm. I ate my BAB and felt very shaky. Then took my pulse and it was running around 120ish and 60ish is norma for mel. I got a LOT done though without getting wiped. Things finally settled around 12:30 pm. Unfortunately, I must have gotten a little flu bug last night as my stomach was cramping from around 4:30 am til around 10 this morning. Now I feel wiped out and the day is a bust. I really was hoping to join the other craaaazy people out there for some fun shopping. But instead just laying low.

I had 1 bad day of pms and no breast tenderness so a huge win for me. Things are really starting to shape up. I have even lost a lb. or something as my pants are starting to fit better and my belly is shrinking. Must be all that CT'ing I have been doing the last 2 weeks. Will keep that going. Maybe since t3 is getting into my cells I will be more successful with that too.. Can't tell you how happy I am about all of this. I do believe my ship is back on course and pulling ahead.. Even sugar is losing its hold on me. Never thought I would see the day. Food is becoming less important and more about filling the space and getting better.

I have been eating wild caught shrimp vs. farm raised and what a difference in taste. Farm raised is so wimpy tasting vs. the ocean flavor in wild caught. Do believe I will keep paying more for the wild caught. Had some mussels and shrimp the other night and it was good.

### vkiernan — 2012-11-25T18:53:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65595>

Don't know wth happened, but so much for 1 day of pms.Â  I was stricken after the fact instead of before.Â  DH has been pretty stressed, etc. and not really there for me the last couple of weeks and that has taken its toll too.Â  This has happened before with after-the-fact and maybe CT helped it along.Â  Headaches and flu-like symptoms.Â  Really bad headache today but it is finally gone.Â  I have also been napping a lot the last few days from being so tired.Â  Belly is still down so a positive.Â  This tells me to keep CT'ing.Â  MaybeÂ I'm detoxing...Â I have not felt well since my rt3 cleared.Â  Hoping tomorrow is a great day!

Â 

What is up with my fingernails?Â  They look like I have old growth and new growth.Â  A line, 1/3rd of the way up, across every finger.Â  Showed my DH and don't know what to make of it.Â  Could the t3 actually be doing something or the parasite cleanse?Â  Really strange looking but clearly noticeable and maybe a positive.Â 

Â 

Slept much better the last 2 nights.Â  Only woke 2x and fell right back asleep.Â  I am not peeing in the middle of the night anymore either.Â  I'm thinking the parasite cleanse had something to do with that. Â Glad that stopped again whatever the reason.Â  Wondering if the t3Â early am is helpingÂ the af situation.Â  I have been able to stay up later the last several days.Â  So not like me.Â  Now that IÂ am thinking of it, no afternoon slump today.Â  Â 

Â 

Finally going to get all my tests done this week.Â  Doing the saliva and NT tomorrow, the heavy metal on Friday or SundayÂ and probably the GI retest on Sunday to ship Monday.Â  Got to read the instructions once more for shipping to make sure I am good with the days.Â  Will be glad to get these done.Â Â If I get them done this week, I shouldÂ know more by Christmas!Â  The GI test will take about 3 weeks but the rest should take a week.

Â 

Def. like bison more than elk unless my first experience wasn't a good measure, it was tough and no butts about it.Â  Might have to find a bulk supplier like on Eatwild.com.Â  Will be thawing my duck this week for the weekend.Â  Also found out where to buy duck fat.Â  Pretty excited about that.Â  Now I need to get it ordered along with more Nutiva co.Â  Almost out.Â  Yikes!

Â 

Last night we had the gd's and we made bacon wrapped shrimp.Â  Well, mine weren't wrapped buy anyhow.Â  My daughter peeled, I deveined and the 6 yr. old wrapped all the shrimp with the bacon and did an awesome job of it.Â  She also LOVES them and we will be having them again when she is over.Â 

Â 

Found myself a lot hungrier today.Â  Speaking of which, it is striking again.Â  I just ate a few hours ago.Â  Might need a lot more fat.Â Â

Had my first ostrich this am.Â  Not much different than any other red meat.Â  Fried it up as a burger with garlic and onion.Â  Bison is still my fav. of elk, ostrich and bison, so far.Â  Need to explore them more.Â 

Can't get my temp up yet.Â  Not sure if I need to up the t3 or let the adrenal situation get better.Â  Guess it is back to the books to figure this out.Â

### endless — 2012-11-30T12:45:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-65970>

Can you use fish sauce? I make this raw beef regularly and really like it. I guess it would depend on if you are a fan of Thai flavors of course!

Thai Raw Beef

1/2 lb brisket or sirloin, partially thawed into 1/8 inch slices, julienned and diced.
2 Tbsp lemon juice
2 Tbsp fish sauce
1 jalapeno pepper, seeded and thinly sliced
2 green onions sliced thinly
1/3 cup chopped cilantro
1/3 cup chopped mint

Mix all together in a non-metal bowl and marinate, covered, for several hours.

I omit the jalapeno because I usually don't have any...

### vkiernan — 2012-12-03T14:36:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-66237>

Just checking in.Â  Been a few days.Â  Stayed out way too late Saturday night.Â  Really tired today so wondering if that is the result of Sat. night.Â  I am still not feeling anywhere near as good as I had been.Â  A little here and there but not every morning like I was.Â  I want that back.Â  I even gave up coffee so what gives????Â  I will note though that I have been able to stay up a bit longer in the evening.Â  Taking t3 really early must be doing something along with taking it late afternoon.Â  Now to figure what is best when.

Finally got all my tests sent off for heavy metals, ASI and GI restest.Â  Will be a long 3 weeks to wait for results and see the doc.Â 

I am also wondering my heart is doing this little fluttery thing a lot lately.Â  My pulse is still fine.Â  It seems it is fluttering like when I need more t3 but not sure yet.Â  Really have to pay attention to it.Â  I do not like this and I never had it before my rt3 cleared.Â  What is going on?Â  Can it do this if I run out of t3?Â  This means I need more than the 75 mcg I have been taking or to dose it more often with less.Â  It is doing it now at lunch at the last dose I took was 25 plus 40 sr at 8 am.Â  It did just occur to me that this changed when I cleared but also got a new t3 script.Â  Wondering if something is up with that.Â  Should get a new one in the mail today so I'll try that and see how I fare.

Hoping my oysters and mussels are still good.Â  The directions for my heavy metals test said not to eat seafood so I had to put it off til today.Â  So, that's what'sÂ on the menu.Â 

Made my first batch of kale chips and they are really good.Â  They didn't come out dry so I put them back in the oven (with it off) and actually forgot about them until about an hour or so later and they turned out great.Â  I will be making these again.Â  Might even sprinkle some garlic on them.Â  Something to add to my list.Â  Yeehah!

### freshveggies — 2012-12-16T13:01:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-67642>

When you get your 23 and me run the raw data through http://geneticgenie.org/Â  This will show you something like the Yasko report.Â  I haven't tried this site yet but will be doing it for my family when I get theirs back.Â  I have have run both the 23 and me and Yasko and they came out the same except Yasko test for a couple other snips.Â  Your methylation snips will tell you if you have any genetics issues.Â  With having high metals, you have a likely chance of not running at full capacity.Â  I guess 30% of the population are affected by this.

Looks like I ran the same test you did for metals. After 2 1/2 years of using DMSA (which is a sulfur doner and I need to stay away from them for now)Â  Just saying that by using DMSA it was all around making me worse by dumping more sulfur into my body ---I can't break it down do to the CBS defect.

getting back to the test---after 2 1/2 years of using DMSA here are some of my numbers---(I have the same reference range you do)

lead 20.4

Mercury 26.18

Platinum .221

You need to start by getting your gut in order then making sure you are filled up with your minerals.Â  You can add some basic methylation support in while doing this then work on the CBS defect if you have it, then start with the others.Â 

This gives a pretty good explanation of it.Â  http://www.heartfixer.com/AMRI-Nutrigenomics.htm

Â 

from Yasko---Methyl groups are the body's messengers and movers and shakers.Â  They join with other compounds to jump-start a reaction (such as turning a gene on or activating an enzyme).Â  When the methyl group is 'Lost" or removed, the reaction stops (or a gene is turned off or the enzyme is deactivated), OR when a methyl group is lost a gene is turned on (for example, a gene related to cancer) when it is not ideal to have it turned on.Â 

The byproducts of this pathway can lead to inflammation, a precursor to various conditions ranging from Autism to Alzheimer's to cardiovascular disease.Â 

If you go to Dr. Yasko site Holisticheal.com you can read her Autism pathway book as a download for free.Â  This book pertains to anyone with methylation defects and not just Autism.Â 

Â 

so if you don't methylate properly you can get a build of of metals and virals and much more.

Once you do the genetic genie, if you want, post your results and I will look at them.Â  If you have hetero or homozygous snips you will want to look into this.Â  You need someone to guide you to do this.Â  Dr. Tim Jackson does this as well as another person I know.Â  I will PM you with the name.Â

### Patty Cakes — 2012-12-17T14:07:53+0200

I did the metals test last year. I didn't have really any issues there.Â  Two values were very slightly elevated but not mercury or lead or any regular metal.Â  From what I understand, the hair test tells you if you have it sitting in your system, the urine test tells you if your body is expelling it which I think means you have excess, and I think they also do blood but can't remember much about that.Â  I could be wrong on my understanding.

I think a lot of folks have methylation issues and don't know it.Â  Now things really make sense to me about some things I have experienced.Â  It's so worth that $99 price to find out.

But, we have a plan so that is good and I got some tips from Jack to consider so I will meld the 2 and retest in 6 weeks to see if I'm making progress. She is changing my thyroid meds around a little bit and told me due to my gi effects test that I am probably not absorbing them and that is why taking 10 vs. 20 mcg. doesn't change it for me. My gut is not optimal for sure but she is working with me on that too.

So to recap, heavy metals, low Test, high estrogen, upside down cortisol, neuro isn't too bad, bad gut, parasite, Vibrio sp. (bacteria in the cholera family),

She is starting me on an doxycycline for a week and Forastor for the Vibrio, Alinia for the parasite for 3 days. I will start DMSA for the heavy metals but will also be taking my methyl b vitamins to help with methylation. She also added some supps to assist with detoxing the body. She has added paleofiber and probiotic 225 to also help with the gut.

I really like this doc. She listens and doesn't go by labs only. Is willing to work with you and considers a lot of options. I'm looking forward to a new year and a new me! Yes, this isn't linear but now I have some answers to my burning question of "what in the he!! is wrong with me" and now the path is shorter this year than last year. ### vkiernan — 2013-01-10T14:48:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-70177>

ZonulinFrom Wikipedia, the free encyclopediaJump to: navigation, search Zonulin is a protein that modulates the permeability of tight junctions between cells of the wall of the digestive tract. Initially discovered in 2000 as the target of zonula occludens toxin, secreted by cholera pathogen Vibrio cholerae,\[1\] it has been implicated in the pathogenesis of coeliac disease\[2\] and diabetes mellitus type 1.\[3\] It is being studied as a target for vaccine adjuvants.\[4\] ALBA Therapeutics is developing a zonulin receptor antagonist, AT-1001, that is currently in phase 2 clinical trials.

Gliadin (glycoprotein present in wheat) activates zonulin signaling irrespective of the genetic expression of autoimmunity, leading to increased intestinal permeability to macromolecules. \[5\]

From Brain Gut 2:
We evolved in the East African Rift Zone and it sits on three tectonic plates who have moved quite a bit since we first evolved. This rapid change has hidden our ascents secrets. The rapidity of the climate change also fueled the massive changes. Our guts became leaky because we began to assimilate virusâ€™s from shellfish that were closely related to vibrio species who make a zonulin like toxin. The RNA from these genetic golden nuggets means that hominids are the result of â€œepigentic viral marketingâ€œ.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-10T14:59:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-70182>

RESEARCHERS FIND INCREASED ZONULIN LEVELS AMONG CELIAC DISEASE PATIENTS
Researchers at the University of Maryland School of Medicine have found that the human protein zonulin, which regulates the permeability of the intestine, is at increased levels during the acute phase of celiac disease. The discovery suggests that increased levels of zonulin are a contributing factor to the development of celiac disease and other autoimmune disorders such as insulin dependent diabetes, multiple sclerosis, and rheumatoid arthritis. The findings are published in the April 29 issue of the journal Lancet.

"Zonulin works like the traffic conductor or the gatekeeper of our body's tissues," says lead author Alessio Fasano, M.D., professor of pediatrics and physiology at the University of Maryland School of Medicine, and director of Pediatric Gastroenterology and Nutrition at the University of Maryland Hospital for Children. "Our largest gateway is the intestine with its billions of cells. Zonulin opens the spaces between cells allowing some substances to pass through while keeping harmful bacteria and toxins out," explains Dr. Fasano.

Earlier research conducted by Dr. Fasano discovered that zonulin is also involved in the regulation of the impenetrable barrier between the blood stream and the brain, known as the blood-brain barrier.

Celiac disease offered Dr. Fasano and his team a unique model for understanding the dynamic interaction between zonulin and the immune system. Celiac disease is a genetic disorder that affects one out of every 300 people in Europe, but its prevalence in the United States is not fully known. People who suffer from the disorder are unable to eat foods that contain the protein gluten, which is found in wheat and other grains. The gluten sets off a reaction that can cause diarrhea, abdominal pain, malabsorption of nutrients, and other gastrointestinal problems. Celiac disease can be easily treated by avoiding foods with gluten.

With celiac disease, the body reacts to gluten by creating antibodies that attack the intestine and cause severe damage over time. Unlike other autoimmune disorders, scientists also know that celiac disease is triggered by a specific antigen, which is the protein gluten. Celiac disease is also known to cause increased permeability of the intestine. In addition, many people who suffer from celiac disease also suffer from other autoimmune disorders.

The research team examined the intestinal tissue of seven people with celiac disease, and six patients without the disease. Patients with active celiac disease showed higher levels of zonulin and anti-zonulin antibodies compared to non-celiac patients and patients in remission, who were eating a gluten-free diet.

"With celiac disease, we could never understand how a big protein like gluten was getting through to the immune system. Now we have the answer," explains Dr. Fasano. "People with celiac have an increased level of zonulin, which opens the junctions between the cells. In essence, the gateways are stuck open, allowing gluten and other allergens to pass. Once these allergens get into the immune system, they are attacked by the antibodies," adds Dr. Fasano.

"I believe that zonulin plays a critical role in the modulation of our immune system. For some reason, the zonulin levels go out of whack, and that leads to autoimmune disease," explains Fasano.

Dr. Fasano adds that more research is needed. He is currently conducting experiments with diabetic rats. Preliminary results from his experiments show that insulin dependent diabetes occurs in lab rats about three to four weeks after increased intestinal permeability. The researchers believe the increased intestinal permeability is associated with increased levels of zonulin.

Source: http://www.umm.edu/news/releases/zonulin.htm#ixzz2HbIUw6Du
Follow us: @UMMC on Twitter | MedCenter on Facebook

### vkiernan — 2013-01-30T20:47:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-73376>

Ok, since I haven't posted about what is happening lately, guess it's time. Been so busy with work and a mini sledding trip that I haven't had time. This whole week my stomach has been acting up and I don't know what from. A couple of things: no hunger or getting full really fast (just can't and don't want to eat more (I am never like this)), gurgles are back full force. Hmmm, they went away with the vibrio. K, now that they're back I'm hoping that is good. Back to taking a mag citrate before bed. Still doing the colostrum, paleo fiber and probiotics. I am also feeling more energetic in the afternoons and still more motivated at work. Something seems to be working for right now. Can only hope it continues. Been using a little melatonin since sleep isn't so good and I'm working on that too and won't be taking it for too long. It does help but I need to take 5 mg to really do the trick. I figure if you don't release melatonin and nothing else happens like it should, then I am not healing and that might be worse. All in context!

We went on a long ride (over 100 miles) Friday and I was sore Saturday. We also went on a long ride Saturday that we had planned (about 140 miles). Strange thing is, I took 5 mg melatonin before crashing at midnight on Saturday night when we returned home and I did not get sore. Saturday's ride was the ride from hell. The trails had seen a lot of traffic and no groomers. Every mile was a long one and very taxing on the whole body. I am wondering if by taking enough melatonin that I actually was able to allow my body to heal itself enough to make me not get sore. I swear I should not have been able to walk. Had to stand a lot of the miles too cuz the dips were horrendous.

My goal is to figure how to ground myself at work. I have a wireless internet router right outside my office door and now I believe that is a bad thing. Also wondering how bad my 2 monitors are for me. DH is realizing his sleep is crap and that he really doesn't have any energy. I am hoping he will be on board to really consider the 20 g mat for the bed. He says he is going to read the emf2 blog. Something needs to change in his life. Can't keep doing the same thing and expect good results to come of it.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-31T20:05:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-73496>

You can get a raw data download at [https://www.23andme.com/you/download/](https://www.23andme.com/you/download/)

You can also browse specific SNPs by going to "Account" in the upper right after and pulling down "Browse Raw Data". Then enter the name of a SNP (e.g. rs1801133 for MTHFR 677).

### fitness@home — 2013-01-31T20:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-73510>

Vicki,

Go to the website Geneticgenie.org. You can upload your raw data to the site and it will run a report for you.

### Lyndra — 2013-01-31T21:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-73512>

There are two ways to make sense of it that I know of. One is to let geneticgenie take care of it for you by converting the raw data into nicely formatted results. The other option is to find a list of SNPs that you are interested in and then do the lookup one-by-one yourself. Be aware that 23andme does not provide info on all methylation-related SNPs - the most notable missing ones are VDR Fok-I, BHMT-01, SUOX and NOS3.

Take a look at [http://forums.phoenixrising.me/inde...ndme-yasko-methylation-genetic-results.18309/](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/gestalts-genova-23andme-yasko-methylation-genetic-results.18309/) - on the first page you'll see an image showing a listing of methylation SNPs. You can look them up in 23andme by using the "rsxxxxxx" number.

### vkiernan — 2013-02-03T00:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-73855>

For those wondering about the hydrogen peroxide/Epsom salt baths, this is the best explanation I have found.

Actions of Hydrogen Peroxide/Epsom Salt/Ginger Bath

\* Hydrogen Peroxide - a simple, nontoxic molecule of H2O2 that is beneficial in oxidizing (breaking down) toxins as well as killing anaerobic microbes. It will also detoxify pesticides and petroleum-based toxins and oxidize metals. Hydrogen peroxide is also naturally produced by macrophages in the body to kill harmful bacteria. Most harmful bacteria are anaerobic and cannot survive in the presence of oxygen or hydrogen peroxide. Cancer cells are often very sensitive to increases in oxygen and cannot survive in oxygen-rich environments. When hydrogen peroxide is applied topically to a cut, the resulting bubbling is from the rapid release of free oxygen and, more importantly, from the idling of the bacteria in the cut. Friendly bacteria are aerobic and need and thrive on oxygen.

\* Epsom Salts (Magnesium Sulfate) - Recent research has shown that when one soaks in a bath of magnesium sulfate, both magnesium and the sulfate independently increase measurably in the blood and urine. Magnesium sulfate helps stabilize Hydrogen Peroxide so that its ultimate breakdown into H2O and free oxygen is more gradual and functional to the process of benefiting the body. This research documents the very real beneficial aspects of this bath therapy. The Epsom Salt Council reports the following about the benefits of Magnesium in Epsom Salt baths.

Magnesium, a major component of Epsom Salt (magnesium sulfate), is the second-most abundant element in human cells and the fourth-most important positively charged ion in the body. Magnesium also helps to regulate the activity of more than 325 enzymes and performs a vital roll in orchestrating many bodily functions, from muscle control and electrical impulses to energy production and the elimination of harmful toxins.

The National Academy of Sciences, however, reports that most Americans are magnesium deficient, which may account for our society's high rate of heart disease, stroke, osteoporosis, arthritis and joint pain, digestive maladies and stress-related illnesses, chronic fatigue and a host of other ailments. The Academy estimates the average American male gets just 80% of the magnesium required for good health, while females get only 70% of their recommended levels.

Nutritionists say Americans' magnesium levels have dropped more than 50% in the past century.

Raising magnesium levels may:

\+ Improve heart and circulatory health, reducing irregular heartbeats, preventing hardening of the arteries, reducing blood cots, and lowering blood pressure.

\+ Improve the body's ability to use insulin, reducing the incidence or severity of diabetes.

\+ Flush toxins and heavy metals from the cells, easing muscle pain and helping the body to eliminate harmful substances.

\+ Improve nerve function by regulating electrolytes. Also, calcium is the main conductor for electrical current in the body, and magnesium is necessary to maintain proper calcium levels in the blood.

\+ Relieve stress. Excess adrenaline and stress are believed to drain magnesium, a natural stress reliever, from the body. Magnesium is necessary fore the body to bind adequate amounts of serotonin, a mood-elevating chemical within the brain that creates a feeling of well being and relaxation.

Sulfate: The "sulfate" part of the Epsom Salt (magnesium sulfate). This form of sulfate must not be confused with "Sulfa" drugs, to which some people are allergic. People who are allergic to sulfa drugs will not react to sulfate in Epsom Salt. The benefits of sulfate in the body are summarized by Dr. Rosemary Waring of the School of Biosciences, in Birmingham, England:

\+ Oral supplementation of Sulfate cannot be easily absorbed across the gut walls; therefore, by soaking in this bath, sulfate can be more efficiently supplemented. Sulfate is essential for many biological processes.

\+ Sulfate is needed for formation of proteins in joints; decreased sulfate levels are common in the plasma and joint synovial fluids in cases of arthritis. Sulfate is also low in people with irritable bowel syndrome.

\+ Sulfate is necessary for sulfation, a major pathway in detoxifying drugs and endogenous (toxins from within the body) or exogenous (environmental ) toxins.

\+ Sulfate is necessary for the repair and formation of brain tissue. Reduced levels of sulfate can lead to faulty neurological connections and subsequent dysfunction.

\+ Sulfate is needed for proper digestion and for proper utilization of digestive enzymes.

\+ Sulfate is essential for the production of mucin, a protein which lines the intestines. Mucin stops food and drugs form sticking to the lining of the intestines. It also blocks the transport of toxins from the gut into the bloodstream.

\* Ginger - functions to open up the pores of the skin and increase the blood flow at the surface of the skin so that the oxygen from the hydrogen peroxide can do its work in detoxifying poisons and kill bacteria and fungi. The pores open wider so that the magnesium sulfate can be more readily absorbed. Ginger is invigorating and can stimulate preparation for the elimination of toxins.

It involves light box and humidifiers and lots and lots of plants in the house (or where ever I'll be living by then). \*Preferably some place small so as to accommodate these better.

### vkiernan — 2013-02-17T17:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-76206>

Time for an update since it has been awhile. Trying to not be in the "emf" zone so much which means less out here plus I don't have internet until dh comes home at night which is usually my bedtime. This has actually been a blessing in disguise for me as it will help me clean up my life around me. I have also been better about wearing my sunglasses at night. My sleep the last 2 nights has been a lot better and I have been able to sleep longer. I have also forgotten to take my last dose of t3 in the afternoon the last 2 days. Wonder if that has something to do with my better sleep. I am going to roll with it and see what comes of it.

My gut is "working" again. I had a bad weekend of gut issues a week ago and after that things started changing. It was almost like a detox thing - flush it all out or something. And, unbeknownst to be, it was pms week. Didn't realize it until after I had a blowup with dh and then when I finally came to my senses it became apparent. He also stopped smoking, which I am still in awe/shock of, and of course he was cranky from that. I told him next time he was going to make a like changing event to please ask me where I was in my cycle so I would know if I could support him or not. LOL! I hadn't a clue about this and he didn't tell me until he was already a couple of days in. He is doing great so far and I am extremely proud of him!!!!

I felt pretty good yesterday and today as well. I have one more week of chelation therapy and then we will see where things go. Doing my gi effects test tomorrow and the heavy metals next Sunday. Then it's off to the good doc to see where I fare. Pretty excited cuz I can tell at least my gut is better. Also seems like my skin is not nearly as dry as it was just a few weeks back.

The sun is coming up earlier and staying later. This makes me really happy. I am getting up just before it comes up and when I go home in the evening it is still light out. I like it that way.

Something has changed for the better with my focus at work. I am getting things done and have been for the last several weeks. I don't seem to dawdle as much trying to figure things out and my projects are coming together great. I have pushed the progesterone now for almost 2 months. Finally backing off of it this cycle for the 1st half and will monitor, of course, and send in the big guns if I need them.

Looking to get some D-ribose along with more coq10. Also still doing the detox baths and I wonder if that was part of last weekend. That sure was weird.

I have to get my butt into the cold water. Our house and work is always cold but I know that is not enough. I started CT'ing last February so I know I can do it again. I just have to "do it!"

Off subject, but I was just talking with my hubby about all of this emf stuff and I believe I have him on board to help me figure out how to minimize the emf's around. He is a computer guy so he can really help with this. I have a wireless access point right outside my office at work that he probably installed (they do our computer work) so I am going to see what can be done about it. I'm sure they can move it. Now to get my boss on board!!! This sort of stuff really excites me. All the little things one can do to help themselves. I am a whole lot more aware and am always looking and thinking about emfs.

Just wanted to note too that yesterday I did another detox bath (even got the dh to go with me) and I felt for the first time something like a caffeine rush. I cleaned the house and was pretty tired but I didn't get that fall asleep issue within a couple of hours. I even made it until about 8:30 before crashing. ### Lyndra — 2013-02-18T18:01:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-76322>

I think it was time that made the difference. I started on a low dose of Armour last June, then doubled it in August. I visited my doctor in November and just by looking at me thought I was hyper. When I told her about my symptoms - unexpected 5 pound weight loss, feeling hot all the time, racing heart & palpitations, and even worse sleep, she said I had too much thyroid hormone. We cut the dose in half and ran a thyroid panel. My FT3 was still on the low side but FT3/RT3 ratio had gone up to 22 (from 10). I stayed on the half dose until early this year when the racing heart & palpitations and constant feeling of being hot came back and I stopped it altogether. My energy is not as good as it was back in December but my heart rate is back to normal and sleep is improving (but I still need get rid of the hot flashes). I don't know if there are any lessons in this for you. I think I may need to try T3-only but will need a different formulation than I used initially last summer because it gave me headaches.

### vkiernan — 2013-03-07T20:46:24+0200

Another quick check in. Still doing a lot of things for my gut. Mood is holding stable and I am feeling a little happier again and sleep is soooo much better. I truly believe it was getting trashed from my new laptop last fall. I have backed way off the last 2 months and I'm sleeping. Matter-of-fact, I am not waking up on time to take my t3 and I am not worrying about it either. Bedroom is too warm though. Have to back the temp down further. 62 apparantly is too high. The DH and I are a little cold adapted. We realized this when we come home and the house is 64 and it is too warm. How is that possible?LOL I will take it. Makes me think that CT in 50 degree water is not far from that and should be a lot easier than last year this time when we all started and had no cold adaptation. I don't have time tonight or tommorrow but I have the whole weekend and will JUST DO IT! Looking forward to reaping the bennies that come from it like shedding a few lbs. We turn the clocks ahead this weekend and I've realize I am ready for bed by 8 and wake around 4:30 so this will throw me right into a good schedule from 9ish to 5 or 6ish and maybe start ct'ing in the am again.

A thought based on the last episode of the big D. I am starting to think it was a detox thing or too much mag and not the Vibrio. It has not reared its ugly head since. My breasts are not sore anymore either. This happended when I was CT'ing a lot and had the estrogen dump and it feels like that. Time to keep doing those baths along with ct.

Based on some of the things I have been reading out here I have started bringing my water from home again. Our well is deep and cold and the water tastes great. Don't know what is in it but I am working on testing it. I believe it has to be better than the filtered stuff I have been drinking at work that runs thru a lot of really old piping. Our house is only 12 years old so the piping should be good.

Really working on the DH for the Magnetico sleep pad but not gaining any ground. He doesn't have time to research it himself and I cannot explain it to him like I need to. He gets grounding but not the magnet like the earth intended. He told me to sit in a tub of water and I would be grounded. Is this true? Anyone? Why do we need to augment naturally occurring magnetism?

There are two major factors adversely affecting magnetic resonance.

First, a gradual cyclic decline in our geomagnetic field has left the atoms of our bodies in a lower energy state, making it more difficult for magnetic resonance to occur. 6 It is estimated that we have lost 70-80% of our magnetic field in the last 4000 years. 7,8,9,10

Second, in this technological age, the outside electromagnetic frequencies your body is exposed to are higher and stronger than ever before. As a result, these frequencies override the vital brain function of magnetic resonance with your organs and tissues. 11 This interference leaves you in a fatigued state that, over an extended period, can contribute to the development of chronic ailments. This development is due, in part, to a lack of restorative resonance. 11

How can you restore the magnetic field your body needs?

These deficiency states can be overcome by exposing your body to an enhanced steady state, magnetic field (much like the Earth’s) during sleep. In North America, the magnetic field must be pure negative and completely pass through the body in order to complement the negative field of the Northern Hemisphere. 11 By using our patented Magnetico Sleep Pad, you ensure your body will receive the right direction of magnetic energy, which is essential for organ and tissue restoration.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-16T18:25:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-80783>

H pylori is the bacteria that causes ulcers. It's treatable with antibiotics.

I had so many rounds of antibiotics when I was having major sinus issues caused by the silver pins in an old root canal that the last time the doc said I needed more I burst into tears in his office. I finally found Garden of Life's [Primal Defense](http://www.gardenoflife.com/ProductsforLife/DigestiveHealth/PrimalDefensesupsup/tabid/638/Default.aspx) and it fixed my gut. I still keep a bottle handy. When you read the guy's story you will be absolutely amazed. Fix your gut, fix your health.

As to sleep -- separate bedrooms for a while? Even though I'm not happy about it, my sleep is much better now that we each have our own bedroom.

boy - your sleep sounds totally affected by the TV situation. I would change it asap. I am dreaming of turning off all circuit breakers I don't need at night (i.e. except for the one outlet for my CPAP) but not sure if I can manage to get DH on board.

Trouble is, our mattress is a 10 inch thick latex one which I LOVE but not sure how much will 'go through' it.....and I have to convince DH yet, too...

### vkiernan — 2013-03-21T19:45:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-81341>

Saw my doc last night and it went well. My heavy metal cleanance has made great progress. So-much-so that I might only have to do this last round of chelation. Some of them dropped 75%. I am very excited about that and I think she was pleased as well. I am also doing methyl b vits from Neurobiologix too to help with methylation and chelating. Not sure I have the problem but I sure had a lot of alleles for it so I'm going with it. The results are speaking for themselves too so I am going to continue my current protocol. I am also eating a ton of mushrooms for the b vits and will continue with that as well.

She is putting me on GastroMend-HP for the H. Pylori since the last antibiotic further trashed my gut. Surprisingly, she is going to put me a probiotic that is organisms from the earth like Primal Defense. I am happy about that. Introduce something different and see how things go. She has to order it so it might be a week.

I am still having the bouts of the big D every 7 to 10 days and it lasts for about 2 days. No reason in sight yet. I have not done any special baths that might have been creating the problem or taken mag. Supposedly the Vibrio is gone so I have no reason and the doc doesn't either. I will be doing a full gut panel after I do the G-mend for the H. Pylori in between chelating and hopefully this will expose the culprit. I will also be retesting my metals at the same time.

She is looking into buying an amethyst pad for her patients to use (I'm sure there would be a charge but I am looking forward to trying it if she does). I have googled it and it sounds like it does everything the magnetico sleep pad does but more. I am very interested in this. Anybody know anything about it???

Used my calming cream to help me sleep and I think it is why I am tired today. Got to keep a journal on the stuff so I know. It sure helps the sleep though.

Looks like we will be heading North for one more blast of snowmobiling. We might as well since it is still very much winter here.

Got to get my cold squishy buns in the cold tub. Did it Monday and I froze that night.

Wouldn't surprise me about DMSA, Vickie.....with me - it totally made me tired. Of course it was at a very stressful time - DH had to look for work, we were deciding to move to DC for an opportunity and I couldn't function so my functional MD lol said to take a break and I never got back on it after four weeks (but I have been dumping LOTS of mercury I think bc of my iodine intake in the last year). Of course I was super super low on iodine and didn't know. I am sure I was super depleted all my life. In fact I have been convinced that I am at least a third generation low thyroid person. My grandma died at age 36 from a type of fever after childbirth. The MD who helped her give birth, he passed it around from another woman but I am convinced if her thyroid was stronger, she'd have survived - I don't care that it was WWII Germany. I sure wish she'd met Albert Einstein at the time since he lived there in his summer home..ugh. This spring for the first time ever my DH was having a bad cough and I did not get it - and I blame my better iodine and of course all the JK Rx's I am doing. And I can't wait to get the magnetico sleep pad. Have you thought about it more? I had to get it. Gotta try it for six months at least. There are too many things I think it could help with and ten years from now I wanna know I did all I could.

So anyway...the DMSA as it helps you dump the mercury and anything else it is able to dislodge from your body will naturally upset your gut flora.....if you have candida, as the mercury is taken out, candida is also killed and that can cause detox symptoms too - the candida is partially there to protect you from mercury (so you can't get rid of candida until you really get rid of mercury!), it's a fascinating thing. But this is ultimately a good thing, you just have to make sure you rebuild the flora and support it with Epcot and so on and stay on the foods you know are good for you. My DH doesn't get it either AT ALL about food. Though he does see how sick I get when I eat something I should't so I try not to.

Not surprised your mood would tank while chelating too. You are literally changing brain and body chemistry etc. Think of it as a kitchen remodel. Doesn't look too bad before. THen, you start ripping stuff out to get it ready for the new shiny stuff...and while that is happening it all looks awful, lots of dust...and then when the bad is out you put the new in and everything runs much better and looks better too.

I wish sometimes my DH would get it more about food and even light and wifi (my big struggle!!!!) and so on. I think in part it is because as a woman I am more sensitive to it all (and Dr K said it too in his webinar)....and also because I know what it's like to feel sick and not able to do all I want, as I used to when I lived in small town Germany and wasn't - in my belief - exposed to as much crap as I have been here in the NE US in the last 17 years. Well he doesn't. He doesn't know any different from his usual MO. His usual falling asleep TO the TV, ugh, low energy, blaming low libido on being in his 40s. I wonder too if Dr K wasn't so sick all those years ago, if he might have really dug as deep as he now has. I know I wouldn't. All my passion for health and so on is bc I know what it is like...

All the best to you!

Tme for a quick update. This weekend will be my last weekend for my 2nd go around with chelation and I will be testing Sunday/Monday for my heavy metals. Also, I should be close to being done with my gut repair for the H-pylori so I can test again for that too. I am getting tired of taking all of this extra stuff. Gets a little hard to keep track after a while.

Interestingly,at my last appt., my doc couldn't figure out why I wasn't getting better with having cleared a lot of metals out. I think I may have found out why. We have had a propane gas leaks in the house for a long time and just found out this past week. We are almost done with fixing them. The plumber will be coming out shortly to fix the last one. I have always smelled gas by my stove and in my bedroom closet. DH wasn't really on board with me until we ran out and the house smelled horrible from it. Thank god they put that smelly stuff in it. Our propane supplier replaced the regulator on the tank and on the house. This tells me things were pretty bad for a long time and that is why I have been smelling it for so long. I am hoping for good things to come my way once this whole thing is fixed. Pretty sure it contributed to my fatigue and god only knows what else.

Also noting is what the d-ribose is doing for me that I can tell. My hunger is pretty much gone. I can eat very little for lunch and supper and be fine. It is so strange, but nice, to not have to eat at 5pm anymore and not feel that crazy overwhelming hunger. This has been going on for about a week and half plus or minus. I have been taking the d-robose for a couple of weeks. This is really cool to actually feel a bennie from something. I take a lot of supps and really don't feel anything from anything except this.

Guess it's time for an update! Been working hard on the gut trying to clear the H-pylori, parasite (again), yeasty thing and getting my good bacteria up and my bad down. Interestingly, after a short time on the anitibiotics for the H-p, my gut changed almost overnight. Strange it was. I no longer have intermittent D which I didn't know where it was coming from after the Vibrio. Anyway, don't have that problem or constipation anymore either. Pretty amazing!

I believe I had die-off last week from treating the yeast the last several weeks. I was very fatigued (tired and no energy), didn't feel well and my joints ached like crazy. This week is much better. My sugar cravings are much better too and my mood is better. Still treating the yeast and have about another week to go then it is test time again!

I just had my hormones, thyroid and hs-Crp labs done. My thyroid had high FT3, low FT4 and very low RT3. We are upping my T4 and dropping my T3. At least my RT3 is staying down so I feel pretty positive about that. Estrogen is crazy high again and I am taking stuff to drop that. Need to CT to help too. T is a little high but not bad so I have to drop back a little on the bio's. My crp was 1.4 (1-3). My doc says it's fine but I think it is still too high. I still have crap wrong so need to work on that yet. Damn, if only I could get my squishy buns in the tub.......somehow I have to make it a priority!

I am also still chelating and have a few more months of that before testing. I can't wait for the day that I don't have to dump crap down my throat anymore.

Although I am still not full of energy, I do believe I am getting better even though it is slowly! I do want to start yoga. I have been saying that but just haven't gotten but I will. My shoulders and neck are full of knots and I think that would help greatly. Going to the torture chamber (massage therapy) again tonight! Will have to soak in epsom salts tonight to ward off the pain.

And, I just got off the phone with my dh and he said go ahead on the magnetico. Did I really hear that??? Yep, I did. He wants someome to work on his Z car and this is his tradeoff. I like having a business man for a dh!!! Guess I had better make the call before he changes his mind.LOL!

And, I have been making "sun" water. Gotta try anything I can to help my ferrari run better!

### vkiernan — 2013-09-10T20:55:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-late-than-never.961/post-95463>

Wow, it has been 2 months since I got the Magnetico and I have not reported a thing out here. I'm afraid to write anything as it always backfires but I think it's time. For the last couple of weeks I have been feeling better. Not so cranky and I have more energy and it lasts longer thruout the day. I also have a lot less hip/leg joint pain. I started borax a good month or so and upped my iodine to 25 mg a day. Something is working whether it's the magnet, the borax, the iodine, the dmsa, the gut stuff my doc still has me on or a combination of all of them but I will take it. I have been out here learning and working at healing myself since June of 2011 not to mention everything that I did prior to that. Jack is the only one that lead me down the right path to figure out what is wrong. That is a long time to be working on stuff but it feels so great to start to think I might be making progress. I think I need to go another couple of weeks to really know if I am making forward or just temporary progress.

What's more amazing is that all of my kids are now gluten free. My dd has celiac and we are pretty sure 1 of my ds's has it. The other ds decided to go dairy and gluten free for his wife (dairy free) and his brother (gluten free). He just had a baby and she has gerd and is on medicine for it. My ddil has been told to stop the dairy and see if that impacts the baby's gerd. She thinks it is helping but time will tell. My new granddaughter seems to gag/choke on occasion and I have to believe it is due to the reflux as she struggles to handle it. Watching her really scares me. I have to say that my 2nd ds use to think "mom" was crazy and would tell his bro to not listen to me well I think he now thinks different. Pretty funny actually. My other ds feels so much more alive and happy after ditching grains. I am so happy for him.

I love my magnetico. I am able to sleep in now and I never was able to do that. Now I want to sleep in everyday and can't wait til the weekend. Some days I come home and take a little snooze on it. Dh has stopped coughing after quitting smoking and I believe the magnet is helping him too. He would like to be better but he still doesn't do anything different besides not smoking. So, I wouldn't think he would make much progress either in other directions.

Got to get back to work. Busy time of the year here!

# Thread: Is menopause a must have in life?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-19T02:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16390>

Why menopause? Is it neolithic or?

Great minds on here have info?

### Souldanzer — 2012-04-19T02:18:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16398>

Interesting question.

I think it happens when a woman runs out of eggs? ... in that case, yep, a must.

### skline@peak.org — 2012-04-19T02:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16399>

From what I've read about it, menopause is unique to the human race. Animals just keep on pushing out babies till they drop dead.

I consider this evolutionary evidence that human women have things more useful to do for the survival and well-being of their immediate groups than just raising the population at the maximum possible speed.

Personally, I was delighted to stop the cramps, mess, bleeding, and emotional lability. Of course, Doc would probably say that these drawbacks stem from being "neolithic" and he might be right -- still, I think a lot of women feel like menopause is a ticket to freedom of many kinds. The freedom to do what one ought to be doing, what one is truly suited for. The freedom to choose what that is.

Whatever the purported benefits, no way is anyone going to get me to take enough hormones to start cycling again ...

### chocolate — 2012-04-19T03:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16422>

There is an article about reversing menopause. We can grow more eggs.

[http://www.news-medical.net/news/20120409/Eggs-developed-from-stem-cells-could-change-reproductive-technology.aspx](http://http://www.news-medical.net/news/20120409/Eggs-developed-from-stem-cells-could-change-reproductive-technology.aspx)

there are certain receptors on all tissues and organs. The get alarm signals and inflammation signals from the white fat alot. The way my peabrain understands it. The brown stuff needs activated. There are tons of cold rat studies. The study above is about human eggs grown from stem cells on ovaries. Its not donor eggs.

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1009-Leptin-production-lt-10-BF](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1009-Leptin-production-lt-10-BF)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16439>

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-19T04:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16449>

Looking at the cycle you have the maiden, mother, crone

The maiden is the the transition from child to woman at the onset of menses

Mother is when the woman actually becomes one

Crone is when the mother no longer can provide the sustenance in the womb or breast and transitions to an elder who helps guide the young women through the stages and supports the tribe w/child care etc. she is the midwife and shaman for the tribe

As to the physicality of stopping menopause I've never minded TOTM b/c I always too the 1st day for me (when I could swing it). I learned from the onset it was something to cherish for it was special. Given my hormonal craziness. I'd like to keep from loosing the hormones. But when I make the official transition, even if Im still on BHRT I'll wiling enter the circle of crones.

### chocolate — 2012-04-19T05:54:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16491>

[http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEUQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.buzzle.com%2Farticles%2Feffects-of-high-altitude-on-the-human-body.html&ei=GHyPT9-gCqbjiAKdy5HuAg&usg=AFQjCNGwQKy\_vXuOanizKSpoB5fgcsda4Q&sig2=jGJZPCdQGI5OnV6l1pAjrg](http://http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEUQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.buzzle.com%2Farticles%2Feffects-of-high-altitude-on-the-human-body.html&ei=GHyPT9-gCqbjiAKdy5HuAg&usg=AFQjCNGwQKy_vXuOanizKSpoB5fgcsda4Q&sig2=jGJZPCdQGI5OnV6l1pAjrg)

Effects of High Altitude on the Human Body

Well all of us have insulin receptor problems, usually from too much iron in the blood apparently. My kid showed elevated risk and her blood work showed it as well. This is supposed to be the cause of macular degeneration and lots of stuff. It is a symptom of high elevations. Increased mitochondria is also. The article is good to read. Just the altitude alone causes a default to ketosis. It doesn't matter what you eat up there,( to a point.)

Macular degeneration and insulin resistance at low elevations....probably the perfect calibration for high altitude.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-04-19T15:16:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16555>

>
> The maiden is the the transition from child to woman at the onset of menses
>
> Mother is when the woman actually becomes one
>
> Crone is when the mother no longer can provide the sustenance in the womb or breast and transitions to an elder who helps guide the young women through the stages and supports the tribe w/child care etc. she is the midwife and shaman for the tribe
>
>
>
> As to the physicality of stopping menopause I've never minded TOTM b/c I always too the 1st day for me (when I could swing it). I learned from the onset it was something to cherish for it was special. Given my hormonal craziness. I'd like to keep from loosing the hormones. But when I make the official transition, even if Im still on BHRT I'll wiling enter the circle of crones.

Hehe, not me. I plan on defying gravity.

### Shijin13 — 2012-04-19T15:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16557>

> **chocolate;16561 wrote:** [http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEUQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.buzzle.com%2Farticles%2Feffects-of-high-altitude-on-the-human-body.html&ei=GHyPT9-gCqbjiAKdy5HuAg&usg=AFQjCNGwQKy\_vXuOanizKSpoB5fgcsda4Q&sig2=jGJZPCdQGI5OnV6l1pAjrg](http://http://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=web&cd=4&ved=0CEUQFjAD&url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.buzzle.com%2Farticles%2Feffects-of-high-altitude-on-the-human-body.html&ei=GHyPT9-gCqbjiAKdy5HuAg&usg=AFQjCNGwQKy_vXuOanizKSpoB5fgcsda4Q&sig2=jGJZPCdQGI5OnV6l1pAjrg)
>
> Effects of High Altitude on the Human Body
>
> Well all of us have insulin receptor problems, usually from too much iron in the blood apparently. My kid showed elevated risk and her blood work showed it as well. This is supposed to be the cause of macular degeneration and lots of stuff. It is a symptom of high elevations. Increased mitochondria is also. The article is good to read. Just the altitude alone causes a default to ketosis. It doesn't matter what you eat up there,( to a point.)
>
> Macular degeneration and insulin resistance at low elevations....probably the perfect calibration for high altitude.

This might explain why I always feel better at altitude, and sleep better at altitude as well.... things to think on....

### chocolate — 2012-04-19T18:05:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16602>

Its awfully hard to build muscle post meno. I am trying to get myself as strong as I can and the keto and cold do it best. Plus, I'm not so thrilled with waning eyesight. I guess the definition of pamper is relevant. I hate being weak and the off being balance that comes from weakness is frightening. I used to work at the airport and the people that pamper themselves are the weakest. You will see an 85 year light bit of fluff walk hard and fast for a distant gate with a wheelchair aide chasing her and the woman crabbing the whole way for them to leave them alone. If its an strong spirited old man (not the same as pushy and demanding) , you have flirt and get edgy to get him to let you "help". Then you'll have very large sixty something couples wanting someone to push the ass they don't even want to haul. I don't want to be those pampered people.

But on the flip side, the post menopausal skilled and classy are wonderful. I home schooled in LA and had my kids in several parks and rec daytime adult classes. The adults that take those classes in our area are retirees and wonderful. They share and give and enjoy, We were very blessed. The ice rink in the daytime has the same, and you get tons of free lessons from people that actually know what they are talking about. Some of my best mentoring came from college professors that had my elementary aged kids in their classes. They were peeved at first but started coaching me a little when I would hit a wall. They all hit bumps with their kids and would share how to get over them or how they might have done things differently, rethinking. I appreciated it, because I need constructive criticism.

Glad there wasn't a lipitor ad with the article.

### Croak — 2012-04-19T18:35:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16613>

I think the "live long and pamper" was referring to pampering the progeny, not yourself.

### MamaGrok — 2012-04-19T20:42:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-16683>

I don't think menopause = running out of eggs. We're "supposed" to have only a few dozen cycles in our lives, not hundreds. Most of the eggs will go unused in that situation, no?

I think that menopause has got to be far worse now than it's supposed to be. I know for a fact that cycles are not supposed to be painful, or preceded by hormonal uproar. They're also not supposed to happen for most of our lives. Biologically, they're supposed to start around 15, be followed soon thereafter by a pregnancy, then disappear for 3 years or so till the next one or two cycles are followed by another pregnancy; rinse and repeat for a few decades.

I don't know when they're "supposed" to end, biologically, though. I'm still looking for data from traditional peoples that shows the typical age of menopause, and duration of perimenopause (which I also suspect is artificially long now).

Menopause is NOT supposed to be accompanied by hot flashes & hormonal uproar, or followed by a decline in hearing, eyesight, bone composition, etc., etc.

I read an interesting article in the Price-Pottenger Nutrition Foundation journal that gave a nice explanation of why modern "age-related" decline takes far longer and is far worse than it's supposed to be. Yes, hormones decline with age, but today they decline far too early and far too much.

I do think menopause is a gift (just as my regular intervals of 3 years without cycles for the last 15 years are a gift), and that the post-menopausal woman should be a gift to the community, not relegated to "irrelevant" status the way she has become post-60's ("Don't trust anyone over 30").

### kathylu — 2012-04-20T15:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-menopause-a-must-have-in-life.975/post-17003>

> **MamaGrok;16753 wrote:**
>
> I don't know when they're "supposed" to end, biologically, though. I'm still looking for data from traditional peoples that shows the typical age of menopause, and duration of perimenopause (which I also suspect is artificially long now).
>
>
>
> Menopause is NOT supposed to be accompanied by hot flashes & hormonal uproar, or followed by a decline in hearing, eyesight, bone composition, etc., etc.

Just another N=1, but my mother-in-law didn't stop regular periods until she was 66! She had 7 children, which I'm sure contributed to the late menopause. She has amazing skin and energy and looks far younger than her 80 years.

### MJ* — 2012-04-20T21:41:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-17172>

> **Jim;17086 wrote:** So which ice packs are you using? BTW I am originally from Syosset but now live in Virginia so unfortunately I can't make it. My tap water is now 60F.

I use a few different ones, but the brand Coldpac by Chattanooga is my newest...what I love about it is it's larger size...I got the 11x14 and it covers my whole stomach, and it's pliable so I can squeeze it into wherever I have freezer space...they also have a larger size that was more money that I might invest in eventually. I also picked up a face mask that you freeze (found in the beauty isle at King Kullen) I love that too, it's soothing and I'd swear the skin above my eyes is looking tighter! Then I use these smaller ones called thermacool, and what I love about these is they stay icy longer...I try to put these where the brown fat is...Why is everyone moving away? I love LI...I grew up in Hicksville, our towns bordered I think...

I leave near Wincoma and not far from Crescent Beach, Huntington Bay.

### Claudia — 2012-04-27T16:45:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19323>

NIce photo Dr. Kruse. Still taking baby steps over here. I have done 55 cold baths so I don't think 52 will be that different, but it will be full immersion so we'll see and I have done no CT at all this week. IN my bath, I have my arms out and shoulders out of the water, gotta hold my book to read. I stay in 30 minutes andy haven't been able to extend that more than that.

I think the hcg is making me colder this week. So Sunday will be a huge challenge and it will be down to 30s at night and maybe 40s when we're there.

Maybe I will try to meet you for BAB anyway, but I won't be eating anything. I'll make sure to have my protein before I leave. I'll see how it goes that morning.

> How about Hesse Trail head in Eldora - nice creek with icy snowmelt. There's a really nice spot at the end of Eldorado Canyon Trail where it meets Walker Ranch... icy blue creek. Only stuck my feet in last time b/c I didn't know any better.
>
>
>
> Where have all the Boulder Superhumans gone? Cavemam? Lyndra?

Seriously! We live in the perfect place where we can watch the sun go down behind the mountains and sit around firelight after dark - all after a day of cold dips and whatnot.

I have a sadly deficient knowledge of the secluded mountain areas/streams. I really REALLY want to find a place. I'm very motivated to take my son camping and enjoy real dark/real quiet/real fire. And of course a cold mountain stream would rock.

Wonder if the REI folks might know, or if there are camping clubs we might ask?

### Souldanzer — 2012-04-29T01:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19686>

There are spots all around you, actually. Heck, the Boulder Creek is a populated summer time dip... nobody would even notice. And still cold b/c of the snow melt. Eben J Fine park is bikini central in the summer.

There's another place farther up on the Boulder Canyon trail where there's a tiny little "beach". Always taken in the summer. Just got to be careful with the speed of the water current at times of the year with the creeks. If you go further up there's a place where people have put rocks and stuff to make a little "cold tub" that diverts the current.

Hiker over here  I wish my head wasn't still hurting like hell ... I'd be out there in no time.

Oh, and if you go up the first part of Bear Canyon Trail, past NCAR, there's another little "tub". My dog likes to go there for a dip when we pass by.

None of these places are particularly private unless you want to add a day hike. But they're not necessarily exposed, either. And who cares anyways... Keep Boulder Weird.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-29T01:59:14+0300

> Begin civil twilight 5:44 a.m. Sunrise 6:12 a.m.

Love it.....sunrise pics.

### Jim — 2012-04-29T15:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19758>

OK I did it...here's the proof.

Sunrise

Beaverdam Reservoir

Air temp 41 deg F

Water temp 55 deg F

First let's make sure CTing is legal....

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

I see nothing in the rules that say anything about CT so it must be fine.

Now let's survey the scene.

I must say that it is beautiful at dawn. This is the boat ramp area for the crew teams.

OK remember I am taking these by myself....notice how I chopped off the top of my head in this one. I guess the camera somehow knew this was a bad hair day!

As you can see I have lost a good bit of my visceral fat but still have a lot of pesky subQ fat to go.

So Ok quit stalling...time to go in...

So I stayed in for about 15 minutes or so. It was very easy given the water temp and the lack of wind. I hope my LI brethren fared well today and weren't eaten by a horseshoe crab or something.

### Claudia — 2012-04-29T17:49:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19803>

Hey, everyone. I did it. I have no idea what happened to everyone else. I had my breakfast here at home, went to Sweet Mama's to meet everyone. I did say I would be later since I wasn't eating. No one there. So, I drove to Sunken Meadow beach by myself and took the plunge, despite the many signs that said Swimming Prohibited. I took photos with my phone but couldn't get online, no reception.

No idea the water temp, but guessing low 50s. Air temp 48-50. I stayed in for 15 minutes, up to my shoulders but with my arms out, from 9:45-10am. I am home now still shivering. I am so excited that I did it !!!! I will post pics in a bit.

### Claudia — 2012-04-29T17:50:35+0300

### Claudia — 2012-04-29T18:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19820>

SOrry your wife is sick, PaleoDentist. My husband pulled something in his side lifting something heavy yesterday and was moaning and groaning all day and night. I told him to ice right away and he listened but then, he had spasms and blamed it on the ice. ANyway, he said he couldn't watch the kids this morning and I hesitated and thought maybe I shouldn't go. And then, I said well, do your best, and I went away . I am proud of this, too. I never put me first. They all managed just fine. I made them breakfast before I left.

### PaleoDentist — 2012-04-29T18:15:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19822>

Hi, my wife was throwing up all night and morning. I could not leave her. Sorry I let you all down. Hopefully e will do it again soon.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-04-29T18:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19824>

WHAT A BUMMER about that Claudia. Well, you Jim and I did the deed! It was exciting, right?

All warmed up now, here are stats:

air temp 63, water temp 51. stayed in for 25 min, up to neck, was in a very sheltered area called the cove, right by Shark River Inlet. I wanted to be safe since I was going alone, I also checked tides, it was low tide, so cove + LT = I felt safe. I was so excited on the drive down. I marched right down to shoreline, dumped sweatshirt and ran whooping like a lunatic in a tank top and booty shorts right into the water. THe guys fishing on the jetty were laughing. I swam, treaded water, bodysurfed, and sometimes just stood still. It was wonderful. There was a nook by the jetty where I could sit with the water up to my chin, but that was boring, I wanted to move around and play. AWESOME. I will definitely do this again!

### Claudia — 2012-04-29T18:35:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19832>

Can't upload my photos, they are too big 1.1 and only 97 allowed. Any ideas?

### Claudia — 2012-04-29T18:37:54+0300

### Dali Dula — 2012-04-30T04:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-19988>

I knew you could do it. It is better standing up and being able to move. Body surfing in the cold is a blast. I was with you all in solidarity in the 48 deg pond this morning.

So I've been doing this pond thing almost everyday for the past 30 days. Tonight i looked at my glucometer which stores the BG averages.30 day average is 119. 14 day average is 113. 7 day average is 109. 1day average(today) is 104. I am off all meds at this time.

Three years ago I was diagnosed diabetic with a A1c of 13. WTF! Everybody get in the water!

Hey, Tom, I missed this because I haven't been on. I also went in the sound on Mother's Day. I wanted to go to the beach so we went to Sunken Meadow. I didn't know what temp it was but I went all the way in this time past my shoulders and felt completely fine, stayed in for 30 minutes. My H was worried about hypothermia. I didn't have the strength to argue with him. My kids went in, too, and were fine. I imagine it was probably the same water temp as yours, around 58.

### MJ* — 2012-05-27T21:14:23+0300

### PaleoDentist — 2012-05-27T23:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-27920>

Well, we finally did it! So nice to meet both of you! It was a blast, and not as hard as I thought it would be. (although I was the only one who shivered) The water was actually really nice.

Great talking to both of you and getting your real world perspectives.

MJ: very impressive for someone who never did a bath plunge!

Claudia: I am inspired how you are raising your whole family Paleo. There is hope for my son!

Hopefully we will make it at least a monthly meet up. It would be great to have others join us as well. we can chat as we ocean plunge.

My goal is to do the Long Island Polar Bear Plunge in Feb!

I'm looking forward to the pics!

Alex

### MJ* — 2012-05-28T02:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-island-swim-meet-up.977/post-27942>

Well, we finally did it! So nice to meet both of you! It was a blast, and not as hard as I thought it would be. (although I was the only one who shivered) The water was actually really nice.

It was awesome!

Great talking to both of you and getting your real world perspectives.

We all had a lot to talk about!

MJ: very impressive for someone who never did a bath plunge!

I'm telling ya, ice packs rule, get your ice packs on so you're not such a shivering wimp next time

Claudia: I am inspired how you are raising your whole family Paleo. There is hope for my son!

I agree it was really inspirational to see how you are bucking the trend!

And Alex it is obvious you really care about your patients, and their overall health...you're a very good guy!

Hopefully we will make it at least a monthly meet up. It would be great to have others join us as well. we can chat as we ocean plunge.

Yes we will definitely do this! Kristen we can't wait to meet you and anyone else that wants to ever make the trip!!!

My goal is to do the Long Island Polar Bear Plunge in Feb!

A great goal! \[

I'm looking forward to the pics!

Haha they were far away and the towel shots were not great, but the ones I took of you and Claudia look great! I will make sure she's good with them and then post, unless you want to see them first too...
