Thanks Lin, it's good to read those again. In fact I read it again and again! Still confused - wondering if parts of me won't get any more leptin sensitive than I am? My HS-CRP is good, my BS is good, heart rate blah blah - so I just don't know still how this will apply to me.

In real world application, the days I've done "steak days" have been fairly easy to do. Yesterday my energy was good. The one before that it was a little slower. No emotional ups/down or any other signs that I am NOT buring fat for fuel

So yesterday I did a "steak day" and it also qualifies as IFing too. I did have a bit of fat through the day. I also checked my blood sugars after eating and they seemed good.

I think the best thing I heard from you is "having to start over" and eliminate. I keep outhinking this one. . .the phrase "hypervigilant" came out in another post. For me that type of behavior is a sign of not right. It doesn't gel with my relaxed, low stress, enjoying nature Inger type way of being, so I "feel" like I'm on the wrong path when doing that. OTOH, is it addiction and me being stubborn and not embracing change?

Thanks all for reading and commenting - having someone else observe and comment is extremely valuable!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-06T17:15:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43211>

End of 2nd week of P3 of HCG Round.

211.8, Down 3 lbd from yesterday, 3.6 lbds up from LIW (tmi but I need to know when looking back had a MBM before weighing).

Was verrrrry tempted to IF and steak day it again today. I'm sure tomorrow will go up from the BM alone.

Trying something new today. Having my protein upon waking, but not a BAB. I've been thinking a lot about if I'm storing more of the larger meals than utilizing them, due to bypass. Jack if your reading - is this even a possibility? I really need to figure out how all that works. Can't find much info on the googles.

Bfast: Coffee, HWC, Cocoa powder, spices, mct oil, 3oz or so of leftover sausage.

Hungry at about 3 hrs after starting bfast. BG 80. Thinking of food.

Snack: three hrs after starting sab, green tea w/ CO oil. (after claiming I have no bypass issues - this great amount of CO (4 tbs about) gave me a fat dump! I think I ended up losing all my lunch thus causing hunger between lunch/dinner. hehe my hope is since I cleaned my pipes that will help with lbds off for tomorrow. I know I know, no bitching on how insane and wrong that is. Don't hate me because I want to be pretty!

Lunch: Sole, shrimp, tomatoes, onion, garlic, bone broth, fresh herbs all in a stew. 1p, so CO worked for hunger - plus friend stopped by and lunch delayed. One hour after lunch BG 98

Snack: Hungry again about 2 hrs after lunch. After spending a.m. learning about digestion after bypass decided to have coffee with bullet proof protein mix. Starting to think snacking may be something I need.

Dinner: 5 meatballs (onion, garlic, anchovies, coconut flakes, seasonings and gelatin), about 1/2 cup of spaghetti squash and about 1/2 cup of tomato sauce (homemade). Should not have had the coffee w/protein - it ended up making me delay dinner cooking because I wasn't hungry. Probably should have just started cooking and having dinner closer to 5 than 6. So now bedtime no earlier than 10. BUT, a/c is working now so I'm expecting a nice cool sleep! Forgot, also had 1 strawberry 2 raspberries and a black berry.

Fasting BG, 77

30 mins after drinking coffee and eating sausage, 88

1 hr after 84

3 hr after 80 - feeling hungry, 3 hrs after starting sab.

1 hr after lunch 98

Noticed the sniffles after eating sausage! Same congestion as last night at dinner. Only thing in common perhaps is spices? I used a jeanie-awesome spice rub on my steak, but sausages were pre-made (the good stuff with actual ingredients ftw).

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-06T20:05:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43247>

In case you are a bypass person and want some info. . I came across this paper that has some good nuggets.

[http://www.lapspecialists.com/gw\_publication-\_physiology\_after\_gastric\_bypass.pdf](http://http://www.lapspecialists.com/gw_publication-_physiology_after_gastric_bypass.pdf)

Actually, this is a great explainer for anybody with an interest in what happens to food once it goes inside the body.

Summary:

The part of the stomach that normally processes protein is bypassed.

Due to the severing of vagal nerve the "stuff" that is normally secreted to help with protein also cuts way back. With me, Jack says vagal nerve has grown back - so one can hope that at least the secretions are upregulated to normal levels. All of this means much of the protein molecules remain intact and too large to be processed even later on in the small intestines. Protein deficiency is something to watch out for.

I don't have any symptoms of protein deficiency, so my thinking is perhaps I need to make sure to eat MORE protein? I need to figure out if I need to eat smaller portions more often. Perhaps I need to let go of the leptin reset stuff and think about my special needs in regard to best nutrition? Perhaps although 3 meals no snacks is best for most - it might be counter productive to me?

Similar to protein, the carb absorption is reduced as well, for the same reason. The initial breakdown of food happens in the part of the intestines (duodenal) that was bypassed. So perhaps I need to have a little bit of carbs to prevent the protein I do eat from being turned in to glucose, as protein absorption and utilizing those proteins should be towards the top of my eating hierarchy?

Guess what - same with fats. The portion bypassed is key to fat absorption. Fat doesn't form the spheres/groups needed and can end up in the colon undigested and pooped out (steatorrhea).

REALITY CHECK: This all sounds awful, but over I don't have - and didn't have, many of the side effect symptoms. No dumping from carbs and fat, no protein deficiency and no oily poo.

There also seems to be a correlation between all the nutrient issues and how "skinny" someone gets. In that regard, I'm in good stead - the operation didn't exactly work wonderful for me. AND, looks like that could end up being a very good thing for my version of optimal.

Clearly, maximizing nutrition and absorption is something I should be doing. Perhaps that explains run-away hunger even when I'm eating well? My body isn't absorbing it like most. It does make me wonder if trying to eat a couple of big meals a day is optimal for me.

Will I have more nutrient absorption eating several smaller meals through the day?

Perhaps I need a staggered program of grazing/fasting. I wonder would my ideal day look like this:

Continue to mind circadian stuff. No eating after dark and 4 hrs before bed. But upon waking, eat protein and fat in the a.m., add in a few carbs later in the day. Try to eat as many nutrient dense food - but in small quantities until it's stop time for eating. Every week or so, do a 24 hr fast as long as energy is up and feel good.

### NWgirl — 2012-08-07T02:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43320>

Cavemam, this is great info, great food for thought. I bet it's a different effect between the different wls surgeries though. For instance I had a DS duodenal switch so fats are more of a problem for me to absorb. But I do think I have some protein issues. But someone that had a RnY would be slightly different.

I am down 150 pounds from my highest weight so I guess it 'worked' for me, but I am by no means a normal weight. That is the frustrating part for me. So it still left me fat but also deficient.  I'm kinda bummed.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-07T02:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43324>

> **NWgirl;43607 wrote:** Cavemam, this is great info, great food for thought. I bet it's a different effect between the different wls surgeries though. For instance I had a DS duodenal switch so fats are more of a problem for me to absorb. But I do think I have some protein issues. But someone that had a RnY would be slightly different.

>
> I am down 150 pounds from my highest weight so I guess it 'worked' for me, but I am by no means a normal weight. That is the frustrating part for me. So it still left me fat but also deficient.  I'm kinda bummed.

I'm about the same on the weight loss. Lost a lot, but still "fat". I would read the paper, its great seeing how things normally work, and then after bypass - there are some observations about DS vs RNY in there, particularly pertaining to the fat thing.

I've been struggling with only 3 meals, but doing it for a long time on the reset - I will try and talk with Jack about this at my next consult. What I don't understand is: Will the same amount of nutrients be absorbed if eaten at only 2-3 meals per day, or will we absorb our food better with grazing/small meals. I completely get how it's important NOT to snack, and to give our bodies the rest between meals - but at some point it's rock and hard place. I'm not trying to be difficult, I will do what I think is right - but I wonder if trying to only eat 3 meals is hurting absorption with WLS folks. I have had consults with him, and he didn't say anything about altering the RX for the bypass so I could be over thinking this completely.

### LinD — 2012-08-07T04:49:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43356>

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-07T16:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43411>

3rd week of "stabalizing" after HCG round. Nothing about this has been stable - so best advice seems to be keep in P3 until you go three weeks stable. I have vaca in Vegas next week. Should be do-able. Do I want to be the crazy lady that packs a scale because the hotel doesn't have one? Perhaps I should just spend the money on daily fee's at a spa each day!

I see for me, looking at the scale everyday as a way of tracking my labs. If I were in a "healthy range" on weight, it wouldn't be necessary to do it so often - but as it is, it can be important possibly.

I'm a woot wooting today. Part of me thinks perhaps my body was rebelling against all the heat (our a/c was out for about 5 days).

210.8, Down 1 lbd from yesterday. Up 2.4 from LIW.

No eggs or nuts (other than Macadamias) the last 3 days. I am going to stay egg/nut free another day - then perhaps "test" one. I was not really sensitive to the HWC and yay on that. Perhaps eggs are ok too. 6 days till vacation! Ate tomato and onion - lost weight both days.

Bfast, Coffee w/ HWC, mct oil, CO, protein powder, 5 meatballs leftover from night before

Lunch, Breve at Boxcar and a disc of 75% chocolate, 1/2 bison ribeye, littlebit o broccoli/asparagus.

Snacks: I sipped greentea and coffee through the day that had fat and protein powder with coffee.

Dinner: 1/2 bison ribeye, littlebit o broccoli/asparagus.

Hunger very good during the day. I actually wasn't hungry for Lunch - but mini-me and I were having a day out down on Pearl St. He likes sitting with me at Boxcar playing ipads. He plays math games, and I read Jack-posts. It's awesome  Each time we do it, I imagine him as an older man remembering that every time he steps into a real and awesome coffee shop. He was super hungry for lunch so we went to Ted's (they have kid buffalo sliders and don't balk at no buns).

I decided to eat lunch, and try and skip dinner since I was actually working with clients and was not going to be having dinner with the family. Ended up eating the leftovers after got done with work. I think it was habit eating not hunger eating. I was a little hungry, but it wasn't REAL hungry for dinner. I want to keep IDing this and making progress on understanding when my body actually needs food.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-08T16:30:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43647>

210.4, Down .4 from day before, 2lbds over LIW

My hunger and eating over the last 3 days seems to have a sustainable rhythm to it, so I think I'm going to coast along until after vacation with no nuts and eggs.

What I've been doing differently: Drinking fat-drinks through the day and added in protein powder. After reading so much about post-bypass engineering, It hit me that I keep trying to put my square peg in a round hole. I need to figure out what is best for me. If that means letting go of the no snacking thing in order to get enough nutrients absorbed then perhaps that is it. Of course, the ol demons on the other shoulder are going "NO, YOU ARE QUITTING, YOU ARE MAKING EXCUSES JUST TO INDULGE IN WHAT YOU WANT VS WHAT YOU NEED! FATTIE FATTIE!" Those demons are like terrible teenage girls. Mine is named "Kelly Woodward". She was a horrid beeeeatch that lived next to me and made my life hell. You will have to buy the book for the entire awkward and painful story. It involves a dead rabbit and a long stick.

Breakfast: Coffee, HWC, MCToil, Cocoa powder, spices, protein powder, about 4oz sausage.

Lunch: macadamia nuts, more tea with CO, square o dark chocolate, bacon wrapped scallops

Snack: macadamia nuts, more tea with CO, lobster tail w/butter, coffee w/protein powder

Dinner: Can of sardines with salsa on top.

Gotta say, I am craving red meat way more than seafood even though I'm eating more and more of it. I wonder if my low iron is making me seek it out? I must stop with the Macadamia nuts. I need to just fix some meat damnit! By the time I suck down two handsfulls of those, I coulda had a small steak!

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T21:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43729>

> **Cavemam;44018 wrote:** What? Are you saying plotting my revenge on her for 30 yrs isn't the way to go? hehehe, actually - I was never a revenge type person - although I do suffer from the schadenfreude when it comes naturally.
>
>
>
> So she came to mind as she is an essay in my David Sedaris type book. I'm working on her chapter now! I am NOT changing her name either, as the radio lawyer I listen to said once that truth is a defense for slander.

My point was more that people can only hurt you when you allow them to. If you hold on to something that happened years ago, you're still letting that person have power over you, in some small way. Although, I suppose writing a book could be quite cathartic in that regard. Cast off all the bad juju all at once, kiss their worthless asses goodbye, and get on with your fabulous future. In fact, if I ever write an autobiography, I'm going to call it "Kiss Your Worthless Ass Goodbye"...LOL

### NWgirl — 2012-08-09T05:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43816>

Hi Cavemam - as a bypass person myself I'm hanging on to your every word. What type of protein powder do you use, and do you put anything else in it? I'm assuming it doesn't effect your blood sugar, I really hope I'm the same way. I just wonder if eating protein powder between meals will make me hungrier in the long run. I'm really liking this not having to eat every two hours thing.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T16:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43886>

211.6, up 1.2 from day before. Poo. 3.2 lbs over LIW.

Steak day. With fat laden drinks.

HIIT: 5X, 20-40 sec, hr up to 165, 6-9.5 mph

b: coffee, butter, CO, MCT oil

L: coffee, butter, CO, MCT oil

D: salmon and an apple.

Energy today was great. I've noticed I have more energy on fat fast days than real food days.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T18:41:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43911>

> **NWgirl;44107 wrote:** Hi Cavemam - as a bypass person myself I'm hanging on to your every word. What type of protein powder do you use, and do you put anything else in it? I'm assuming it doesn't effect your blood sugar, I really hope I'm the same way. I just wonder if eating protein powder between meals will make me hungrier in the long run. I'm really liking this not having to eat every two hours thing.

ok, so I used the bullet proof whey stuff jack has on here. What I noticed was it had zero effect on things when I had it with a meal (like put a scoop in my coffee with breakfast). But I'm not sure having it outside of a meal was a net positive. My blood sugar went up a teeny bit - but BS is not a problem for me in general. My fasting is 77ish, and highest is under 100 after meals. Of course, I've not checked it with a high carb meal as I'm eating pretty low carb these days. If I can ever get stable with the current weight - my plan is to test blood sugars with foods and see how quickly the transition from eat to increase BS is and if certain foods react better or worse than others.

SO, I haven't tested it more than a couple of days - but I think my hunger was lesser between meals without it. And - as Anne pointed out I don't seem to have any symptoms of protein deficiency so perhaps I don't need to worry about it.

I seem to be able to eat a very high fat diet - like 80%+ in fat. . . .this is where we are going to vary drastically. I'll say more about that later! Right now, off to show a house!

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T20:25:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43925>

> **ealachan;44020 wrote:** My point was more that people can only hurt you when you allow them to. If you hold on to something that happened years ago, you're still letting that person have power over you, in some small way. Although, I suppose writing a book could be quite cathartic in that regard. Cast off all the bad juju all at once, kiss their worthless asses goodbye, and get on with your fabulous future. In fact, if I ever write an autobiography, I'm going to call it "Kiss Your Worthless Ass Goodbye"...LOL

sigh. that's something only someone who has never been damaged by hurtful behavior can say.

My on-again, off-again therapist (who is beyond insightful!) gave me some scientific joo-joo about why some of us have difficulty letting go of traumatic memories. It seems (according to some major scientific honcho at some big name school who's name now escapes me) that TRAUMA inhibits the proper storage of these kinds of memories. Thus they play and replay in your head as your brain tries to put them away.

TAPPING will short circuit this replay and help you put those memories into storage where they will no longer bother you. Yes. It works. Why don't I use it more? It hurts to replay those memories which you HAVE to do in order to break the pattern. But it's a tool I know I have and which I (on rare occasions) drag out and work with. THAT will fix this stuff. Not trying to wish it away or forcefully PUT it away. Biology dictates a different path. And sometimes a cigar is just a dick.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T20:28:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43927>

Besides, human protein requirements are much less then we used to think years ago, so I doubt you are protein deficient on primal diet, especially if you don't have any symptoms of it. And also it would add yet more calories to your daily intake.

Cites please. I don't take anyone's word for anything on the internets. Except for Jack, of course.

### ealachan — 2012-08-09T20:34:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43928>

> **DarleenMB;44220 wrote:** sigh. that's something only someone who has never been damaged by hurtful behavior can say.

That made me laugh out loud, quite frankly. I've sustained my fair share of damage and done my fair share of therapy. Sounds like my therapist and yours had vastly different tacts. To each their own.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T20:56:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43935>

Therapy - Schmaripy, that's feel good stuff I am not wired for. I think I may be separated at birth from Kathy Griffith - there is NOTHING I can't joke or find humor in. NOTHING.

I Lump it in with Sports, Yoga, feeling joy at the beauty of a flower . . etc. All of of those things (including the god gene) are completely missing. Like to me, if you said "I've taken up the sport of throwing pebbles up my cootch, and hope to go to the olympics next time" that would make as much sense to me as what any other olympian is doing. I see zero difference in throwing pebbles up your cootch and running around a track or swimming laps.

This has made having friends very difficult because not only don't I care, but when 99.9% of the rest of the world feels the need to tell me about those things - I also feel like I'm being a liar mouth by smiling and nodding. So I often feel I have to tell them I think they are retarded! Otherwise I'm lying to them, and that is no way to treat a friend.

CLEARLY the planet I hail from gives me different genetic switches cause that stuff NEVER has the same impact on me. I've often wondered if I have some weird version of aspie that is combined with extremely empathetic for SOME things, yet sociopathic in my lack of regard for others.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T21:10:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43940>

> **Anna K.;44117 wrote:** I'd think protein in between the meals could affect insuline and hunger, I'd stay away from protein between the meals. Besides, human protein requirements are much less then we used to think years ago, so I doubt you are protein deficient on primal diet, especially if you don't have any symptoms of it. And also it would add yet more calories to your daily intake.

I'm kind of over calories as a concept to care about. I suppose they are useful in managing a symptom (fat) but - it's all so inaccurate. Unless one is sitting and actually measuring with one of those calorie burning things the identical thing going in their mouth it doesn't add up.

Isn't "food" a delivery device for "nutrients?" and won't the body either slow or speed up to manage the incoming calories?

I am not convinced an all meat/fat diet is "optimal", and actually all this gut stuff of late supports that - that seasonal veggies etc provide our bodies with lots of stuff. One being a gut full of bacteria ready to eat whatever it gets fed. To me, it stands to reason that if we keep cutting and cutting all these foods we may be doing a disservice in the long run as our guts become myopic and unable to handle new foods and or the food that is actually available.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-09T23:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-43962>

> **Anna K.;44248 wrote:** I doubt that our gut bacteria knows what season it is

heh, more likely we won't have any issues in our lifetime about food availability.

It does make me question - perhaps the bacteria DO know what season? Via cold/heat/light signals. Blind people's bodies respond to sunlight via alternate receptors. The more and more I explore all this Krazy stuff (tm) the less and less I think we know about how things work.

If our bacteria outnumber us 10/1 - which one is the dominate in this symbiotic relationship. With so many different living things inside us, it seem silly to think we can influence, much less control the good/bad. . . . Ok, rambling today with no point!

Perhaps a vegetative/foraging diet with periodic "kills" full of fat and protein. Or cycles. . .how long WOULD a big kill feed the tribe? Also, I'm reading "The Art of Fermentation", and I also question what we know about prior access to fast food. Perhaps they kept stores from a kill or kept their veggies from summer. Also, why not engineer our health? Perhaps homo optimal IS not identical to a hunter gatherer tribe. . .who says they couldn't have been teh awesome if they were not limited by resources (assuming they were limited, which easily may not have been the case).

ok, again, apologies to all reading. . .I'm rambling today.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-10T03:57:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44038>

ok intrepid readers. . .just got some great info directly from Jack regarding my recent curiosities about what is best for me.

So, if you are a bypass person. The bypass, by it's nature, changes EVERYTHING about how your body works. It just DOES NOT work the same as anyone else, so all the Leptin RX rules may or MAY NOT be the best way for you to go. You MUST do N-1.

He says this goes no matter what kind, and used the example that 2 people can have the same bypass and they WILL be very different.

So, I'm taking from this - if it's not working (such as the BAB and trying to keep things at 2-3 meals) very well may not be my ideal.

You have no idea how happy this makes me - I believe Jack's advice, is good, but I felt something was off. So now I feel good about doing my experiments with myself.

### janagram — 2012-08-10T04:22:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44045>

I am happy to hear this, that you got that issue resolved...now keep us posted on your n-1 and thank you for sharing!

### NWgirl — 2012-08-10T05:19:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44056>

Thanks for sharing Cavemam! I'm now wondering if I need to eat dinner in two portions - the 4pm portion and the 6pm portion. I did that tonight and my hunger was managed so much better. Of course I've been on the plan a lot less time than you.

I'm also wondering if I need to eat less fat due to the type of surgery I had. This is going to bum me out if it's true.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-11T20:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44362>

> **NWgirl;44352 wrote:** Thanks for sharing Cavemam! I'm now wondering if I need to eat dinner in two portions - the 4pm portion and the 6pm portion. I did that tonight and my hunger was managed so much better. Of course I've been on the plan a lot less time than you.
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering if I need to eat less fat due to the type of surgery I had. This is going to bum me out if it's true.

From what I understand on your surgery, fat is one of your biggest problems - so perhaps incorporate small quantities of fat through the day to try and absorb more of it? Start with say, a teaspoon of CO in green tea. If that goes well try and bump up quantities and sip through the day. My bet is if you are eating a larger meal, much of the fat just goes through - but test yourself and see if small more frequent quantities might lead to better absorption. Also, fat is the one that just "goes through" so you should know immediately if you are not getting fat enough. Also, presumably the more gut help you give yourself the more you should grow the bacteria that can help with the fat. . .so you want to keep feeding them with different good fats and all that gut bacteria options.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-11T20:17:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44364>

Jack didn't say anything like that - in fact, I don't seem to have any problems with fats. . I think I'm probably above 70% of calories as fat. I have been doing really well with my hunger lately, with few cravings since I started sipping fat in my drinks through the day. I did have like 4 tbs at once the other day, and it went through fast! So I know that is too much all alone. . .but I suspect the smaller quantities more frequently might be working for me.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-11T20:21:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44366>

> **caroline;44387 wrote:** Cavemam - I am so interested in all your theories re - more fat or less protein. I know I don't feel good on all this protein. I feel so heavy and kind of sick to my stomach - it doesn't feel right for me but I don 't know what direction to go in. Agreed - and there isn't any evidence that says we need to max out as much protein in a day as we can. The leptinRX is just that, a fix for something broken utilizing protein to reverse damage. I'm sure there is an ideal zone based on what you do and what nutrition your body needs.

The foods should be seen as nutrition delivery devices.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-11T21:13:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44371>

I'm enjoying reading about your inquiries and revelations about yourself and what your n=1 experiment will be. It makes a lot of sense to me that doing the traditional leptin Rx is not going to work for you. You've tried it, been rigorous, and moving to something else is a reasonable next step. I have a huge streak of impatience and know how hard it is to change just one thing and give it long enough to notice an effect. However, I think it may be possible to apply a binary search approach, where you change multiple factors, wait a while to evaluate results and then based on results, decide there was either was a change or there wasn't. If no change, then stay the course or proceed with more experimentation. If you you did get a change, then scale back and cut out half the variables you changed in the first iteration. Wait a while and see how it goes and then repeat as necessary. You'll eventually isolate whatever changed the problem (assuming it wasn't a combination of two or more that have an effect). I've done that with supplements & hormones; I am taking so many and changing so much, that it would take a year to do one at a time and wait 2 weeks for result.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-12T03:42:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44439>

THIS IS FOR YESTERDAY 8/10/12

209.8! Under 210. Love it. DOWN 1.8lbs from day before. Salmon day did the trick.

JOC = jeanie optimal coffee - hwc, mct oil, spices, sometimes cocoa powder, sometimes a square or two of 99% dark choc.

Breakfast: 2X JOC, 2 oz sausage, 2 oz, salmon, 2 bites of egg, 1 strawberry, 3 oz liverwurst.

Lunch: Creamy soup with asparagus, zuchini, turkey and bone broth. 1 big bowl.

Snack: Caffe Breve and disc of 75% dark chocolate

Dinner: Yuck ate out. 1/4 lbd beef patty, small caeser with their dressing + parm, 1/2 slice cheese, 1 slice tomato.

We ate later, as it was movie night! Son went to drop in day care and hubby and I saw the new Bourne movie. It was about 7 when we ate. . and after movie I was totally not hungry. The menfolk were starving, so we went to a grill type place. . .and once I was in smelling the food I got hungry and decided to eat. I really wish I had not! I was sniffly and slightly upset tummy - and it wasn't even tasty! Oh well. . .next time I could opt to go on a walk or sit in the car and read while they eat. Or just decide I'm not really hungry, and know that the smells will trick me and abstain.

For Breakfast the family played Hell's Kitchen "blindfolded taste test". This made the consumption of Bfast take about 2 hrs.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-12T03:50:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44441>

Weight same as yesterday! 209.8 - nice.

TOM day 1

TOM was a complete suprise! No pre-stuff, no idea it was coming. In fact, seems like my weight went down the days before. Interesting. I don't know how many days since last. I think I wrote it down somewhere, may go look. somewhere around 26-28 days I Think.

Did LHT this a.m. in fasted state - not even JOC.

Bfast after workout, JOC

Brunch: 2 oz sausage, salad with 3 oz salmon and balsamic vin. 10 macadamia nuts.

Lunch: JOC, bottle of kevita (this was drunk over about 2 hrs in my perrier.), square of 99% choc

Dinner: Salad balsamic vin, grilled pork chop, 1oz grilled zucchini, and a BOATLOAD of carmelized onions/garlic/mushrooms. 1/4 cup of pinot noir. 1 sq 99% dark choc

I don't want to jinx things, but my hunger/cravings have totally been tamed - and I'm assuming it's my liberal use of fatty beverages through the day. I also read that the HCG has this effect on people for a while after quitting a round, so perhaps that is an influence?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-12T06:01:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44477>

> **Anna K.;44778 wrote:** Cavemam, congratulations on your progress!
>
>
>
> What do you think about this article?
>
> [http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/weight-loss/low-carb-and-calories-part-2/](http://http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/weight-loss/low-carb-and-calories-part-2/)

I think since that was written (2008) it's become clear that insulin is only one leg of a many legged table. That aside, what it made me think about was the "nuts/cheese" theme running through the blog and comments. Something in nuts that inhibit fat loss? Also, cheese can mean many things - chunk o cheddar or sprinkle of goat cheese. . .two different things.

I also think the "won't gain on low carb" terribly over simplified - and not true for many.

I think it's still a balance of managing symptoms and resolving broken things. Being hungry enough to eat more calories than your body can deal with is the broken, gaining fat from it is the symptom.

I still dismiss calories as one side of a fat gain equation. (think A + B = C). That does not mean I don't dismiss "over eating", but what is OVER - we don't have a way of measuring for any given person on any given day. Yet. Would be nice if we did.

What do you think? Do you count calories? Have you ever been overfat?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-12T17:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-44530>

209.4, down .4 from yesterday. 1 lbd over LIW.

This is good, as tomorrow is travel day and I was thinking good day for a steak day. Drink my fat while traveling, check in, then head to fancy steak house for BAS! (big ass steak).

I'm totally wanting meat over fish right now. I blame low iron stores. I really hope one of my new docs will consider iron infusion.

Breakfast: JOC w/protein powder (had about 2 servings left in bag thought I should just use it up and be done). Ate about about 1/4 cup of sweetpotato with lots of CO oil. My thought was it's summer and hot and I'm headed out to spend some time kayaking in the sun. That seems about right. Also had some bacon and liverwurst. And now I'm going to take a JOC with me to sip on the way to the lake.

Lunch: JOC, 2 sq chocolate, 8 macadamia nuts, 3 tbs yogurt.

Dinner, small steak topped with langastino lobster and 5 scallops, salad with balsamic vin. 3 tsp of "bulletproof" ice cream. I think it was sweetened with maple syrup.

I am not looking forward to facing the airport tomorrow. I used to fly 200+ times a year, and I had a routine that I loved. I would download (or read depending on how far back you go) a new book, check in, head to the Annie's Pretzels then a chocolate store, then McD's (for a big icy diet coke), and sit down and eat and read. It brought me great pleasure. So much that I loved to travel.

Those days are gone, I'm sure my pleasure would NOT be there - so I am not tempted, but I know just the smells will do SOMETHING to my brain. I best fat up before heading inside. Now, what kind of NEW tracks should I lay down?

oOooops, up 2 lbds!

211.4, 2.8 lbs above LIW

Ok, lets see. . .I thought that with all the activity yesterday, and that I was out in the sun, I could probably try a few things like: sweet potato, yogurt, teeny touch of maple syrup (in the form of a bite of bullet proof ice cream - which is fat, fat, fat, fat and a touch of sweetner).

Frankly, the amount of each of those foods was so little I totally didn't think it would hit me. Yet, I was 2 lbds up. I was trying to increase variety of foods so I can increase the variety of bacteria.

Today is vacation woo. Flew to Vegas - decided to fat fast. When I got off the plane, I ended up being quite hungry. I had a really fat filled coffee and that totally held me over till dinner. Since any scale I get on here won't be "the same" as mine - it seems silly to weigh. Instead, I'm just going to spend the week and try to keep my food choices optimal and mind my hunger feelings to see if I can learn anything.

Breakfast: Coffee with tbs butter, tbs, CO, spices and sq of 99% dark choc.

Lunch: Coffee with tbs butter, tbs mct oil, spices and a pour of half n half from the coffee shop at airport.

Afternoon Snack: Coffee with 2 tbs butter and tbs CO.

Dinner: super duper ceviche, probably about 1/4 cup. Hubby and I shared appetizer

White fish (guess 5 oz) w a few slices of tomatoes, 4 olives, some bok choi all in some kind of broth that was

awesome!!! There was rice in the bowl, and I think I ate about 8 grains. I can't believe I'm counting grains of rice. Seems insane, but I feel I'm really accomplishing something by accounting.

So walked my patooty off today, chilling for a few mins and then headed to the pool with the kiddo. It's only 104 degrees!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-20T03:45:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45778>

I'm baaaaack! Let's try and play a little catch up on Journal. I was debating on continuing - as vacation was a great reminder about how hyper-attentive to one topic I can get, and how my quest for optimal sometimes elicits an un-optimal amount of ocd/attention. I then decided I NEEDED to do it as an exercise in discipline and because I've never done the whole "maintain an exact weight" thing before - and I want to play this out till I can get to an equilibrium WITHOUT trying (like I just did on vaca!).

So, during my 5 days away I got more and more relaxed about food and eating and just went with the flow. I was so with the flow in fact, I didn't want to hit this blog. Instead, went to the pool here yesterday and today and harvested basil to "put up" pesto last night.

On Wednesday night I enjoyed a glass of wine and a bite or two of dessert and tastes of a few lesser than optimal things. The week also included "mostly" sticking to an epi-paleo template, but with lower quality foods. Net result is I weighed the morning after getting back home:

Saturday 8/18: Weight 210! Woooopie. Within the 2 lbds of LIW I'm trying to maintain.

Sunday 8/19: Weight 210.4, 1.8 lbds over LIW - I see a steak day for Monday.

Both Saturday and Sunday, my focus has been on eating high quality/high probiotic nutrient dense foods. Over vacation I had some eggs here and there, along with a few nuts I think - and my weight didn't balloon. Of course, I was walking 3+ hrs a day and spending daybreak by the pool relaxing and playing. I only had one minor client thing to deal with - it was the most relaxing I've done since I started in real estate. I was up and out in the sun 1st thing, in bed by 9p and walking constantly. I also had raspberries, strawberries, yogurt (the good stuff) and lots of kevita these last couple of days. My mood and sense of well being is a rockin.

I've kinda looked at them like filling up the nutrient tanks kind of days - and that I will fat-fast/steak day on Monday.

Speaking of fasts, been listening to several podcasts and then reading about fasting in WOMEN and it's effects. Guess what. . . not so clear cut! Those darn hormones etc. I'm thinking since my "steak days" are not fasting really - I'm eating fat. . .so mostly it's just a semi-low calorie day thrown into the mix. Nothin wrong with that!

I've read a few posts here and there - got really annoyed with one and decided to take a blog break while away and read some fiction. . . I wanted to come to defense of another member to Jack and Misty - I thought they missed the mark on understanding what someone said. VK, you can put me in the column of "got it as you intended" on that webcast follow up thing. Totally understood what you meant. I frankly, don't see how Jack nor Misty could have taken it the way they did.

I don't know VK from adam really - but I could tell how much that probably hurt her feelings, and found myself getting stressed about it. sheesh. I think it comes from the fact that of course we are free to "take it or leave it" as far as this site and Jacks great service to mankind - but if we leave it, there really is no other option. There simply isn't another route to take. So it's his way or the highway to hell. It's akin to the anxiety I feel when on a cruise ship (hate them!) - sure it's lovely and a great deal, but just BECAUSE I can't leave - I stress and get anxious the whole time I'm there. I was probably putting myself in VK's place and finding it not a place I wanted to be.

Please insert all the kudos and thanks and appreciation for what Jack DOES - this isn't a sideswipe in anyway, just an observation that I didn't asses the situation in the same manner. I'm sure both Jack and Misty are sitting there just dying to know my opinion on things and were awaiting my ruling on this matter. hah.

I'm also pretty damn sure Jack isn't as big a fan of blind sycophantism (is that a word?) as the run of the mill surgeon with a god-complex, and will appreciate a little hutzpa and fight back now and then. . .

Now I'm back, I look forward to catching up! MJ - saw you were back on. GLAD! I'm hitting your guest blog next!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-20T05:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45803>

Poker Update: Hubby got in the $ on a big one, so we made more than we spend (which is our goal for the annual Vegas getaway). I made it to the final table in 3 tourneys - and on the last one. . .I did so well I made it to the bubble WITH my hubby. But, it was time for babysitting to end, so one of us had to sacrifice. I got into a hand with my husband, and he was a betting the farm. I figured he had the nuts so I went all in (knowing I had the losing hand!) in the hopes he would win my chips. Oooops, I forget he's not that tight of a players. So we both donated our chips to the chip leader on the bubble. . .so that one hand we threw was worth a LOT of money as we both had enough chips to make it in the money.

Oh well! We have both considered at some point in our lives becoming pro poker players. I know I could make a lot of money at it, but the though of sitting there grinding it out hours on end doesn't sound like fun.

It's the exact same reason I quit being a musician. Practicing scales and whatnot 8 hrs a day and performing 6 songs every 4 weeks was not satisfying musical nirvana.

Now, the reason I quit racing motorcycles. . . .

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-08-21T00:49:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45933>

yep, steak day only if 2lbs or over LIW.

there are also Salmon days, egg days (eat up to 12 eggs through the day any style), Fat days (8 oz of mac nuts or 1000 calories of 90% fat, that it), shake days (do up to 100 g of protein in shakes over a full day, up to 1/2 c of strawberries per shake, coconut milk or raw cream can also be used, goal is 1100-1400 calories) and greek yogurt CDs (32 oz GY plus 8 oz berries) that have some level of success. it's a YMMV kind of thing, shake, steak and GY (I do goat or sheep's milk) days work for me, none of the others do.

there is also a method to do a steak day, eat clean P3 the next day and then do a day with coffee plus fat for breakfast, tuna plus fat for lunch and then fatty steak for dinner.

personally I think you are doing very well maintaining as most people don't stabilize fully until after the 3rd week.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-21T01:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45937>

> **Anna K.;44912 wrote:** If you forget insulin, doesn't this make sense:"If your dietary intake meets all your bodyâ€™s energy needs, however, your body will simply use these dietary calories and leave the calories in your fat cells alone. And you wonâ€™t lose."?
>
>
>
>
>
> to lose much at all), but the cousin only stopped gaining weight, but she is not losing. Well, the difference is calories in her case. By going low carb the father stopped drinking bear and chips and so all these extra calories are gone, but they all ate the same portion size for dinner. So 5.10 man and 5.2 girl are eating the same amount of food on low carb, she is not active, yet eats the same as a much bigger person, how would she ever lose any fat? Why did low carb work for him but not for her? I know the typical answer is hormones, but it's just as much BS as "calories in calories out". Why do they eat the same portion size? is it because she is hungry later if she gets less? NO! She admitted it when she tried eating less, she wasn't hungry, but she LOVES FOOD,.

I think this is the part that doesn't ring true to you, because you are not very over fat - and probably your brain is simply wired differently in the dopamine/serotonin department. I don't think it's just a "out of balance hormones", but instead a complex nut to crack that involves reward systems and hormones and satiety signals and probably 10 things we don't even know about yet.

I don't know if you and I are saying very different things - I agree "eat less lose weight". . . but what I don't agree with is "eating less" is a behavior issue - in fact, I think approaching it from a "behavior modification" program as opposed to trying to fix what is broken in the brain is a net loss game. If you are fat an malnourished or using foods as a pleasure signal or some combo of both or if it's simply pathways laid down in our brain from past behaviors (such as low calorie and exercise a lot to lose weight) that need to be corrected - it's a tough nut to crack.

Additionally, I can tell you a teenage girl that is fat WANTS to lose weight. I think that alone is more than enough motivation to overcome "habits" and "behavior changes". So if she is still fat and still wanting to overeat - it's because the signals are off. I don't even think Dr. K has the answers for every over fat situation - and perhaps the labs etc can point to some big things that can be addressed.

Do you have kids? I think your perspective is like that of a non-child person knowing exactly how kids should be raised. . . until you've been in that situation (over fat), you just don't have the input required to understand what it feels like to be fat, desperately not want to be fat, yet still over eat. You sound frustrated for her - you clearly want to help. . . . but how many fat teens have been helped? Do you know how many people who are fat in their teenage years manage to "eat less" and become healthier for the rest of their lives? I don't think there are very many. Perhaps the best you can do is understand that it's not a character flaw - and help her find the info I'm sure she needs. What's worse for her health. . .being fat or being mis-nourished? Perhaps more time focusing on food as medicine and the weight loss will come. . . .hah, I am perfect example of how hard that is.

You won't find anyone with more will power to push away, or more perfectionism for a plan than me (Except maybe Souldanzer  . . .

and I could never make it work without drastic life sucking intervention (weight loss surgery).

On the HCG - yes it's low calorie - but the significant difference between "500 cals with HCG an 500 cals without HCG" is that utilizing the HCG in the right situation does not cause the same metabolic changes that simply low cal does. In fact, the few studies done so far show that with strict adherence to the diet prescribed, one uses more energy after a round - and one doesn't leap into excessive hunger after either. Muscle mass is retained. . there is a significant different in fat loss with HCG than without. . .Simply restricting 500 calories up regulates burning lean muscle, where fat burning is up regulated with HCG. Also, hunger is different. VERY different.

I think the feeling of hunger is very different between different people. . .it's like if we lived in different worlds, and your world had blue but not red. . .and us trying to converse about what blue is. Just no framework for communicating it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-21T01:33:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45941>

lol, I don't often read on here b/c some of what's going on here are triggers but I did catch that one  I have no doubts that you have one of the toughest will powers on this planet Cavemam!! \*you\* are not the problem here for the most part imo. I think eating problems/weight issues are sometimes difficult to understand for people who do not have personal experience with them. And with that I'm checking back out of here

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-21T01:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-45943>

> **ColleenM;46317 wrote:** yep, steak day only if 2lbs or over LIW.
>
>
>
> there are also Salmon days, egg days (eat up to 12 eggs through the day any style), Fat days (8 oz of mac nuts or 1000 calories of 90% fat, that it), shake days (do up to 100 g of protein in shakes over a full day, up to 1/2 c of strawberries per shake, coconut milk or raw cream can also be used, goal is 1100-1400 calories) and greek yogurt CDs (32 oz GY plus 8 oz berries) that have some level of success. it's a YMMV kind of thing, shake, steak and GY (I do goat or sheep's milk) days work for me, none of the others do.
>
>
>
> there is also a method to do a steak day, eat clean P3 the next day and then do a day with coffee plus fat for breakfast, tuna plus fat for lunch and then fatty steak for dinner.
>
>
>
> personally I think you are doing very well maintaining as most people don't stabilize fully until after the 3rd week.

Thanks Colleen! That is good to hear, as when I start to "fail" at something (like getting 5 or 6 blds over LIW) It's very hard for me to stay the course and try and get back on that horse and not go to the "dark place" if ya know what I mean! I'm hoping each week that ticks by gets easier and easier.

One thing that is definately different post HCG is the volume of food I want. It's fascinating really - I have had no issues with cravings of bad foods or wanting to binge etc. . .it's all been academic deliberate thought processes. This is good!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-21T16:54:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46035>

211.4, 2.6 over LIW, Steak Day!

We had steak for dinner last night, so probably will do a salmon day. That seems to work nicely for me too. I usually only lose 1 to 1.5 on those. Sure wish I could get 2 lbds BELOW LIW. har har. Does anyone do that? Doing CO + MCT Oil in coffee and tea today . . .my steak day is a fat fast.

Read the latest gut blog, still digesting. Need to read again and again. My muscles feel like doing a LHT workout. I think I may go to the materials store and actually buy heavy things for my backyard and you know, lift them and move them around. How novel!

Purchased starters last night for some cold weather veggies. The plant place said "plant now!", so I'm gonna! We've been eating a LOT of salad as that is what is growing in my garden. I'm adding cauliflower, bok choi, broccoli, cabbage, spinach, kale and romain (son loves ceasar salads!).

Apples are lookin good on the tree. We missed eating the peaches, they've all gone bad now. Oh well. . . more to learn.

Really trying this seasonal eating right out of the ground. I even just shake em off and don't wash very much. Dirt! It's what's for breakfast! Plus, after reading the blog, I think my gut was telling me something (har har). . to eat variety to get a variety of bugs.

I've always had a bad feeling about this method of narrowing down what you eat to only a few things. Perhaps this feeling is a "listen to the body" kind of thing. . . I wouldn't know. My body used to tell me EAT SOME GOD DAMN CHEETOES AND WASH IT DOWN WITH A DIET COKE, and sometimes it's hard to forgive that kind of betrayal. We've moved on, but I don't forget.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-21T17:10:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46037>

Many days when I get up after a good sleep, sit out in the sun w/ my coffee and love life. . . instead of just enjoying the moment - I spend time making a list of all the things I'm going to do that day, all the productive things to get done. I fail at completing them many times. Like today. . . I have work to do, and I want to plant plants, read blog again, cook all meals for family, do laundry, do dishes, go buy and place rocks and steppers, teach my son to tell time, go to the pool. . .and on and on.

So by the end of the day, I feel I've failed - and instead of enjoying my bounty of things to be grateful for. . .and sitting outside with my son enjoying the beautiful day and hanging I futz it up with crazy expectations.

Is this a woman thing?

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-08-21T17:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46045>

> **Cavemam;46420 wrote:** 211.4, 2.6 over LIW, Steak Day!
>
>
>
> We had steak for dinner last night, so probably will do a salmon day. That seems to work nicely for me too. I usually only lose 1 to 1.5 on those. Sure wish I could get 2 lbds BELOW LIW. har har. Does anyone do that? Doing CO + MCT Oil in coffee and tea today . . .my steak day is a fat fast.

GY days can drop you more than 2lbs if you are not sensitive to dairy, my last GY day gave me 3lbs. those and traditional steak days seem to give the biggest losses.

As for those that are under LIW surprisingly yes they exist but in general they have fewer metabolic issues and often this is the first time they have done a strict protocol (previous diets are usually WW or JC type diets) so their bodies appear to be less sensitive to changes in macros and hormones. oh yeah, and Men   most of those bums seem to have few problems staying under LIW if they follow protocol to a T.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-21T19:44:29+0300

Thanks, I might try that just for variety. . .plus you know it at least gets in some probiotics! I have access to raw milk, and I'm going to try and make some myself - I hear the real stuff has a gazillion more of the good buggers. Of course, I need to read my fermenting book and find some starter! I'm falling behind in the make probiotic stuff arena.

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-08-21T20:40:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46097>

fwiw the "traditional" GY day is 32 oz of full fat GY and 8oz of berries throughout the day. there is also a version that does 1-2oz of raw mac nuts with the GY and no berries.

making your own with raw milk is a good idea. do you have a [strainer](http://http://www.amazon.com/Cuisipro-Donvier-Yogurt-Cheese-Maker/dp/B000064841/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1345570944&sr=8-1&keywords=yogurt+strainer)? you can get it really, really thick (think yogurt cream cheese consistency) if you like it that way (I do)

### debbiemikus — 2012-08-21T20:47:15+0300

My body used to tell me EAT SOME GOD DAMN CHEETOES AND WASH IT DOWN WITH A DIET COKE, and sometimes it's hard to forgive that kind of betrayal. We've moved on, but I don't forget.

Cavemam, I love your spunk. I have tried and failed on Hcg so I admire anyone who can do it. My body is just a hot mess with hormones all whacked out, autoimmune issues, crohns and thyroid, so I am trying to just eat healthy and forget the weight for a while.

I fail at completing them many times. Like today. . . ### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-22T17:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46314>

Tickled Pink! Weight 208.6, Same as LIW. I've made it almost a month at the same general weight. Whew! That might be a record. IIRC there have never been any "weight stable" periods. I've always been either losing or gaining. With the Leptin RX at first I lost a little, then started to gain again.

Ok, I'm working on embracing some discomfort and taking advantage AND ENJOYING the good stuff.

My work has so many advantages, and I love it. BUT - one thing that I can't get away from is "being availalable" for clients 24/7. I started in this biz at the bottom of the market, and was in the top 10% by year 2. What I feel got me there is once I take on a client, I obsess FOR THEM (putting my thirst for singular topic to profitible use) and I am available at all time, and when needed. The result is my past clients adore me, and provide me with the right number of new clients.

The downside to this is I completely FREAK THE \*\*\*\* OUT, when I am out of cell phone range. 2 hr plane ride? Mini-Panic attack. Movie during the middle of the day - can't concentrate as I leave 10 times to check my phone. Clearly childhood abandonment issues in play - but my body feels if I don't do "service" perfectly, they will leave me, call another agent who CAN show them a house right now.

Unfortunately the reality is many times this is not unfounded. People want help when they want it, that's that. So, if I miss that one buyer with 2m in cash who must by a fancy condo in Boulder before the weekend - then someone else gets the easy (and quite grand) client.

I hope none are reading, but most agents are simple extroverts who have the ability to take those knocks and move on to the next telling themself some rose-colored story on how it was not meant to be. There are so few INTJ real estate agents, that when I was forced to take a sales class that dealt with personality types - they didn't bother addressing my a few of us NT types.

I always have a hard time getting over any real or perceived rejection. I know where this all comes from, and frankly - I've found a way to use my my insane need to help someone and serve to perfection into a lucrative career. So, doing what I'm going to do today is definately embracing discomfort. Although, I'm sure those of you stuck at a desk grinding it out have little sympathy. . .nor should you really - it's all relative right? (fwiw, I ground it out in customer service, and corporate hack jobs for 20+ yrs so I know you!)

So, my point - I'm taking my son to YMCA of the Rockies today so we can play mini golf, do crafts, hike, play tennis, make matching tye dye t-shirts, sing songs and have general merryment in the Mountains.

The problem? THERE IS NO CELL PHONE OR WIRELESS SERVICE for most of the park. I could be out of range for hours! The "lodge" where you check in has wireless so I can "log on", but I'm going to try to not make my sons memories of mommy twitching her way to the lodge every hour to check email.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-22T17:51:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46317>

And if you really do mess up an opportunity like that, then you will go to bed tonight remembering all the incredible things you and your son did today, how much you love him and he deserves to have a mother who is present, and that no money in the world will ever make up for the relationship you just built with your child. There will be more clients. There's only one son that's yours.

Have fun  And soak up some mountain goodness for me, too!

There will be more clients. There's only one son that's yours.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-24T04:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46642>

Weight and food catch up:

Today 8/23, Weight 211, 2.4 lbds over LIW. . .this calls for a "steak day", but I opted not to as I just had one the day before yesterday. Also last nights dinner was full of things that would cause bad voodoo - so I ate super clean today and will see what happens. Will do one tomorrow if it didn't correct.

B - Fat Coffee, 1 bite sausage, 2 bites egg, 1/4 bottle of kevita

L - Chili with pork and liverwurst with avocado and yogurt on top

S - Fat Coffee with dark chocolate

D - Salmon, asparagus, mixed veggies, pickled ginger, 1 sq dark chocolate, 1 bite of peach, 2 raspberries and 2 tbs yogurt

Trying to up the quantity and variety of veggies and probiotic stuff.

8/23 - 208.6! Exactly like LIW. Man, I was excited - it was the lowest I had been since stopping HCG. It was blown quickly with a 2.4 lbd gain in one day.

B - Fat Coffee, fried egg and liverwurst

L - Chipotle style salad with pico, grilled chicken and guac + serving (1/2 c?) of ground buffalo with taco seasonings with a sprinkle of cheese on top

S - Fat Coffee

D - Gumbo I was in denial about, (It was thick, and part of me knew it had to be flour thickened), Cucumber slices with oil and vinegar, boatload of collards with bacon, about 4 oz of realllllllly dried out chicken breast and an apple.

Spent the day at YMCA of the Rockies, and my son was just ape\*\*\*\* over the idea of getting to eat in a cafeteria!! They have a cafeteria at his school and he never got to eat in it, so it was a thing that he was being denied. It was 6:30 p.m. and we had not eaten dinner yet - so I thought we could make do. They did have baked sweet potato so he pretty much ate that. He did try the horrid dried chicken and proclaimed it inferior and that the chef was not master chef material.

I'm sure my food had all kinds of bad mojo including sugar, flour and msg. So the 2.4lbd gain was not unexpected. I ate a reasonable quantity of high quality foods today - so I can't wait to see what the scale shows tomorrow.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-24T04:54:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46647>

**DOCTOR VISIT STUFF, LABS, DENIAL?**

Ok, had a great Doc visit today with the PCP Souldanzer has been using as well as Lyndra has seen. I am starting to wonder if I am in some kind of denial about Thyroid stuff . . . MartiD's recent diagnosis scares me! I started researching symptoms to get ammo for what I could reasonably claim to help the Doc find it a good idea to test for me - and I realized "maybe I do have these symptoms".

On the other hand, reading this damn forum all the time makes one a borderline hypochondriac I think.

BUT - he is ordering the Thyroid stuff. Yay! I do have to pay for some, but who cares!

He's also going to order the Metrimix GI test - and considering the bypass, this seems like a very good idea and big priority for me.

I wanted to leave with a RX for Metformin but didn't, but we were OUT OF TIME, and he was all "Ok, now you've educated me about X, X, and X - what tests do you want? What did Jack say to order?

Clearly you fine ladies that laid the groundwork for me and it had some very positive effects. Happy Hour Oysters at Sugarbeets on me? 4-5:30, M-F.

He said he had started and tried to read the blog but it was so vast and intense, he didn't get very far. He hopes the book comes out soon so he has something in order that is comprehensive.

He was very open to learning, expressing when he didn't know something, and taking notes to look like he was going to research it.

Perhaps he could work for some of you other CO ladies and Wyoming folks. He even mentioned wanting to talk to Jack and

I brought up the idea of him participating in a consult.

He finally gave my husband an RX he had been wanting - and he was great with my son. So now we have a new family doc.

Next Thursday is my Appt in Glenwood Springs with Cindy Hall for BHRT stuff - I didn't even bring up the whole hormonal thing with Doc B, So hopefully I'll have a plan in place for pulling blood/labs on hormones after next week.

Woke up this a.m., got coffee, sat outside to stare at the sun and did that breathing exercise going on around here a while ago. I have no patients for that kind of stuff. BUT - son did it with me, and he seems like he could be the kind of person that might be of a spiritual nature.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-24T22:20:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-46813>

B: Fat Coffee, 2 eggs, 4 bacons and a couple of oz of liverwurst.

L: 1/2bottle kevita, 10 mac nuts, handful of coconut flakes and fat coffee. Weird. I started the nuts and coconut flakes and suddenly my body really wanted that. I know I should eat a little protein but seems like too much trouble.

D: Planning on going to oyster happy hour since I'll just be wrapping up with a client at that time. I hope they also have pate on the cheap menu - hmmm, wonder if that's a bad idea to combine because of the sat fat thing.

Husband and Son are fending for themselves so my evening plan is to raw seafood it out and go get something to have thawed and ready to cook for bfast. I have not been eating enough seafood I guess, nowhere near starting to "crave" it. It's still a chore. I crave steak and beef tho. Could be my low iron/bvits.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-26T03:24:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47172>

> **Souldanzer;47286 wrote:** still not

Note to self, a whole peach, some chocolate and some berries was too many carbs. I've been super hungry all day. My tummy feels full, but I'm still very hungry. I ate lots of shrimps and scallops and crab meat for dinner that was low carb - but also started eating some plantain chips my husband though might be nice to have around and I had a big handful of those. Then about a quarter of an apple. A very big downhill slide into carb-ville.

I was thinking since I was out working in the yard all day, and that the peaches were local and seasonal - that made sense.

I seem to do ok with a few berries here and there, and onions, tomatoes and carrots. I also seem to be able to stop at just a few bites of sweet potato or rice.

I just don't seem to stay satisfied with the seafood. . .always seem hungry after that meal. Frustrating!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-26T19:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47279>

Ooohkay, so yesterday's fruit, plantains and eating late did as expected.

Weight: 211.8, 3.2 over LIW, you know what that means folks! Steak Day! Hubby bough me a nice bison ribeye a few days ago that needed eatin, so it works out. Seems like enough time has passed since the last one, so I'm sort of looking forward to it. My only "problem" with a steak day is that I want to make sure I'm squeezing in enough actual nutrition.

I am going to FF (fat fast!). Did a HIIT workout this a.m. (still need fast paced music suggestions, come on people!). Auntie is coming and we will go to the pool for a few hrs - it's starting to cool, and pool closes next week so we are squeezingin one last day in the chlorine.

THEN - Son is having a couple of friends over to cook hot dogs over the new firepit. Thankfully applegate provides us with some grassfed bad mojo free ones now, so it's not even a bad thing. I've already spoke to son and said his friends will have buns, but he won't. Yay me. Going to make them some sweet potato fries to go with and dessert will be a cookie my husband made with ground almonds. It's not epi-paleo to be sure, but I'm ok with it for a social gathering.

So, one of the kids coming over is 4 - almost 5 yr old girl who is super obese. I just want to hug and love her, I always imagine little-cavemam. When inviting them I said something like "ok, so we eat a little different - but would love to have you over for XX". I'm hoping the topic of how we eat and why can come up.

This girls mom is very pretty, never had a weight issue - and her older brother doesn't have any issues. I just want to take these fat kids and put them in "camp cavemom" for a year. I can see the pained and uncomfortable looks on the childs face - and I also see that mom doesn't recognize or get her awkward feelings. She was at a bounce party and didn't want to go play because she didn't fit in, but clearly was itching to go bounce and do the activities.

Pretty and popular mom has zero idea (I could be wrong - but doubt it) what this girl is going through. For the most part, this area has normal looking if not somewhat extra healthy looking children. That or at least they don't let the fat ones out. Go out on any weekend and it looks like an REI commercial. So a very-fat kid is unusual to see here.

What can you do though? They have to come to you - you can only just be ready to teach right?

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-26T23:18:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47334>

Could you talk to the mom.... not approach it directly but maybe gently.... share your own story or something like that? I know my mom would have given the world for someone to tell her how to help me eat.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-27T00:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47363>

For a discussion on ethics. . .

Kids coming over to play that are far from optimal. I just grabbed some coke and sprite out of the basement that we had sitting around from a past party.

Knowing these kids drink this stuff all the time - I thought win-win. But do I suck for doing that? What say you?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-08-27T01:08:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47368>

> **Cavemam;47807 wrote:** For a discussion on ethics. . .
>
>
>
> Kids coming over to play that are far from optimal. I just grabbed some coke and sprite out of the basement that we had sitting around from a past party.
>
>
>
> Knowing these kids drink this stuff all the time - I thought win-win. But do I suck for doing that? What say you?

Hi Cavemam. These are super hard decisions. Here is my 2 cents (said without judgement if your call is not the same as mine...). I will not serve rubbish food to anyone, whether they are my children or not. I feel like I extend, even in the tiniest way, compassion and care for everyone by not serving sugar and carbs like this. They may not know, but I do. And I also can't inhabit a position of not wanting my children to eat that stuff if I serve it to others. Yes, they get it all the time. Yes, they want it. But I draw a line. If I know it's poison, I don't serve it. My children recently had a fundraiser at school where they were to sell chocolate bars (the crappy marshmallow and caramel sort, not some high quality dark chocolate). I wouldn't let them do it. I told them I'm not going to have them earn money this way. Instead I said I'd donate the equivalent profit to the school and return the candy. I want them to know I care about all children, not just them.

And while I'm at giving my 2 cents, re: the little girl who is obese. In my experience working with families EVERYONE knows when someone has a weight problem. The not talking about it is just the way to pretend it isn't causing a problem. I'd say cultivate a relationship with this mother and bring it up directly. You can lead with your experience. To be invisible, regardless of why you are, is the most painful human condition. This little girl deserves to be seen fully for who she is... this includes the fact that she is overweight. If you deny that, you deny her. But if you include that, I believe she also gets to show you the rest of her. And if she is a little Cavemam, this is something worth knowing!!

Good luck.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T01:20:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47369>

> **Cavemam;47807 wrote:** For a discussion on ethics. . .
>
>
>
> Kids coming over to play that are far from optimal. I just grabbed some coke and sprite out of the basement that we had sitting around from a past party.
>
>
>
> Knowing these kids drink this stuff all the time - I thought win-win. But do I suck for doing that? What say you?

In the ideal world, you'd have refreshments that are healthy and enjoyed by mainstream people - fresh-squeezed lemonade with stevia perhaps? On the other hand, the soda is nothing new to them and is what they normally consume. I have soda and beer around for when my relatives come over. We don't drink the stuff but they do. I respect their rights as adults to choose what they consume. The issue is more complicated when it's children. You want to role model good choices, but IMO that really applies to your own child, not to someone else's. In this case, I think you did no unusual harm but did no good, either. If the kids were older, say mid-teens, then I'd say press them to consume something healthier as they're more apt to be able to fully understand your point of view.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-27T01:33:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47374>

The not talking about it is just the way to pretend it isn't causing a problem. I'd say cultivate a relationship with this mother and bring it up directly. You can lead with your experience. To be invisible, regardless of why you are, is the most painful human condition. This little girl deserves to be seen fully for who she is... this includes the fact that she is overweight. If you deny that, you deny her. But if you include that, I believe she also gets to show you the rest of her. And if she is a little Cavemam, this is something worth knowing!!

Second that. It was so obvious what was wrong with me. You'd think someone would have told my parents they need to do something. But no. Everybody discreetly looked away from my bones sticking out everywhere.

### Lyndra — 2012-08-27T01:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47377>

I think it would be a great kindness if you could find a way to approach the mom and share your own personal story. You could open up a dialogue about yourself rather than initiating a discussion about her daughter, which might put her on the defensive. One thing I learned a long time ago - never, ever criticize someone's child, even after they have just ranted about the kid.

Did steak day yesterday, and didn't weigh today. As I was debating it, I was getting lazy and didn't want to get all naked before going downstairs to attend to family and blah blah blah. Slippy Slidey down the easy path. I need to hop back up on that horse tomorrow fo shizzle.

Weight: Hopefully somewhere around 210.

B: Salmon and scrambled eggs. Bite of yogurt. Fat Coffee.

L:

D:

I wish I were feeling the seafood love. I just don't! I think I need to make myself eat more of it for a few days to see if that jumpstarts the seafood mojo.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T06:03:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47717>
