It came about after discussing how to get out guts full of the good bacteria. I don't think it came up on a webinar, but posts on here. . .or maybe Jack mentioned it in the Q&A. . don't remember.

But the idea being clean off the veggies too much and you are missing out on the good cooties from the dirt.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T06:11:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47719>

This day was off track. I don't know what up.

1st I didn't weigh - I think that is a "not being true to the plan" kind of thing that sends me off the rails sometimes. At the time I didn't see it as off the rails. I didn't make some kind of old-school "\*\*\*\* it I screwed up, might as well screw up the whole day".

I had a nice amount of Salmon and eggs for Bfast, but a couple of hours later I was STARVING. . .and just started snacking. . and getting super hungry.

I ate lots of yogurt with a little honey, a few nuts, some strawberries and some leftover eggs. THEN had a CAFO beef patty, and started eating french fries. I was so hungry, and then dipped in a little katsup.

Then snacked some more later. Nuts, chocolate (77% and 99%), Kombucha, strawberries and more yogurt.

I made a decent dinner, Shrimp and Halibut with tomatoes and onion and a little zuchini on the side. I was totally stuffed - for about an hour.

Now I'm back after a late meeting, and fighting the hunger again.

I haven't had a day like this in forever. I wonder if I just didn't eat enough yesterday? I will weigh in the a.m., but I wonder if doing a "steak day" is not a good idea at this point. . .or just hop back in and call this a lesson.

Grrrr.

Also, really clumsy and I was all dressed up around new people doing meeting stuff tonight and tripped and had a massive fall where my shirt flew up and all the people were going "omg that looked rough are you ok" Frankly, even if I had lost a limb, I would want people to please get the hell away and pleaase ignore me.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47754>

Ooooh, bummer on the fall. But, you are strong, smart and funny and I'm sure you handled the situation appropriately.

Is it possible that you've got PMS? I get unreasonably hungry sometimes when I'm PMS'ing (actually physically hungry, not just wanting to eat mentally), and I know several women who complain of random bouts of klutziness right before TOTM starts. Just a thought.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T19:36:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47806>

Went back and read through the last months of diary entries to try and find one that said when TOM started. It's only been about 18 days, so it's not a "directly prior to" kind of thing. I should probably track that. But for jimminy sakes, I'm just training myself about the scale. One thing at a time.

On the scale - didn't want to, hesitated, but did it.

209\. WHAAAAAAT? Only .4 above LIW. Suprise! Observations:

1\. Clearly at this point there is not a clear causation from food on one day, to weight the next. HOWEVER - until we get the ability to run all kinds of tests on a daily basis, seems like the best tool for me for managing my health. It's all about trends. Speaking of trends here are some good ones.

Since stopping HCG, my weight fluxuations are from about 209-212 instead of 211-216 when first starting. If food sensitivities were causes, they seem to have gotten their \*\*\*\* together. I guess I don't think for me food sensitivity is a thing. I'm eating HWC, Eggs, Yogurt now - and they don't seem to effect things that clearly.

I do seem to be having hunger issues the last few days, and after reading back, I realized I wasn't sipping fat coffee/tea much through the day the last week. 2 reasons, it needs to be warm and it's been hot. AND I've been trying to squeeze in the probiotics.

So, I am going to try and sip fat beverages today and tomorrow and see if my hunger is back in line. Weight is trending lower in general tho since I've stopped doing that. Not enough time to tell if related tho.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T19:43:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47807>

Ok, so when reading back through my Journal I got very mad at y'all. Nobody commented on what I think was my best post to-date. I liked it so much that I've incorporated the ideas into a chapter in le book. So, is it just so damn offensive you looked away and ignored it? I thought it was hillllllarious. This is probably why I have no friends. I'll subject you to this again in hopes I get told to shut up or that I'm just simply awesome.

Here she is. The working chapter title is "Do olympians throw pebbles up their cootch?" Here is the original post.

I Lump it in with Sports, Yoga, feeling joy at the beauty of a flower . . etc. All of of those things (including the god gene) are completely missing. Like to me, if you said "I've taken up the sport of throwing pebbles up my cootch, and hope to go to the olympics next time" that would make as much sense to me as what any other olympian is doing. I see zero difference in throwing pebbles up your cootch and running around a track or swimming laps.

This has made having friends very difficult because not only don't I care, but when 99.9% of the rest of the world feels the need to tell me about those things - I also feel like I'm being a liar mouth by smiling and nodding. So I often feel I have to tell them I think they are retarded! Otherwise I'm lying to them, and that is no way to treat a friend.

### ealachan — 2012-08-28T20:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47847>

What type of response WOULD one expect from another person basically saying "I don't give a \*\*\*\* what anyone else thinks or feels, and I think that's a pretty awesome aspect of my personality"? I don't say that to be bitchy...I'm just genuinely curious what you thought that post SHOULD have garnered in terms of responses.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-28T20:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47852>

> **ealachan;48298 wrote:** What type of response WOULD one expect from another person basically saying "I don't give a \*\*\*\* what anyone else thinks or feels, and I think that's a pretty awesome aspect of my personality"? I don't say that to be bitchy...I'm just genuinely curious what you thought that post SHOULD have garnered in terms of responses.

hehe, ok, so in my head I wasn't boasting about how I don't care what one thinks or feels - more like "I care about you, and you are wrong, and I want to help you open your mind and be funny doing it. Plus, it could be as simple as I was thinking "this is really funny", but perhaps it's like religion and people can't talk about it?

I wonder if this is one of those aspie-personality things that I should learn from. For instance, when friends tease me about some of my crazier behaviors (or crazy in light of what our modern day life is) I think it's funny. It doesn't make me feel bad, or want to change - I mean, I find it really humorous that we are sitting in ice baths and eating raw fish - not to mention poo transplants. This is good stuff!!!! Why wouldn't joking about that be funny?

Seriously, DO you see a difference in some random physical act (throwing pebbles) and the random physical acts we call sports? I objectively do not, and seems like the more people are educated and pushed to think in an open way and get away from dogma etc the more advanced one becomes. Like humor on taboo topics I think does that - it pushes people to think outside their comfort zone.

I guess ultimately I thought my turn of phrase was clever and funny. Clearly it was not, in light of the comments elicited from it - I just wonder why nobody else thinks so. Is it because I'm stepping on the religion of sports? or because it was just a lame joke.

I'm still cogitating on Jack's facebook video and it's ultimate message of narcissism. Could I be pathological?

### janagram — 2012-08-28T21:02:26+0300

Plus, it could be as simple as I was thinking "this is really funny", but perhaps it's like religion and people can't talk about it?
>
>
>
> I wonder if this is one of those aspie-personality things that I should learn from. For instance, when friends tease me about some of my crazier behaviors (or crazy in light of what our modern day life is) I think it's funny. Why wouldn't joking about that be funny?
>
>
>
> Seriously, DO you see a difference in some random physical act (throwing pebbles) and the random physical acts we call sports? I objectively do not, and seems like the more people are educated and pushed to think in an open way and get away from dogma etc the more advanced one becomes. Like humor on taboo topics I think does that - it pushes people to think outside their comfort zone.

>
>

>
>
>

### Lyndra — 2012-08-28T21:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47870>

Shut up AND you're awesome ??

I differentiate between friends and _friends. The social friends, you just sit there with them, nod and smile, and get along. You don't confide your secrets and how often you see them doesn't really matter. Now the real friends_, those are the ones that you are truthful with. Maybe not bluntly, no-holds-barred truthful, but you fully share your feelings and expect them to respect your point of view and share their lives with you.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-29T01:19:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47952>

> **Lyndra;48340 wrote:** I have a number of friends but not many _friends_. Now that I'm older (and wiser?) I'm much better at knowing and sharing my feelings, but less inclined to put up with crap. I like having discussions about interesting topics with people I disagree with, but so few people are able to disagree in an agreeable manner, anymore.

Amen to this, I guess I get energized from that one on one conversation when there is a difference of opinion. I'm ok changing my mind when confronted with evidence to the contrary, in fact, I love when I can go "head to head" with someone and their opinion ends up making sense to me.

I think part of other people's uncomfortableness with this type of opinion conflict is they assume a contrary opinion is a value judgement against them. I think sometimes, when looking through the evolutional lense is that agreeing and being part of the group is beneficial for a percentage of the species. Same goes for outliers, those are just as necessary - just different. So perhaps there are those who are built to garner pleasure from group-ness, and those who are averse to it.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-29T01:35:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-47963>

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-29T16:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48077>

> **Cavemam;48414 wrote:** At the risk of being a bad audience myself - didn't you have some ED type issues before that? Like the Axis Jack warns about had a few issues? I didn't think I did, so I did the HCG - very much against Jack's advice BTW. So the "steak day" didn't actually result in a loss of the weight from the day before? Man, that would be such a pisser! My first couple of ones didn't have big drops., but it seems to have evened out and at least for the last 3 weeks has worked. The constant attention to my weight feels a little disordered, but I'm still debating what the best thing in the long run is.
>
>
>
> On the hunger, I guess that's something I just have to accept is "off", and I have to "treat" it. I know, no matter how much seafood I'm cramming down, that doesn't fix this.
>
>
>
> I have to "plan" eating, I'm not fixed enough to just go with what my body wants. Does anybody? Is this something MOST people have to do? How many people out there can eat naturally?

I never thought of myself as ED before this forum. So not sure what to think about that. But I def had disordered eating. Your basic IR related stuff - eating certain foods triggered too much insulin which lead to a feeding frenzy. Then I usually had some method of compensating - like double exercise or double whatever I was taking at the time for constipation. Now I think of it differently. Dr K says inflammation feeds bingeing bx. I did hcg twice but in retrospect, for me the reason I wasn't losing was all related to my poor over stressed adrenals and the hour long P90x or cardio I was doing 6 days a week. There is nothing worse than exercising an hour a day and gaining a pound a week. I was SO happy with my body at the end of my second round. But felt like \*\*\*\* and had no period. I think stress from my life not being what I wanted it to be (and dating a guy who triggered all my daddy issues followed by breaking up with said guy and then accepting the fact that I was 40 childless and alone) tanked all my hormones. So yes my HPA axis was def on the fritz. I have Hashi and had tanked my adrenals in the past.

I'm pretty pissed that I have to have a spare tire to get my period. But trying to deal.

After my second round of hcg I was loving my body and weight but felt like \*\*\*\*. Mostly really bad mood. Like I wanted to kill ppl for breathing too close to me. I had trouble doing my job which is working with mentally ill adults that can be pretty annoying at times (I love them truly). I started Leptin Rx and what I found was if I slipped and had too many carbs, I would immediately gain aNd could not get the weight off by any means. I was also having terrible bouts of low blood sugar which made steak days and the like pure torture. I tried egg days (as AI not a good idea), chicken days (oddly those worked ok) but basically if I gained I was f-Ed. Then I had a major emotional trigger and the frenzy did not stop until I gained close to 15. Then TOM returned. Believe me, I think about hcg a lot but I know for me it's a trap. I hated the daily weighing. The impact on my social life. And I obviously lost my "normal" fat and it wasn't sustainable. I only wish I could move said normal fat to my butt which would greatly benefit from some cushioning. Or grow about 2 inches.

So that's my hcg. I think it's not good for ppl like me. It's treating a symptom not the disease because my fat is caused by cortisol (probably) Leptin Rx is the cure (I hope). But I know your situation is a bit different and I don't fault you for doing the hcg. It works great for some. Something needs to push your tipping point.

As for the hunger. I know dr k isn't big on the metabolic type diet but I did find some things that help me. I know I need a heavy protein for saity. I always eat beef for breakfast. Seafood lunch and dinner. High purine is what I need. Always wondered why no amount of nuts filled me up - they R low in purine. Looks like mussels, scallops, clams, squis, shrimp,anchovies cod herring haddock mackerel and sardines are high in purine. Maybe experiment with those and see if they fill you up?

Was thinking about Soul and looking at ghrelin. Protein decreases ghrelin. Maybe some types need less fat more protein?

Idk I have never solved the fat dilemma for myself.

I've been thinking about tracking my food again but that kind of kicks into the food obsession trap.

Phew aren't ya glad you asked?

Lol

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-30T02:23:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48240>

Sucky day as far as being "on track". Didn't weigh. I didn't have my regular pre weight movement and though that was a good reason not to. I tsk tsk at my denial.

I was hungry constantly again today. I did fill up when I ate Breakfast and Lunch, but an hour later - hungry hungry. From when school was out and I snacked on apples/nut butter that I made my son, I've been taking bites of everything I've cooked. Now I'm full, won't eat with my family - but will probably be hungry again by the time dinner is "over". I need to NOT snack after dinner time. There must be some rules I won't break.

Bad Mood, Low energy. I did walk for an hour today - but have been down and moody.

For cooking shenanigans I mixed up a mess o stuffing for various things. I like to make it in batches so hubby can take to work and we can eat for lunch on a different day. We snagged some super tasty raw goat jack cheese. I mixed it with the stuffing and put in the bigger long cool looking peppers and wrapped them in bacon. See below. Never made it before, hope they cook up nicely.

Found some kind of squash at farmers market and stuffed that. It smells like beer! Like that yeasty wheaty smell. Probably high carbs - will let son and hubby enjoy that one.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-08-30T16:51:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48354>

Well, slap my ass and call me a donkey! My weight was exactly 2 lbds over LIW, and that was pre morning movement. SO no steak day today. I can't imagine how all my eating recently didn't make my weight balloon. Perhaps my body actually needed the food?! Nah. That couldn't be it. . .that would be. . .well, amazing.

I still feel I had a step back with avoiding the scale, but I'll soldier on. I need to have my thoughts in a place where it doesn't matter.

The "smell of cooking" theory for generating hunger makes perfect sense doesn't it! In light of that, I might try to vacate the house or sit outside of smelling range between meals.

Son and I were up with the sun, then sat out in the backyard with me drinking my Fat Coffee, and him a Fat "hot chocolate" and playing a game of chess. I so wish I could just freeze some moments in time for perfect recall later. I don't know if it's my lifelong habit of planning for my demise, sure that I would be dead by now. . . or what. Perhaps all my instincts are firing and I'm going to be in a fatal event soon. Lately I keep trying to create and/or bring attention to symbolic moments to sear into his memory so he can "look back" upon our time together now with fond loving memories.

For the 1st time since I was 14 yrs old, I'm not working my ass off at some job. I've stopped advertising and only working with referral clients (as apposed to the 60 hr workweek requiring all my mental energy) I put my son in Kindergarten again, and only 1/2 day - so we can spend this year living and learning ala mommy. SO, I'm probably passing up some significant income - but get to get up with the sun to play chess with my son. Totally worth it.

I have wanted to Homeschool - but alas mini-me is a social group loving little butterfly, and he really enjoys doing stuff with others - and his learning style is such that he responds positively to group dynamics. (unlike me, a cranky group bastard).

So win win. In theory. Let's see if Mommy can stay sane without something productive (work) to occupy her OCD inclinations. It worked really well this summer, but when we are in the dead of winter. . . .?

Sweet. Chess w son is where it's at.

Don't worry I'm sure we could find you some side projects to keep you busy all winter long!

Date was a bust. How do I say, he isn't looking for a wife, he's looking for a beard. Sigh. Drawing board

It would be nice if one shared some of the same interest right? The problem, is frankly I don't know where actual MEN who care about health are located. You may want to take sabbatical in Boulder, I think this is the only area of this country where they are indiginous.

Any chance you are Jewish? They seem to have a nice balance of MANLY along with a little neurosis that could manifest in an interest in health

I so wish I could just freeze some moments in time for perfect recall later. I don't know if it's my lifelong habit of planning for my demise, sure that I would be dead by now. . . or what. >

> For the 1st time since I was 14 yrs old, I'm not working my ass off at some job. I've stopped advertising and only working with referral clients (as apposed to the 60 hr workweek requiring all my mental energy) I put my son in Kindergarten again, and only 1/2 day - so we can spend this year living and learning ala mommy. SO, I'm probably passing up some significant income - but get to get up with the sun to play chess with my son. Totally worth it.
>
>
>
> I have wanted to Homeschool - but alas mini-me is a social group loving little butterfly, and he really enjoys doing stuff with others - and his learning style is such that he responds positively to group dynamics. (unlike me, a cranky group bastard).
>
>
>
> So win win. In theory. Let's see if Mommy can stay sane without something productive (work) to occupy her OCD inclinations. It worked really well this summer, but when we are in the dead of winter. . . .?

Cavemam, I totally relate to this. And I think Dr K talked directly about this way of thinking on today's Q&A. I don't think you're fatalistic or even sensing anything to think that you don't have forever on this planet and it matters how we spend our time. Most people have to have a 'wake up call' to get this. Since having breast cancer and living through 3 major earthquakes (read: within 6 months I lived through first thinking I was going to die and then thinking my children had), I get it. I have let go of everything that doesn't hold up to my high standards of what is important. I realised I had no bucket list. I had what I wanted (my children and my husband and some good friends) and the only other thing I needed was good health. But that I had to prioritise those things or I would get sick again AND I would miss out on the time and experiences with my loved ones that makes their importance a reality.

So now we are close to broke half of the time and we live in a \*\*\*\*ty little rental house. But I have channeled my need to overwork/overdo into a new way of being... allowing, opening, seeing where things lead... and generally being grateful down to my boots for my life.

It's amazing what I can enjoy now. Things I wouldn't even have noticed before! And it's amazing what has faded away.

Not only do I believe you'll stay sane, I bet you will feel a kind of happiness you've never felt before. That is my wish for you anyway.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-31T05:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48528>

I agree, Lauren and Cavemam. Money just can't make up for things. I'm glad you are doing what you are with your son now. I think it's part of what I missed out on growing up..... being with my parents. Living life WITH them not NEXT to them

I agree that sometimes we need a wake up call though b/c of the nature of my illness I never offered myself up to others (other than AN). But I did have to realize somewhere inside that \*I\* am the most important person in this world. No matter what I have to do to make it happen. I had to give up a lot to make it happen.... I had to give up what I was convinced was keeping me alive.... AN. I'm not going to say it was easy.

He's doing a draw for more thyroid tests. . .headed back to boulder this afternoon for that! You were spot on with the whole "don't do too many things at once". He is not a co-processor at all (typical man . But he's game for so much and listens to my explanations, I'm pretty happy.

He did tell my husband he needed to go back to the chronic cardio exercise tho  . . . now I have to force him (husband) to either read or listen to why it's not a good idea. He usually goes with whatever I say - but when the doc says DO THIS, he's a good little soldier and really wants to do that!

### finnite@dccnet.com — 2012-08-31T23:29:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48641>

> **Cavemam;49069 wrote:** Fun things from my doc visit
>
>
>
> 1\. His scale is lower than mine - I love it!
>
> 2\. Left with dexa and metformin scripts!
>
> 3\. I am a bit of a conundrum - low tsh that indicates HYPERthyroid - uh, how can i be fat with hyperthyroid? Doing more tests.

_a large body of hypothyroid patients have a “low

normal” TSH without hypopituitarism. Why? Because the man-made TSH lab is often slow to reveal the hypothyroid state. Those with

hypopituitarism will often have a TSH at 0.8 and lower for women, and

1.8 and lower for men, with accompanying hypo symptoms.

What labs will detect hypopituitarism? -low TSH (below 1.8

for men, below 0.8 for women)

\-low ACTH (below 30 for am. Is possible to be secondary with ACTH as

high as low 40’s)

\-ACTH stimulation or ITT that doubles cortisol from a low base value.

\-ITT for GH stim

\-low GHRH

\-low TRH

\-low vasopressin (hypothalamic hormone which is stored in the

pituitary)

\-low renin and low aldosterone

\-very low or below range prolactin-usually this test is inconclusive

for determing if other low pituitary hormones could be present.

\-low oxytocin (rarely tested, is a hypothalamic hormone which is

stored and released from the pituitary)

\-alpha MSH (rarely tested, is a byproduct of ACTH)

6) If I can’t afford all those labs, can you tell just from TSH?

DHEA? If not on any thyroid treatment, I go by the TSH: less than .8

for women, less than 1.8 for men for determining secondary hypothyroid. I use 1.3 and above for women and 2.2 and above for men to determine

primary hypo. In between .8 and 1.3 for women and 1.8 and 2.2 for men

is less certain to whether secondary or not. A serum TRH and TRH STIM

can help if you fall in that grey area. DHEA, if in the ower half of

the range usually, but not always, indicates possible secondary

adrenal insufficiency. Serum ACTH and ACTH Stimulation are the best tests for determining if secondary. If one has already started steroid without proper testing, the next best test for determining secondary AI is the renin test._

[http://forums.realthyroidhelp.com/viewtopic.php?f=12&t=256](http://http://forums.realthyroidhelp.com/viewtopic.php?f=12&t=256)

My tsh has never to my knowledge been above 0.9 usually sits around 0.5-0.7, my FT3 has been below 50% of the range whenever it gets tested (sans meds)

true definition of hyper is low TSH, high FT4 and high to over the top of the range FT3 (all non-medicated test results)

so Soul's right you need to know the FT3 and FT4

although you can be heavy and hyper, not as common but it happens just like there are hypos who are not heavy.

.

.

.

.

.

oh, and I would totally steal that doctor's scale.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-31T23:35:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48643>

> **Cavemam;49118 wrote:** I do seem to, but here is something weird, My Mag and Calcium levels seem great?! Doc K said on my last consult. . ."I do have something good, you can keep drinking coffee!" I've been hanging onto that silver lining like it's a lottery ticket! He did also suggest I get the DEXI so we know what we are working with so I'm trying to prepare for my carte blanch to be rescinded!

Not negating what Dr K said here but maybe he didn't have all the information at that point in time you have now? I would make this decision on my own. Iron, D and all sorts of other stuff low... absorption issues, bypass = no coffee for me. But you must follow your own voice here. Following what someone else suggested in the light of evidence to the contrary... I don't know.

And you need an Exatest to determine at least your mg levels correctly (I got interesting results on ALL the electrolytes). My Exatest was covered 100% by BC/BS and Dr Berger has the kit now and Olivia knows how to draw it. I'd say go for it. Nothing to loose.

### Souldanzer — 2012-08-31T23:39:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48647>

> **Cavemam;49119 wrote:** I am! They couldn't get me in till week after next tho, so I have to wait. I did tell her to scan for body fat too. . .Did they send you home with a CD or pdf or what? I have learned not to leave without results!

They came in my email. I didn't do the body fat scan b/c it's not covered by insurance. Who did you talk to on the phone? The impossible receptionist? Call back and insist. I got in the next day after I insisted and was transferred to the actual tech to schedule me.

Btw, about the calcium and mag levels, throughout my entire AN they were always great on blood tests. But they were really horrible on intracellular testing I am 100% sure. What is in your blood simply does not reflect what is in your cells. I had a blood electrolyte panel drawn parallel to the Exatest and the compared results were... interesting.

I often wonder if I'm just being super nuts and completely hyper-conscious of all this health stuff. It's taken over my life. . .I would say I need a hobby, but I've got a list a mile long of things that need to be done to "support" epi-paleo!

Make more oils/deoderant

Ferment veggies

prep and make ceviche

get more sun

get more tests

replace all my bulbs

make more bone broth

go figure out how to eat more seaweed

LHT, HIIT

fix and eat BAB

Weigh daily

Count hours between meals

go to the farmers market

go to the meat/dairy place

read fermenting book

make sure timing of bedtime is not too close to dinner

Avoid my broker because I don't want to talk about why business has gone down, and that is by design.

and now. . . tell all my friends the reason I don't want to hang with them is because I have a tumor on my parathyroid gland that makes me even more antisocial than normal!

heheh perhaps we've all been infected with some novel bacteria!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-01T16:51:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-48723>

Weight 210, 1.6 above LIW - My last steak day was last Sunday!!

Shout out to Anna, I'm going to do one just because tomorrow even if I need it or not! I think I am experiencing the health benefits from them - I really really don't think I have adrenal hypothyroid issues and I seem to fare better with less frequent meals anyways.

I am totally zen in personality (in person), I'm extremely laid back - I have low BP, HR, and HSCRP, My blood sugars are on the low side for the most part. . .I'm going to focus on my low hanging fruit until I can get all my Hormone tests done.

For me that is:

Vitamin D - going to try and injection

Vitamin B12 - doing injections

Testosterone - stopped regular DHEA

Iron - going to do an infusion

Hell, with my low levels of D and Iron - just fixing those a bit should make me feel like a superstar.

>
>
>
> Make more oils/deoderant
>
> Ferment veggies
>
> prep and make ceviche
>
> get more sun
>
> get more tests
>
> replace all my bulbs
>
> make more bone broth
>
> go figure out how to eat more seaweed
>
> LHT, HIIT
>
> fix and eat BAB
>
> Weigh daily
>
> Count hours between meals
>
> go to the farmers market
>
> go to the meat/dairy place
>
> read fermenting book
>
> make sure timing of bedtime is not too close to dinner
>
> Avoid my broker because I don't want to talk about why business has gone down, and that is by design.
>
> and now. . . tell all my friends the reason I don't want to hang with them is because I have a tumor on my parathyroid gland that makes me even more antisocial than normal!
>
>
>
> heheh perhaps we've all been infected with some novel bacteria!

You are headed into winter. In my experience, you do need a hobby. One that you can do by candlelight! (Because your social life will further crash and it is tricky (though I've tried) to make bone broth via candlelight )

I'm going now to look up glutathione and check it out!

It's not that expensive. 40-50 bucks depending on size of infusion. There is another place in Boulder where you can get it but they require you to be a patient - which costs almost 400bucks for the intro appt.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-03T03:30:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49089>

So this could be coincidence, or if not - could be an "adjustment period" that will wear off.

But, I started the metforming yesterday and I'm noticing my appetite is totally different. It's not non-existant, but I am "full" almost immediately and don't have any cravings at all. I didn't eat much today before going to the pool - and even after swimming and playing, the smell of all the snack bar food had ZERO effect - usually that causes a little sadness about missing that particular pleasure, but NOTHING.

I hope this is a constant effect and my body doesn't adjust. It's kinda awesome.

I went a whole week within 2 lbds of LIW. I decided to NOT to a fast today, as I wanted to finish off the ceviche I made yesterday. Didn't want that to go to waste!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-04T17:22:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49343>

Got the aviva accucheck bg monitor. Readings are all over the place! for the $100 it cost, I'm totally dissapointed.

Doing a fat fast day today, mainly because I haven't done one in over a week.

Day 3 of Metformin. Yesterday hunger was a little stronger than day before. . .I hope this means the appetite suppressing doesn't "adjust". As best I could tell with the new machine, my 30 mins after eating BG was 85. It was also 85 this a.m. after some fat coffee. It then jumped to 97 2 mins later and back to 82, 2 mins after that. POS.

I didn't weigh this a.m., as hubby was in the restroom getting ready for work. But doing the fast day so it almost doesn't matter.

I'm going to see BHRT Doc (Cindy Hall) on Thursday - that should be in the window 3/4 for hormones so hopefully she can go ahead and do blood draw! Good timing!

TOM DAY 1: a few crampyish feelings yesterday afternoon/evening. No change in weight the week prior - in fact, my weight was hovering around 210 for a week. I suppose I need to keep up with this.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T01:49:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49486>

Metformin influence - or evidence of the supernatural?

I sat down to eat my steak day steak. about 10 oz ribeye and an apple. Could only get about 2/3 through the steak and halfway through the apple. I feel super stuffed. Like not just sated, but like physically my tummy is full. I have done steak days before and eating 10-14 oz steaks and whole apple - and could have eaten more. . .so this is different.

I'm taking 500 mg 3X a day at meal times.

Anybody else here that has good blood sugar taking the metformin? My Blood Glucose numbers have been fine - I'm taking for anti-aging (and hopefully weight loss, but I doubt that was why Dr. K said take it).

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T03:41:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49516>

you are taking the metformin as a hunger suppressant? I need that..... desperately!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T03:47:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49519>

> **Souldanzer;50045 wrote:** you are taking the metformin as a hunger suppressant? I need that..... desperately!

After googling, I realize it has hunger supression/weight loss potential.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T03:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49520>

Don't really need the weight loss but I need to fix my out of control hunger! Dr B gave it to you for anti-aging purposes?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T03:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49522>

> **Souldanzer;50049 wrote:** Don't really need the weight loss but I need to fix my out of control hunger! Dr B gave it to you for anti-aging purposes?

He gave it to me because I told him Jack said so, and threw out lots of things like Mtor pathway and other things that glazed his eyes over when I was already over my time. It took the 2nd time I asked. But at that meeting he said something like "we homeopathic docs really love this stuff" (paraphrasing). He did want me to explain why I thought I needed it even though my labs so no signs of BG or Insulin resistance issues.

Isn't your weight going down? Seems to me like right now you should eat as much of that awful seafood you lean towards - so a big hunger for it is good no?

I wonder if it will work for you - as you don't get the physical signals from your tummy that you are full? I'm not sure how this works. . .I need to know more. When I told Doc B, I thought I got it - but I realize now my understanding was just a scratch.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T04:07:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49526>

I don't know about my weight.... I appear to gain from sat fat, probably coconut oil in particular which would actually make sense for me. Mismatch with the cold and keto but I think I still need to eat it b/c of my ED and my brain injury. When I go fish and MUFAS or animal fats only, appear to lose.

I have an overeating issue no matter my weight and I've been gaining slowly over the past 10months. I don't really want for this to continue. No idea about the recent weight lose. other than that I reduced my coconut milk addiction.

On the other hand, I actually DO have high blood sugars. I guess for me it really should be Cycloset but I'm not into adding another rx to my cabinet really right now. I just tested b/c my new BG strips arrived and after no food for basically 26hrs, my BG was at 91. I guess fever and illness can drive it up. So I'm still hoping to get a better reading in a few days

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T16:55:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49587>

209.2, .6 lbds over LIW

Ha, I was hoping the magic weight loss fairies would have visited and taken 5 lbds since my appetite has been so wonky on the metformin. I really hope this isn't one of those things where they brain figures out you are tricking it, then re-wires (like with gastric bypass)

Something someone said yesterday on here got me to thinking. I think I HAVE had genuine changes of appetite and behaviors with all this. They said "I don't get emotional over food anymore". Amen sista.

I remember back when I was on my last "diet", It was the good ol 1800 calories a day plus taking bootcamp and training for a 10k run (at 230ish lbds). I went to lunch one day with someone, and they served my meal with something extra on it that was clearly fat laden - thus more calories than I was mentally allowing for that meal. When the server left, I slammed my fist on the table and started crying. I think I "gave up" shortly after that (now I know my will power stores were fatigued beyond reason - I had been doing that for 6 months and not losing much weight).

I quickly put on 70 or so lbds and made it back to 300 before the breaks went on again - and I went paleo, just over a year ago.

This is decidedly different. On top of that, this Metformin thing is a real eye opener. I wonder if this is how normal people "feel" and approach food.

I'm packing an overnight bag and headed 4 hrs away to meet with BHRT doc.. appt tomorrow a.m.! Instead of spending an hour carefully deciding and planning my food - I was. . . meh, I'll figure it out. I can surely find an oyster bar or steak house if I'm hungry. I grabbed the supps and a couple of 99% bars and called it done.

If I quit being completely engrossed in food - what then? I need a mental passion - this is all interesting, but part of me also thinks it's still registering pleasure on those food pathways as that is what the focus has been. Ironic since circadian behaviors may be a bigger factor.

Could NOT get to sleep last night. It was the 1st day of cycle - so not that unusual.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T17:15:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49594>

> I'm packing an overnight bag and headed 4 hrs away to meet with BHRT doc.. appt tomorrow a.m.! Instead of spending an hour carefully deciding and planning my food - I was. . . meh, I'll figure it out. I can surely find an oyster bar or steak house if I'm hungry. I grabbed the supps and a couple of 99% bars and called it done.
>
>
>
> If I quit being completely engrossed in food - what then? I need a mental passion - this is all interesting, but part of me also thinks it's still registering pleasure on those food pathways as that is what the focus has been. Ironic since circadian behaviors may be a bigger factor.

Raises hand. Me too. Giving up AN was like giving up myself. I was possessed and filled with it, it was my life. I had nothing left after letting go of the food obsession. I'm still letting go. I'm looking at it as emptying my cup.... making space for new and improved. But it's not easy.

One of the most wonderful experiences this last ski season was that I didn't have to worry about the food so much. BAB at home in the early morning, grab my cooler filled with some burgers (or even just a few cans of sardines/tuna if there was not time to cook the burgers), throw in a bag of coconut flakes or a jar of manna, bottle of water and I'm good to go for the day. No bringing snacks with me while on the slopes, no trying to snack in the ski lift b/c my BG just crashed no taking breaks for food..... I LOVED IT. Got back to the car in the PM, tucked into my burgers and source of fat... maybe a few bites of celery sticks or another veggie I had thrown in.... call it done. I stopped eating dinner with my ski partner in restaurants on the way home. I'd still hang out with him while he ate his but I just had a cup of tea most days. It's freeing. It really is.

I used to have break downs in restaurants. Just deciding what to order was way over my capabilities. If things then didn't arrive as expected.... trouble.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T17:23:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49599>

> **Souldanzer;50129 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I used to have break downs in restaurants. Just deciding what to order was way over my capabilities. If things then didn't arrive as expected.... trouble.

Yes, I've done this more than once over eating. It seems so far, but scarily so close sometimes. It's kinda funny in retrospect. I behave just like my 6 yr old having tantrums when something wasn't exactly as I had expected. DON'T THEY KNOW THAT GOD DAMNED SALAD DRESSING HAS SUGAR IN IT? CAN'T THEY TASTE IT? THE MENU SAID OLIVE OIL, THIS IS CLEARLY CANOLA OIL - STAB STAB STAB. hehe.

Managing the minutia of eat this not that according to all this JK stuff, and then whoa - even though you started eating all the meats and CO oils etc, that wasn't QUITE the whole story - now eat fish and only seasonal veggies.

Does anybody else feel a little "duped" by that? I feel like I was a little child who couldn't handle the truth and was lied to a little bit. I'm not saying it's reasonable, or logical, but why send me on a path eating one way - when optimal was another way.

My mind keeps going back to that now with each of these things we add in. Perhaps I'll just skip the MHS and wait for when we have to harvest placentas from babies born in the same months as we were because that's next springs optimal.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-05T17:29:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49604>

I'm feisty today. I took 2 days off work, and I'm taking a road trip that will hopefully involve a soak in a hot mineral spring. And I might finally get my hormones done. Don't know why I'm so anxious about that! Probably I've been in denial about somthing and I don't want to face.

Anyways, I'm in the spring break, world is my oyster, getting away for some Cavemam time kind of good mood. I'm a true introvert, traveling alone is such a great pleasure for me.

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-05T17:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49605>

I'm having "some" feelings about that issue, too. First eat meat then eat fish. Then skip the sat fat with fish. My negative feelings are primarily b/c I feel like I lost quite a bit of time. I think about where I could be by now had I started off like this right at the beginning or at least known to not eat sat fat with my fish after my TBI.

I can also see that there is a plan somewhere underneath the way Dr K is unrolling what he has been learning about health. When I am being told to wait now I think but maybe I could already make this all happen faster \*now\*.... I don't know. If I think about 10 months ago and if I had just been plain told to eat raw fish heads, not sure I would have done it. Possibly, b/c I'm kinda weird that way. And every once in a while I see someone new on these forums (also Dr B) and they are totally overwhelmed with the complexity of the information. You kind of got to understand why you are eating the most disgusting things there are before you can do it. So I get part of Dr K's plan.... but for some of us time is a bit limited.

I think there's some things we maybe don't know. I know he had us eat leptin rx to bring down inflammation before hopping into the ice bath b/c that would have caused some serious detox, iirc.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-06T01:59:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49636>

Raw bar found. Dinner followed by a mineral bath to psych up for another doc visit.

### kathylu — 2012-09-06T17:00:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-49740>

I'm going to be easy on Dr. K. He's been building up our knowledge base slowly, it's true. However, many people feel he's going too fast for them to keep up, so try to keep that in mind. Also, remember that getting people to simply follow a paleo template and to stop crossfitting themselves to death will result in vast improvements for most people. Introducing CT slowly, tweaking your hormones, eating seasonally and controlling light exposure will bring more improvements. Those of us that are more "broken" (including me) will benefit from some of the more "cutting edge" suggestions. And although he didn't focus on it specifically, I believe he has always said seafood is a better choice than meat.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-07T18:06:20+0300

Trying to navigate tests this a.m. to get hormones figured out. AAAAaaaannnd, I made an impulse appointment to get my hair cut into a bob. The hair loss is starting to look old lady bald - so I'm hoping less hair pulling down might fluff.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-08T15:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-50235>

Nice do! Ok, sorry but here is hair 101: If you want more fluff, use a difuser when drying and put your head upside down. I know you probably aren't into using product as it isn't optimal, but Aveda has something called phomollient that works fantastic. Then slightly rat the top if even necessary and you will have a lot of lift and curl with your hair. I can see curls coming out of no where. You look like you have beautiful hair. I only wash 2x a week so I only use product 2x a week. Probably not great but I am into my hair and will not go to work with unkept hair. My hair is semi-curly (which btw, happened as I got older) so if I don't do something with it, it looks very unruly so I "always" do something with it unless of course I am playing around the house or boating. You could even slightly rat the top and pull the front up with a barette or clip and put some heighth into and be ready to go. I bet you would love that and it would pull it away from your face and let your bright personality shine thru even more. I have issues with my hair falling in my face though so I almost always pull it up.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-09T19:48:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-50520>

It's a contemplative day. Work has been slow - both good and bad, but it has given me lots of time to focus and get my thinking in order.

Weight 210....been very stable at that. It's within 2 lbds of LIW. Now I have the luxury of planning a fast day when it works for my life.

I left a post over on the kids thread about my son meditating. Jack made an interesting notice - "Primal Sense". I think this last month has given me the time to get in touch with mine, too. I feel very at peace with things, and not torn trying to decide exactly what to do. (all of this of course could just be my vit d levels going up after switching that around . I also suspect it could be a transitional seasonal inclination. I try to look at all human behavior now through the long term evolutionary lense - and how it might help me in moving forward.

The flip of that "mother nature centered mommy goddess" feeling, is the equally present feeling that all behaviors are simply chemically driven by concrete biological things and there is no bigger point to life.

That all actions and reactions are simply expressions of some predictable biological action in our brain. I have had several "what IS the point of life" moments lately as I struggle to find a pleasure. Which makes me wonder is finding pleasure the driving force of human life? Ultimately are we simply pleasure seekers - and taking away pleasures takes away part of our humanness?

I look at someone like Inger, who frankly ooooozes contentment and happyness without all the cultural BS that I feel strapped down with. Yet, I seem unable to enjoy those simple peaceful moments. I was in a steamy spa like thing last week - and I was completely unable to sit there quietly and enjoy any of it. My mind would not quiet and I was bored.

For a while, Real Estate was a pleasure (and it still is), but I've mastered that and my passion is different. . .now it's about the brief and structured relationships I have with clients.

So then I started paleo. . .and still Food and prep and "the lifestyle" were my passion and kept my mind happy.

Then I went down this path of reduction and restrictions and it got less and less pleasurable. In fact, It's like there is an ascetic undercurrent that is implying a superiority through self-denial. I can't quite pin it down, but my PRIMAL SENSE tells me THIS is not the ideal path for me. Instead of approaching that MHS with relish at the crazy and fun experience that might just do me a LOT of good - it's coming from a place of "oh crap, I really need to do this thing I don't want to". I will still do it, as I do believe there is much good to come from it.

But, I realized the last week I am better simply just relaxing into a life that works for me. I do what works for my life and I feel I'm approaching things with a better balance for having a happy life. Guess what, that has involved a few glasses of wine, a few pieces of chocolate that were less than 99% - because I really enjoyed the experience that went with it. It involved a bite of dessert at a bday party, it involved finding pleasure in preparing a great meal for friends and family instead of TRYING to look at food and the social things as an impediment to my goals. It would be great if I were getting pleasure from something else that made me content and a peaceful person - but I've not found that and so I think not being perfect may have to be what is perfect for me.

I see that some people here have come and gone, and left in a weeping despondent state that is ultimately not an improvement in their life. I also don't see them improving in the bigger sense for having been down this road.

I think it's very very easy to get lost in the minutia and not take the bigger picture view of your own life. What good is a 30K foot view of evolution if you can't see yourself with that type of bigger picture lense? KNOW THYSELF.

Know Your Self.

Life can be very very very short. . . do you want to get to the end next week due to a car crash, and regret how you spent this week?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-09-09T20:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-50530>

Yes Cavemam yes.

Things have been different over in my corner of the world as well. I am a black and white planner girl. Feel really good when I know what's gonna happen when and with whom. Julie MacCoy here. But recently none of my "plans" (aka what Jen thinks should happen) have worked to the point where I've finally given in and stop planning entirely. Well. That's not completely true. I still have things on the book to do but I've stopped trying to MAKE things happen (especially in the dating arena). Had this weekend off and besides going to hospital yesterday, I did nothing. Nothing showed up, so nothing was done. Definitely weird. But something has shifted. I look for the go, go, go drive and it's just not there. So more reading and laying around. Trying to find some motivation to go to the hardware store but I'm currently on the floor in my bedroom tapatalking to your journal. Might just lay here a minute longer...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-11T18:31:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-50966>

209, .4 above.

Pleasure. I am sitting in the sun outside my favorite little cafe in Boulder. Latte sipping and learning things (bg series reread).

I've got to do RE stuff at my Boulder office - including Mingle w the rest of the office for a big photo shoot. Hopefully sun sitting and pleasure will keep me smiling and nodding. My inner voice is so far removed from the rest of the world's reality - I often feel like I'm in a play.

Does it bother anyone else that interactions are so false? It would be nice to connect with people in reality instead of fake pleasantries.

I wonder what the reaction wokuld be if I actually answer the "what have you been up to" question with the answer "thinkin of blending raw fish heads into a smoothy"

### ealachan — 2012-09-11T18:41:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-50970>

> Does it bother anyone else that interactions are so false? It would be nice to connect with people in reality instead of fake pleasantries.

I believe that we're conditioned to do this from a very early age, and that a lot of the time we don't realize we have a choice when it comes to this. Duplicity irritates the \*\*\*\* out of me, so I have decided not to participate in it - I am about as straight-up and honest of a person as you'll ever meet. It doesn't always serve me well socially, but the way I see it, if someone is more comfortable being around me while I'm lying to them about what I think / feel, then they'd be better off just finding someone else to be around.

Plus, how will you ever find others who are interested in drinking raw fishhead smoothies if you don't tell them that you do it?!

Just be you. Everyone else is someone else's problem.

It's not PCOS, I'm sure of that. Lady doc said the ovaries were awesome.

Maybe you are feeling pissy and antisocial not as a result of high T but as a result of reading the result. In other words, if it's wrong (which Jack seemed to imply directly) then it's wrong. But thinking it could be right is making you nuts, angry and antisocial. (Can't say I blame you, I'm sure I'd have the same reaction.) Would it be possible to put it aside and wait until you get the re-test? After all, it is free T which is what is available to you, so there is something complex happening here even if it is right. Hang in there.

Btw, how were your thyroid tests? I can't remember, but the hair can be very related to poor thyroid function.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-16T23:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-51972>

> **Cavemam;52585 wrote:** Thanks for checking in Kiwi - this was the 2nd draw with a high number. We drew again as neither me or the doc believed the first number, and then it came back the 2nd time higher. If you feel like taking a look and making a few comments - here are the labs:
>
>
>
> [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0AmcYkugLB3O4dDZHdndkTVFCMTRoM2VIMTY5cEhUMFE](http://https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0AmcYkugLB3O4dDZHdndkTVFCMTRoM2VIMTY5cEhUMFE)
>
>
>
> Female, 44, 210 lbds, Gastric Bypass 10+ yrs ago - so the complications of Iron and B-12 deficiencies are an unavoidable consequense of that. Working on both.

A few thoughts, not well formulated.

1) You may get a more helpful reading of E and P if you draw on day 20/21.

2) However, with the ranges you've got here, you are low on both E and P. I wonder if you are somehow converting E into T. However, your available T (free T) is not out of the range at all. Your DHEA is also low-mid range (eg not optimal) so it doesn't appear that that is a problem. I'm thinking that your problem, if there is one, would be lower down the hormonal chain. Which makes me wonder what your SHBG and pregnenolone levels are.

3) You look hypo thyroid to me based on your very very low free T3 levels. And I don't think you can rule out Hashi's with those antibody levels until you get 3 AN negative tests in a row over 18 months.

4) Your cholesterol is too low and your LDL (which makes hormones) is also very low.

5) So I'm thinking you're living in the inflammatory pathways for hormone making not the reduced pathways alluded to in BG 12.

Others will have better insights.

Obvious question (again, if this is correct) is what would be causing a rise in Total T?

Yea, they must be floating around bound up to something. . .I'm assuming SHBG, It's not something to dismiss, and the reason I checked and got my doc to run the tests in the first place was because my Hair was falling out. Now I think acne/lowered voice are creeping in.

So, I'm somehow producing hormones - so I'm not unable to produce them. The two places for Testosterone are ovaries and adrenal. . . Ovaries were just given the once over and nothing showed up on them (as far as tumors and cysts etc) - so next step if still a mystery seems like MRI to look for tumor on adrenal.

THANKS FOR COMMENTING!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-16T23:28:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-51976>

210, 1.4 above LIW.

Made mistake of not eating right early in the day yesterday - and it all went to \*\*\*\*. Went to mountains to check out camping spots with T. Just drank my pumpkin coffee and a few bites of T's bfast. . .thought I would try "not eating" and see if I could drop another lbd. Experiment fail.

Longstorshort:

5 guys, burger and fries (w bun and horrid katsup!)

lemon bars

smores

Trying to fight the urge to let it snowball. It's 2:30, so far so good. Doing fats today, and going to have oysters and raw salmon for early dinner. Perhaps the magic of seafood will get me back on board.

Haven't read BG-12. I'm denying myself. I don't deserve it after my fail yesterday.

Started CT again this week since I had hormones drawn. Noticed a decided uptick in hunger - I think the metformin hunger supressing goodness has passed.

Stillpissy

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-52160>

209.4, .8 above LIW.

I thought I would drop more yesterday with only eating Oysters. Have lost more on steak day - I am going to see how the next day shakes out before I judge, as I also did a cold bath and some pretty intense HIIT sprints. Used my 5-fingers, so much better sprinting in those.

My new found testosterone driven (real or placebo, who cares) confrontational nature worked really well for me. I went head to head with a lender that screwed over my sellers on a recent deal. . .just got them an additional $900.00 . I feel like I slayed the beast and I want to rub his blood on my face.

Usually I can get into these confrontations (on behalf of others, never for myself!) and win - but I usually feel so horrid after. . the whole hating it if other people don't like you thing. Feeling bad that someone else was put in a bad position so I win etc. This time I knew the mortgage guy had to eat the $, and that was probably a significant personal hit to his bottom line. . .mortgage broker is a TOUGH business to be in. Today - didn't have one bite of empathy for him. Is this what going through life as a man is like?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-18T02:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-52252>

Went to the hematologist today. I really liked him personally. .. .but here is how it went. . .

They drew blood to check to see where I am on iron. I ended up being at the very bottom of the range on everything.

I explained the bypass and allllll the things I'm doing to even get it up to bottom range. . .including eating liver and lots of iron foods and taking iron several times a day etc.

Sounds like he was kind of strapped by the system - but basically said he can't do the infusion if I'm in range. He suggested I drop the supps and stop eating the iron rich foods and we can re-draw in a couple of months right after a period. He even suggested how fun it will be for me not to have to eat liver anymore! (can't fault him for this really, I probably thought that way too a year ago).

I love that our system is such that I have to make myself worse to get treatment.

On a funny but sad note. . . .his office is part of the Rocky Mountain Cancer Center - and they go that extra step to be hospitable. By hospitable, I mean they have tons of CW packaged foods and sodas in special waiting/snack room! The nurse was feeling very happy to be able to offer me a great selection of snacks! They did have my old nemisis "cheetos, diet coke and a mini-reeses cup chaser", but they did not call to me. Progress!

I refrained from saying "way to keep those clients coming back!".

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-21T17:09:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-53174>

209, .4 lbds above LIW. That is the number I pick, scale all over the place. Going new scale shopping today. To get a cheap one at target or go for pricy from amazon. . .anybody got advice?

The last 3 days I've been VERY low energy. No motivation for anything. I went ahead an made myself get out and walk and do things, but it wasn't my usual happy/peppy self.

I have decided to take a couple days off from the crazy amount of pills I'm popping. . .today I already have a little more energy.

I can't decide what I absolutely want to take. . I should quit popping the D, it's not working. I hate quitting the iron, and I can't reconcile the "make yourself worse so I can justify an iron infusion". Still cogitating that one.

I have NOT felt better at all since taking the Betaine HCL. . .in fact, worse overall. . .so I think I'll skip that a few days. Hopefully Metramix GI will be back soon and I can see if it's needed.

The other things. . .Metformin. Doc prescribed 2X a day (500 mg). Ive been taking it 3X, once with every meal as the internets suggested for anti-aging. Perhaps drop back to 2X a day?

Progesterone, I've been taking 200 a day of that (doc prescribed 100) so I may drop that back. . .what do you guys think?

Pregnenalone, doc prescribed 50 - and I've been taking that.

I didn't have labs back when I got the script - the prog/preg were going to be needed. ONce I saw how low I was I figured UP THAT DOSE BEEEATCH!

Some is good, more is better right?

Story of my life. . .

### Souldanzer — 2012-09-21T18:10:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-53204>

P can make you sleepy. I take 200mg/day but when I tried 400mg I think I got a bit loopy. Make sure you take it at night.

I think I would try putting on the D topically maybe. When you take the drops do you hold them under your tongue for a bit?

Iron. You know my spiel on that

In general, I've found out for myself that it isn't necessarily the thing I'm intending to supplement that is making me feel bad. It's the things I get in the other 90% of the pill.

But I'm totally with ya'. I keep wanting to eat less supplements. I do with food what I can..... eating turmeric instead of taking Curcumin, drinking valerian tea instead of taking a supp and so on. I still take a ton  and whenever I try to stop one it backfires. I want to feel better so I want to keep filling in what is needed but if the bucket's got a hole on the bottom.... it's really frustrating.

My Spectracell nutrient panel showed very clearly the things I've been supplementing.... except for the b vitamins. Those were bad despite.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-23T21:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-53675>

Thanks on the nice backyard comments! I'm so proud of myself for it. We MOVED here (before I'd heard of Jack BTW) to live more congruent with the natural world. My spidey sense told me this was the path.

This was a long range plan. I saved for YEARS squirrling away cash in a little jar (ala dave ramsey), worked on the design and learned about materials. This was the year. . got bids, found the right contractor, made lots of compromises because damnit I was NOT going overbudget.

Plus, since I work in Real Estate, I have some good connections - a good friend and neighbor is a landscape architect so she wouldn't charge me enough to draw up beautiful plans. I hit the materials store for remnants all the time and got stuff on sale. My contractor is also a neighbor who didn't charge me enough. It took most of the summer as it was worked in between other jobs and I thought I could do some of the work (if you recall my back/leg issue from too much heavy lifting!)

I did this on a very tight budget. Interestingly enough, the part I THOUGHT would be the hard part (excavation, doing levels) was the cheap part!!!

I'm lucky in that I see 20+ houses a week, it gives you a feel real quick for what you want, and value of things.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-23T21:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-53677>

> **ColleenM;54268 wrote:** so if I fly you up will you do mine?? I LOVE your back yard, serious envy going on right now and as I am rather design challenged it ain\`t happening here.
>
>
>
> you mentioned a new post apocolyptic book can you share the title, I love those!

New York Times best selling author William R. Forstchen now brings us a story which can be all too terrifyingly real...a story in which one man struggles to save his family and his small North Carolina town after America loses a war, in one second, a war that will send America back to the Dark Ages...A war based upon a weapon, an Electro Magnetic Pulse (EMP). A weapon that may already be in the hands of our enemies.

Months before publication, One Second After has already been cited on the floor of Congress as a book all Americans should read, a book already being discussed in the corridors of the Pentagon as a truly realistic look at a weapon and its awesome power to destroy the entire United States, literally within one second. It is a weapon that the Wall Street Journal warns could shatter America. In the tradition of On the Beach, Fail Safe and Testament, this book, set in a typical American town, is a dire warning of what might be our future...and our end.

I ended up staying up till past Midnight because of this damn thing! Post Apocolyptics are my most favorite, I just have an insane feeling that I was made for post apocolyps! It's my purpose in life! hehehe.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-09-24T17:57:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-53799>

No iron for me. Still trying to come to grips with the "stop what you are doing so you get worse" thing.

Sleep - wierd! Last few nights I've slept like crazy. The first night, I was up till Mid reading, so sleeping till 9 a.m., not so bad. Today, went to bed by 9:30 last night (yes after getting up at 9a!) and slept till 9 again!

Scale has been good - there is that. 206.

One concept I'd really like to discuss is the idea that over-fat causes major inflammation - yet doing all kinds of tiny thing and adjustments for inflammation when losing a few lbds might be a bigger helper. aka, should I do another round of HCG in Jan?

For the historical purposes, been weighing everyday. I feel this is a very positive step for me. Of course, it's easy when it's below my target.

The last couple of days I dialed back all the pills and moved my doses to:

Meforming 2X a day

Progesterone 100mg, at night.

Pregnenalone 50 mg mid day.

100 mg 7-keto dhea if I feel like it (every other day?)

Magneseum plus 5-htp before bed if I feel like it. Like last night legs a little cramp so I will take mag today.

1 brazil nut

I am still debating the Betaine HCL. sooooo many pills. Perhaps after my GI test.

1/2 way through webinar (saving it for CT motivation or during cleaning) - feeling smug about:

Pre sleep NM, been doing it.

Curcumin - been having lots of tumeric

Garlic and Onions -been having lots of it

Resveratol - have red wine 2x a week mebby, and eat lots of chocolate.

Rockin the bristol

My new garden has lots of herbs and I'm using them, and my veggies are often grown here with dirt intact.

Appetite is totally under control! I've been minding my autophagy and have been IFing from 6p till 10 a, I know, I know, not BAB. Get a rope!

I heard a great podcast with he who shall remain unsaid (Paul J) where they talked about this and how having a little MCT during the fast actually does something to the reward pathways, un-doing them from foods - making you cravings and appetites lesser. I'm trying it!

Lastly, I feel the need to say - I'm getting more and more convinced that all this cutting out of foods and limiting ones diet is counter-productive right along with taking lots of supps.

Start eating more and a variety of good foods people.

You may go now.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

So the story goes, he touched a table at school where some sick kid left a devil germ. He then rubbed his face or picked his nose and ate his booger\*, The devil germ got in his body and killed all the good bacteria. The dead soldiers all flooded out in his first bout of poops. Then there were no good guys protecting the lining of his gut, or the intestinal brush border - thus when he ate food again, it hurt from flailing devil bacteria.

So first we drink bone broth because it has protectors called glutathion that love on the lining and protect it. Then we will eat some fermented veggies and the super greens I made (full of tumeric and garlic) and they hopefully can grab hold of the glutathion protectors and live happy and reproduce and finish killing the devil bacteria.

ta-da. I wonder if Jack wants a guest blog on this.

\*\*\*my booger eating theory is that it's actually good for kids because they are either building anti bodies or getting their bodies used to all kinds of stuff.\*\*\*

### KiwiLauren — 2012-09-27T09:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-54706>

> **Cavemam;55480 wrote:** Lauren - are the glasses you got actually sunglasses? Perhaps I'm messing things up by asking that they NOT be sunglasses, but just blueblockers. hmmm.

Sorry, I just saw this question. Apparently (according to my friendly optometrist) sunglasses block some blue light, but only about half what blue blocking lenses do. Depending on the lens, some can also function as sunglasses. But what I got was lenses that only block blue light. He was able to tell me the percentage of blocking of blue light as well as how much overall light they block (the latter being the sunglasses sort). Mine ended up being a pretty brightish orange colour. They were the only medical grade ones and the only ones pilots use. So, no, they aren't sunglasses.

I think I found a humble optometrist. I thanked him for being so knowledgable and he said he didn't really have a clue when I first contacted them but he 'made a few phone calls'. He said there are people who specialise in specialty lenses and he just rang them up, then ordered a few different lenses for me to come and see.

I pick them up tomorrow. If I can figure out how, I'll take a photo for you. I just borrowed Gretchen's MO and told them my doctor recommended them because of migraines. Is that an optimal lie?

Yup! So, since I already know I need to fix hormones, and I've made all the livestyle (living epi paleo, doing ct, minding my circadian) I haven't seen what ELSE there could be to do, so why bother with that now.

It makes more sense to me in many ways to triage these things, and it's a cascade - and certain things need to be fixed in order. If my hormones were great, but there was something else weird going on - then I get the ASI.

BUT - I'm kinda tired of jumping on the "test this" bandwagon and have no actionable things from it (23andme is an example). Of course, I'm going to squirt vitamin d up my lady parts so what do I know!

> So the story goes, he touched a table at school where some sick kid left a devil germ. Then there were no good guys protecting the lining of his gut, or the intestinal brush border - thus when he ate food again, it hurt from flailing devil bacteria.
>

>
> So first we drink bone broth because it has protectors called glutathion that love on the lining and protect it. It's only 10 am, and it's a great day. I thing the fun chat last night has a lot to do with it. I just don't have any "hanging with the women of the tribe" moments in my life, and so when I can connect with people in a group setting It's rare and welcome.

I also took my b12 shot after going a few weeks without. Had a big bfast today because i realized my energy has been low - and I think its because I have not been eating enough.

Got up and was glad it was a crisp and sunny am, so went on a nice walk around the lake.

Now I'm CTing - from the chest down - cant bring myself to get my back in today.

Have that life is great mojo going today, so after ct I'm headed out into the world with my little guy.
