### indigogirl — 2012-10-02T17:20:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-55822>

Your gumbo sounds yummy!

I wasted $ on three red snapper heads that I made into a soup and then couldn't bring myself to eat \*sigh\*...

Have a question for you about HCG. I know they say people with eating disorders shouldn't do it. I've lost significant weight on it only to gain it all back (2X). I think most people who are obese have some type of eating disorder, no? Contemplating doing it again...but the definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over that hasn't worked before. Its a quickish fix and I would be eating Epi Paleo and more healthfully than I am eating now. Thoughts?? And thanks!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-02T17:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-55826>

> **indigogirl;57013 wrote:** Your gumbo sounds yummy!
>
> I wasted $ on three red snapper heads that I made into a soup and then couldn't bring myself to eat \*sigh\*...
>
>
>
> Have a question for you about HCG. I know they say people with eating disorders shouldn't do it. I've lost significant weight on it only to gain it all back (2X). I think most people who are obese have some type of eating disorder, no? Contemplating doing it again...but the definition of insanity is doing the same thing over and over that hasn't worked before. Its a quickish fix and I would be eating Epi Paleo and more healthfully than I am eating now. Thoughts?? And thanks!

I wouldn't do it lightly for sure. FOR ME, I felt I had "fixed" many things in my head - including eating disordered behavior. I was able to eat properly (I was doing epi paleo since my first consult with Jack in the spring when he said eat fish heavy). I was not gaining weight. I was living a very epi-paleo lifestyle before it was cool just through intuition and because that worked for me.

Another me me me concept - I think my bypass makes me a bad example to look to. I don't know if that helps in ways that other people don't experience.

The trick is the hard work with HCG is AFTER you stop. You have to take time to get your body in homeostasis at the new weight. No easy feat. Read back through my blog in July/Aug and you will see my extreme frustration at steak day after steak day.

I think for YOU, you kind of said it - you've done it before and were not able to get your body to balance at the new weight. Is there anything you are doing differently now, that is fundamentally different that you think will make this time different?

My perspective in theory is this: HCG if you understand it fully and do it in a way that works WITH That, then it can be a tool to get fat off without slowing metabolism. Recovery is more important that the 30 days or so of weight loss. FAT itself is inflammation - we see LOTS out there that it's really high up the pyramid of "bad things you should fix". I think the "recovery" phase from the weight loss is much longer than people realize, but I also think for some it can be a net positive.

So far I'm stable at a weight right around where I stopped. I noticed my body is really TRYING to stay there. . .if I eat alot, I get more energy, if I eat little - my energy stops and metabolism slows. . . body trying to stay here. Of course it's only been a few months.

My personal plan is to keep epi-paleoing through December and get as healthy as possible and do another round in January.

Have you kept your weight steady without trying and eating an awesome healthy epi-paleo style diet for a while? Do you think you have disordered eating pathways still very firm and active?

Lastly, were you at all bingy or anorexic or super obese as a child? I think a full blown ED is a different animal than an extra 50 lbds in a person who's eating is simply \*\*\*\*ed up from following CW.

An anorexic does not CHOOSE not to eat - if you have ever been there, then NO NO NO on HCG.

### indigogirl — 2012-10-02T17:59:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-55838>

Thank you for your thoughtful reply.

Not anorexic. Not overweight until my mid 30's (I'll be 60 this year). No weight problem until I started emotionally over eating. I tend to be a black and white person...moderation is hard for me in terms of eating.

I kept the weight off for 6 months after my first round. Actually continued to lose for 6 weeks after stopping HCG. Was VERY careful about what I ate. Ate kind of South Beach and tracked everything. Did steak days maybe twice. Just slipped back into Sugar. That is my demon now. I eat Epi-Paleo meals for the most part. There is still a piece of me that wants to eat junk.

Probably no reason, other than some desperation, that it will be any different this time.

Nothing worse than being in a crowd of real estate agents. I should know. I worked for 5 years as the Executive Officer for the local Board of REALTORS(r) and then a year as an agent. WORST job on the planet. I'd rather scrub somebody else's toilets for a living. My hats off to those who can manage to do it.

I'd send a lifeline but I don't think it will reach from here to there. sorry.

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-04T23:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56326>

Deja vu from AN.... I thought that was one of the reasons to start eating again. Doesn't sound like I got any closer to my food is non of your business. I will remain anti-social I think.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T00:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56346>

> **DarleenMB;57578 wrote:** Nothing worse than being in a crowd of real estate agents. I should know. I worked for 5 years as the Executive Officer for the local Board of REALTORS(r) and then a year as an agent. WORST job on the planet. I'd rather scrub somebody else's toilets for a living. My hats off to those who can manage to do it.
>
>
>
> I'd send a lifeline but I don't think it will reach from here to there. sorry.

The lifeline wouldn't help, because there would be 5 well meaning people to disconnect it so they could elicit a conversation about the 5 people I called this week to try and meet my number of sides for the year and on and on. I really like some of these people personally, but there seems to be a giant inability to understand that there are differences in the world and change your behavior appropriate to the situation. They are completely unable to put themselves in other people's shoes. I suppose that's why they are in sales.

For me, I'm probably the only introvert that has a mostly TXT relationship with clients and doing very well doing the opposite of what I'm taught. I know that if all the other agents are doing it, I probably should do the opposite (much like healthcare!) I fill a niche, geeks need to buy houses too!!!

My first couple of yrs in RE were the hardest thing I've done in my life. Nothing like working for 60 hrs a week for no paycheck for a couple of years! Now I get to pick and choose clients and if I don't want to work with someone - no drama, I don't have to! Much nicer than working for a company where you MUST work with the other employees no matter how silly the situation or their work.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-05T01:34:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56356>

FINALLY got Dexa scan stuff back. Bone density is just fine. Yay. The pdf is like a bad photo copy, but looking at the photo on there scare me. I was hoping to see WHERE the fat was (sub q vs visceral) does anybody know how to read these and how to tell this?

My body fat % was higher than I was expecting and quite depressing. 40% I totally thought it would be lower. sad.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-06T16:54:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56592>

I think I may start tracking body fat percentage too. It is WInter and CT is rolling in.

Speaking of CT, I forced myself yesterday to go out in the freeze without much clothes for a walk - today I notice I am craving a cold bath.

The other thing I have noticed is when I do real CT my appetite is very large - I was hungry yesterday in ways I normally am not lately. I have noticed the opposite of appetite suppression with CT. I ask this - women, talk about your experience with hunger and CT?

I am doing a bulletproof fast this am, to see how that works in combo with CT and my appetite. I'm showing houses today so I wont be around food, at should give me a good benchmark of real hunger vs boredom or pleasureseeking.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-06T16:56:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56593>

> **indigogirl;57778 wrote:** Sunrise is gorgeous! That's frost on the ground?? It's 84 here and humid : (

First snow and freeze of the season! I wanted a little more fall - but now its here and i can wear my cute fur vest, i am pleased.

### Endless — 2012-10-06T19:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56607>

I have not noticed any correlation between CT and hunger yet...but something is making my appetite go up and down. Some days I am counting the hours between meals, and will wake up in the wee hours absolutely painfully starving, while other days I am fine and don't seem to need much food. I will try tracking it more closely.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-06T21:40:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56619>

I hope the crazy isn't coming back. Perhaps my CT yesterday is detoxing some old crazy. Bear in mind I'm happily married.

Unreasonable mood of the day: Single guy client who I've had a great relationship with - we've done several deals and we always have the best time. He got engaged, I met the fiance for the first time today - she was perfectly nice. I was completely let down and my feelings were somehow hurt because she was a total good looking skinny swimsuit model looker. And she just isn't that interesting of a person. . I don't see us becoming friends. I am annoyed by this. I have zero right/reason to be, but there you have it. She probably has the crazy too.

I have a theory, that crazy drama women must have a sexual dynamo hormone panel - because there is no other reason I can come up with that men gravitate to them as girlfriends.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-08T17:39:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-56896>

206, -2.8 below LIW. 39.8%

It was exciting to see the low the last few days, but I knew it was a water thing. Back to the 206 range.

I stood outside this a.m. on the freezing concrete to stare at the sun and breath - I do this almost everyday. It's JUST started being super cold. I noticed when I went inside, the spot on my back where BAT is supposed to be was feeling hot. I was sitting there getting excited about the idea of BAT rampin up. Then I moved, and realized there was a sunbeam shining on the spot. POOP!

Mr. Cavemam is getting meniscus surgery this a.m. He's never been under anesthesia so I'm a little nervous. I had a horrid time with it when I had my bypass. . . so I keep thinkin the worse.

I made him take the day off work yesterday and do things with our son in case these are the last memories son has of daddy. Morbid I know. But, all of my family is dead through a variety of freak accidents and really bad choices. It gives one a perspective of not only CAN bad things happen, they DO. Enjoy life today!

This reminds me, one of the essay titles on "the book" is:

Pull the plug Jeanie Kay, just pull the plug!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T04:01:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57238>

Didn't get to weigh this a.m. BUT, I took a supp free day. I am taking my hormones, but didn't take the metformin or betain HCL. My energy was low today and I wasssssssss hungry. I guess that Metformin is working on my appetite. I also didn't feel like doing my walk or ct. Didn't do much of anything.

I'm a little afraid of what weight will be tomorrow. I just have to face it. Ignoring it would be a slippy slide in the wrong direction so I need to steel my resolve and get up and do it, AND go for my walk tomorrow. If I can pound out that walk, then usually everythin else will fall into line.

At least I didn't eat anything awful, just lots of stuff. Mostly good, but there were many handsful of nuts I nibbled on through the day. And I think I ate 3 chocolate bars.

and now it's dark and I don't have my glasses, crap the day is going to \*\*\*\*!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T17:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57335>

204.6, 39.4%, guess my eat fest yesterday was my body tryin to get the energy up. Also, didn't sleep well last night - drugged up hubby was a snore fest, and I had the windows open to cool the room and there was lots of outside noises too. Oh well, moving on.

I'm curios - will my practically DOUBLE calories yesterday translate into lots of energy today? I had very low energy yesterday and got little of the running chores list done. (I actually enjoy putting on the podcasts/books and doing house stuff while listening).

My hubby is hanging at the house this week - which interferes a bit. . .he's a typical man when it comes to being an invalid. . and that's all I'll say about THAT!

I'm going to make myself go do a cold walk this a.m. and then taking son to bike track in the afternoon, so forced activity!

I had FAB, with xtra mct oil, and took a metformin, pregnenalone and 1 betain hcl. Now I'm burning up, sweatin a little from the forhead and feel woozy. Wonder which it was? what is reacting with what?

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-10T19:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57363>

Earplugs. Haven't slept without them in years I think. I live in a noisy environment.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T19:59:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57372>

> **Souldanzer;58670 wrote:** Earplugs. Haven't slept without them in years I think. I live in a noisy environment.

I don't know why this has not happened. It's like I'm going to cut off my nose to spite my face. I already do the eyecovers - and make son use them.

It just \*seems\* so wrong to have to put eye and ear plugs for sleeping. Logically I know it's wrong for our environment to have so much ambient light and unnatural noise. . . so which is worse?

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-10T20:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57376>

I can tell you that when I lived in my mountain cabin I never used ear plugs to sleep. That would have been scary. I \*needed\* my ears to work so I would wake up if something wasn't right.

In town, I rely on my roommate, neighbors, smoke alarms and dogs instead while my ears are plugged. Personally, I get the better result this way. I think this is an "unnatural" adaptation to an "unnatural" environment that results in a better "natural" outcome for \*me\*.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-10T23:47:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57442>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57452>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57461>

love my white noise! it's on all the time....in my room. Oh and speaking of my room.....DH and I have our own rooms.....due to his snorrrring!!!! Before that , I used earplugs, but my ears have problems.....possible exzema, so no earplugs now.

### Endless — 2012-10-11T04:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57511>

If I wake at all, I need more work on my sleep. I learned to sleep in a bowling alley when I was a kid...my parents were in a league. I can tll my sleep is poorer now because noise wakes me...then it didn't.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-11T05:04:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57525>

> **Anna K.;58834 wrote:** I was not born in America
>
> But noise machines just work !

Totally agree. My dh has tinnitis and we have a noise machine going every night. We have it set to a rain storm cuz that is usually when I sleep the best. This helps him and it also helps me not hear the small noises that would wake me up. Personally, I'd rather it be quiet but it does help me. Ours has a few settings and I liked the pebble brook but it has birds chirping and I think it is time to get up when I hear them. It's kinda cool, it even makes more noise when someone makes noise, so if dh snores, it gets a little louder in response to it to blend it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-11T06:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57529>

Well I woulda suggested moving to Wyoming because it's so quiet you can hear a gnat fart from 100 yards away. But frankly I'd as soon put up a fence around the border and just keep everyone else OUT. Sorry.

And just to add insult to injury here, my sleep has become so good (how good you may well ask?) ... that most nights I close my eyes, roll over, and it's morning. God I just LOVE THAT!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-11T18:54:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57616>

207.6, 40.5%

Cold bathing, 62 degrees. My CT now is walking in the weather scantily clad + cold baths at tap temp. I'm going with nature! If I recall, March last year was about 50 degrees out of tap, so perhaps it will get even colder than that. I'm going with what the earth delivers.

At first I was going to spent my CT time working on website design for my RE business, but I am not as excited about it - and my business is exactly balanced right now. So now I'm sort of thinking I'll start a health type blog. The "paleosphere" is full of stuff from men. Where are the women. . .the over 30 women who are trying all this. I'm thinking podcasts, too. I love listening to the newer guys - but all the IFing, workouts, foods, path's etc seem like 90% male. Just a thought.

Reserved domain: thefatladysang.com and cavemam.com Thoughts?

cavemamspeaks.com ?

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-12T16:17:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57743>

207.6, 40.3

No major change, not bad considering I had 2 glasses of wine! I was spread out, but 2 is just toooo much for me I think.

Got the metramix GI test.

Good: Nothing really off that I didn't expect. No celiac, no bad stuff like H.Pylori or Candida.

Fermicutes and Bacteroidetes are in range - my doc pointed out "see, there are no excuses here for being overweight" arg.

I do have some off results in the fat absorption area exactly as expected. I did start the HCL Betain, so I'm thinking of starting some ox bile enzyme stuff.

I do have a curious Low slgA. . .lab says "low can result from stress of malnutrition".

Anaerobes both obligate and faculative, all in range. . .lactobacillus is at the tippy top.

I suppose if I had found out I had candida or something I could treat it would have been good. As it is, It cost a bunch of money and told me that I can't deal with fat very well. I knew this, moving on.

My Doc had the unwelcomed reflection that "you've done all these tests, there is no reason here, so what is the plan for your weight" Then he ask about my exercise and prescribed more cardio. (my exercise is walking + sprinting 1X a week + random heavy lifting when the mood strikes)

More cardio.

Did I mention? More cardio.

### ealachan — 2012-10-12T17:19:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-57758>

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-12T19:14:23+0300

I like this crowd sourcing thing. Perhaps you guys are right. I've been doing the slow/heavy/short program sort of like "body by science". plus I whip out some pushups and squats when at the playground.

BUT - you see this in men, and I'm starting to get that men do not equal women. I wonder what an ideal weight program for me would be.

ooooo, shellfish porn! yummy!

As to a strength training program, have you tried Fred Hahn's "slow burn fitness revolution?"

If my metabolism EVER gets back online I'm going back to that. It's 30 minutes ONCE A WEEK.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-14T18:45:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58125>

40%, 207.8, yikes. -1 below LIW, but high sounding since I was in the 203's a week ago.

Of course this week contained 3 glasses of wine, and restaurant food yesterday + too much light too late last night (don't regret it, enjoyed it, for me a net positive).

I was hungry this a.m. too - not a usual thing. Playdates today + website stuff. Time to re-invent the RE business by working out the kinks on cavemam.com

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-14T18:56:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58127>

Had movie/lunch with a girlfriend yesterday. A month ago we were the same weight. She is now 20 lbds lighter than me and feeling quite good about it of course.

She has been using artificial sweetners, low calorie frankenfoods, mostly carbs - except for her weekend cheats with white wine and junk food. . .yesterday she ate fresh potato chips and a pasta dish with 2 glasses of white wine.

She told me all about her 2hrs of cardio 5X a week - IN HER BASEMENT AT NIGHT WITH THE BIGSCREEN TV FOR ENTERTAINMENT.

I am so not making this up.

Yes, there I was - same weight, doing all these epi-paleo non CW things.

She totally had the "I so win" vibe . . .like I'm pretty sure the reality of where we each are did nothing but cement her resolve that she is doing the right thing. Some starfish I am! My example of how I wasn't tempted by the pretzels at a party the night before kinda paled in comparison to the "we have to shop for new jeans because I lost so much weight!".

To me, this does beg the question - with no "changes" how do we know we are on the right path?

On the upside, I went to lunch and a movie with two friends - Pitch Perfect, SO FUNNY! Of course I was a nerdy music-geek in HS/College so it resonated. I had a glass of wine and two plates of raw tuna app. It had some kind of evil sauce and tons of sesame seeds so it probably was a net negative. Oh well, moving on.

That is social things on Thursday, Friday and Saturday!!! That's a \*\*\*\*-ton for me. I usually have to hide out for a few days after socializing. I wonder if my hormones are balancing. too bad it's not realistic to check these babies every day with a stick like with the beeties stuff.

### Inger — 2012-10-14T19:16:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58128>

> To me, this does beg the question - with no "changes" how do we know we are on the right path?

Oh.. Cavemam! Never compare yourself to anyone else! When I start to do that I feel soo ugly. I compare myself with how I was, and how my sisters are ageing. They have almost the same genetics. And that is when I know I am doing something very right. I check my skin, my hair.. all. But I do not compare it to others. I does not work that way. I go about how I feel too not only looks. Do I feel happy? Light and well? Confident? Peaceful? Much energy? Such stuff tells me if I am doing someting right or not... and there always are these darker days but they are just moments and they pass. And it takes time for changes to happend.. especially if we cheat a lot if we are still broken..

The more strict and crazy the faster changes occur I guess..? Or that is how it works for me..lol But to go slow is fine too, if one is happy with it for sure!

To be thin does not mean one is healthy.. not at all. Here are many very thin people and many have lots of issues too.

Cavemam, you look beautiful in the pictures!

### Inger — 2012-10-14T19:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58130>

> To me, this does beg the question - with no "changes" how do we know we are on the right path?

Oh.. Cavemam! Never compare yourself to anyone else! When I start to do that I feel soo ugly. I compare myself with how I was, and how my sisters are ageing. They have almost the same genetics. And that is when I know I am doing something very right. I check my skin, my hair.. all. But I do not compare it to others. I does not work that way. I go about how I feel too not only looks. Do I feel happy? Light and well? Confident? Peaceful? Much energy? Such stuff tells me if I am doing someting right or not... and there always are these darker days but they are just moments and they pass. And it takes time for changes to happend.. especially if we cheat a lot if we are still broken..

The more strict and crazy the faster changes occur I guess..? Or that is how it works for me..lol But to go slow is fine too, if one is happy with it for sure!

To be thin does not mean one is healthy.. not at all. Here are many very thin people and many have lots of issues too.

Too bad about the huge TV screens.. everyone starts to have them here too. I was babysitting by my brother, they have a 3 month old baby and they have a huge screen on all the time..

I shut off all the lights and the TV and opened the balcony door.. and just sat there with baby enjoying the dark silence. The baby slept with his little head on my heart for hours.. it was so beautiful. I felt so sad for him having to be in front of this big screen and so much fake lights on in the appartement..huh but I promised myself every time I babaysit I will let him feel the peace and have him close close to my body. He loved it, and so did I.

Cavemam, you look beautiful in the pictures!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-14T19:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58134>

> **Inger;59608 wrote:** Oh.. Cavemam! Never compare yourself to anyone else! When I start to do that I feel soo ugly.
>
>
>
>
>
> I shut off all the lights and the TV and opened the balcony door.. and just sat there with baby enjoying the dark silence. The baby slept with his little head on my heart for hours.. it was so beautiful. I felt so sad for him having to be in front of this big screen and so much fake lights on in the appartement..huh but I promised myself every time I babaysit I will let him feel the peace and have him close close to my body. He loved it, and so did I.

Inger - you are so right about the comparisons. It never ever helps. It means there is a winner and a loser. Neither position is a positive either. You can't win in that game - I know this, but my mind is so programmed to do it. I suspect it's a deeply embedded brain-road in people who've been the underdog for most of their lives.

Reality is that people are always being shuffled and categorized. . that's how our brains work right? We simply must categorize and file. Now the real question is how to move away from having yourself and other people be in the process. I have gotten to where I can get out of that space with clients/strangers. . .but I seem to hit a brick wall with old friends and people who knew me when I was much less the current version of me.

I've re-invented in a super major way 2 times in my life, and each time I've Jettisoned everyone. . .and I mean everybody from the prior life. Sure, I have facebook friends and professional contacts etc - but those are not real friends. Those relationship I find to be more of a burden than a plus. I think that's part of the introvert nature.

But this friend is just about the only person who I was close with in the Cavemam 2.0 (I am now Cavemam 3.0) and I always go there.

On the baby note, how awesome for you to do that!! And how awesome for you

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-14T19:56:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58138>

Jessica was last seen on Oct. 5 when she left for school. The fifth-grader never showed up at a nearby park where she was supposed to meet friends for the one-mile walk to her elementary school.

After an extensive search of the area, including knocking on doors and FBI road blocks, Jessica's dismembered body was found on Wednesday in an Arvada, Colo., park, miles from where she was abducted.

That is a blurb from news here - don't know if national is all about this, but our local is as it's within 30 mins of here. Caveboy has a playdate this afternoon, and the mom wanted to make sure I don't let the kids outside because of the psycho on the loose. This is so uncomprehensible.

I just want to go help the mother with ammo and a good solid plan to capture and torture the person that did this. I'm pretty sure if something like this happened to caveboy, I would have shark like focus on destroying the person who did this - and once that job was done I would have to go too. I'm not cut out for jail! (I know, I've been in it!).

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-14T20:35:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58153>

This has been big news here. Very sad. One thing that we do is teach the kids to look for another mom w/kids if they get separated/lost from us. I've also been meaning to pick up the following book [http://gavindebecker.com/resources/book/protecting\_the\_gift/](http://http://gavindebecker.com/resources/book/protecting_the_gift/). It's a great book about teaching kids to trust their own instincts about situations. Kids are very smart & if we raise them to think constructively and listen to their inner voice they'll know what to do in a scary situation. That said when we do big excursions in public they wear road Id bracelets w/all critical info (very impt for Havoc since he's hard to understand)

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-14T21:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58166>

> **Cavemam;59605 wrote:**
>
>
>
> To me, this does beg the question - with no "changes" how do we know we are on the right path?

This is the same question parents ask, especially parents who value relationship with their child over things like power & convenience. Parents who are conscious and connected end up with children breastfeeding for a long time, children in their beds with them, children who show a wide range of emotions (even when they are inconvenient)... in other words, children who don't try to fit in with some cultural need to be good and quiet but children who can express themselves, their needs, their fears, their jubilance. They are whole, but society tends to prefer children (and adults) who fit in more than they encourage wholeness.

You, Mam, are working toward wholeness. The focus solely on weight is about fitting in. Including weight in your health, but also realising it has to do with stress levels, hormones, brain function, longevity, happiness, etc... this is what you are doing.

I go about how I feel too not only looks. Do I feel happy? Light and well? Confident? Peaceful? Much energy? Such stuff tells me if I am doing someting right or not... and there always are these darker days but they are just moments and they pass. And it takes time for changes to happend.. especially if we cheat a lot if we are still broken..
>
> The more strict and crazy the faster changes occur I guess..? Or that is how it works for me..lol But to go slow is fine too, if one is happy with it for sure!
>
>
>
> To be thin does not mean one is healthy.. not at all. Here are many very thin people and many have lots of issues too.
>
> Too bad about the huge TV screens.. everyone starts to have them here too. I was babysitting by my brother, they have a 3 month old baby and they have a huge screen on all the time..
>
> I shut off all the lights and the TV and opened the balcony door.. and just sat there with baby enjoying the dark silence. The baby slept with his little head on my heart for hours.. it was so beautiful. I felt so sad for him having to be in front of this big screen and so much fake lights on in the appartement..huh but I promised myself every time I babaysit I will let him feel the peace and have him close close to my body. He loved it, and so did I.
>
>
>
> Cavemam, you look beautiful in the pictures!

Inger, this brought up such a poignant memory for me. When my sister's baby was about 3 months old I visited her in the USA. The whole family was around (so I wasn't babysitting) but there was a daytime party happening. People were loud and eating/drinking, talking and her baby was sitting in my mother's lap getting sadder and sadder and starting to wriggle and fuss. No one could hear her. No one tried to hear her.

I asked quietly if I could have a turn holding her. Then I took her outside on a beautiful spring day and she promptly fell asleep in my arms. I walked up and down in the grass with her head against my heart. Just like you. People arrived and left. My father went to the supermarket and returned shaking his head saying "Are you still standing there with her?" Then he looked at the baby and said "Well, I guess she does look comfortable." There was just no way for her to be comfortable and relax in all that chaos. And no one thought to think about how it may effect her.

I wish I had been able to be there for her regularly like you are for brother's child. I remember just whispering to her what I wanted her to carry in her own heart.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-15T17:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58312>

ok, 209.8, +1 over LIW. . . and 40.5% fat.

This is bothering me. I guess considering I had some bad oils and a few glasses of wine this last week - my answer is right there, but jeeeebus people.

I can't get the "must win" competition feelings exorcised. My friend, doing CW's finest is now 20 less than me. My prefrontal cortex and limbic system are having a very large smackdown. I'm mad feeling today, and anger is focused on my inability to fix this fat.

I had gotten quite happy at being under 205. . as that felt more like under 200 was do-able. Now I'm 10 lbds away it seems like I'm going in the wrong direction. I really don't want to go to the gym or force exercise, but my mind is screaming "if you are not being obsessive and suffering, you are not going to lose weight".

I know you all will have great advice to help with these wrong thoughts (it's logic vs emotion going on here) . . . .

I've seen great health strides living Epi-paleo, but my major manifestation of inflammation (fat) is still going strong. I am NOT building muscle, my bf% isn't going down. I've added in CT in the last 3 weeks to boot.

I feel an overwhelming urge right at this moment to do SOMETHING about it. Doing the same thing I have been doing (epi-paleo) isn't working, so by logic I must change something. I had the Suzanne Sommers books, and went and re-read them. I ended up skimming a lot on first passes as all those personal stories annoy me - givemethescience.

So, for those that missed the fabulous fireside chat on Saturday, we ended with me asking Jack this question:

"How do you know all of this works on women?" I ask that because people are starting wonder this - we see plenty of men examples of impressive success with an epi-paleo lifestyle. . . and from some pretty broken men to start with. So far, I'm not seeing the same types/speed of results with women. I am a strong epi-paleo supporter, and a fan of Jack's work - but we are in completely uncharted territory here and I am driven to make changes for ME.

Back to the point. . .Suzanne Sommers. Jack's response was "look at Suzanne Sommers". Well after seeing some of the comments from the peanut gallery about her, I did just that.

She isn't epi-paleo! Certainly not epi-paleo with the ferver and crazy-making zealotry that we can manifest.

I am NOT going to make grand statements or come up with my plan for the day. I'm going to try something new. I'm going to take the day minute by minute. . .but I've started in hard on my winter "deskwork", where I'm sitting in front of computer for hrs a day. Right now I just want to have a big cry fest while cardio-ing it out on the elliptical. She's right about one thing, if you are doing that, you are not doing other bad things because it just takes up so much time.

### ealachan — 2012-10-15T17:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58318>

Several points:

1\. Are you lifting weights or doing body-weight exercises? Muscle doesn't generally build itself, as far as I can tell...not on women, anyway.

2\. You said you started CT again recently. It's been established that CT (in the form of Zeltique, anyway) can cause inflammation initially, which can take 6-8 weeks to subside. Is it possible that your recent re-introduction of CT has caused a bit of inflammation that will eventually go down as your body re-acclimates?

3\. Do you meditate at all? I know you don't do "the woo", but meditation doesn't have to be woo. Sometimes just training yourself to sit quietly and shut your mind off for a few minutes a day can really help you to be able to recognize and dismiss negative thoughts the rest of the time. I actually learned mindfulness meditation way before I ever went Paleo - it was taught to me by my therapist as a way to deal with my all-consuming anxiety, and it helped more than I ever imagined it would.

I know it's way easier said than done, but don't focus on CW friend's 20lb loss. It means nothing to you. It's her loss, her body, her life, not yours. You have an entirely different existance than she does (epi-paleo or not), and there are so many millions of factors that could be at work there that you'd drive yourself nuts trying to figure out why she's losing and you're not. Just let it go. Like I said, way easier said than done...I totally get that, trust me.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-15T17:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58319>

I just read my post from this a.m. Then it hit me. This is all my old demon of putting all my self worth in being intelligent, thus right. In this scenario, I look wrong - thus stupid for doing all this "crazy" sheeeeit.

I look wrong in the same way I've always been wrong and jumpin on the next health bandwagon. Wrong for finally finding "THE RIGHT ANSWER, THE REASON FOR IT ALL" and then failing at it, and well. . .actually BEING wrong.

Is this just another version of "fat makes you fat!" "atkins for meltin fat!" "become a runner, they are all skinny!" "you just need to push away from the table" "more healthy whole grains".

My crazy today is coming from the fact that it sure does look like I'm going down the health-fad-zealot path A G A I N. And it's not working, A G A I N.

I know, it's not logical and science is on my side - but I guess my intuition is telling me SOMETHING is not quite right and not quite working. I simply don't have that confidence in the process so I can sit there sure of myself when others are getting ahead of me in the race.

The crazy, it runs deep today.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-15T17:41:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58320>

> **ealachan;59830 wrote:** Several points:
>
>
>
> 1\. Are you lifting weights or doing body-weight exercises? Muscle doesn't generally build itself, as far as I can tell...not on women, anyway.

I have been doing something like "body by science" which is a 20min a week type thing where you lift to fatigure etc. The idea is get the metabolic changes that weights give you - that is where the magic is, in that theory anyways. Clearly the magic aint happening, my bf% isn't going in the right direction.

I wonder if some 10 day crazy bootcampy weights/running thing might be useful to freak my body out. Then I could go into a epi-keto full ice recovery mode.

I wish Barry or Ben Greenfield would come train me!

### ealachan — 2012-10-15T17:50:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58321>

Have you ever tried yoga? Yoga kicks my ass in really good ways. I built muscle doing a yoga / pilates hybrid class that I never was able to build just lifting weights...and that was when I was still eating relatively SAD.

### Shijin13 — 2012-10-15T18:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58326>

FWIW - I haven't seen any major weight movement (except up this summer when I added seasonal carbs) since December of last year. no real change in body comp etc. is it frustrating, hell yes. I don't know if you remember but on the non-optimal chat - I made the comment, that at this point I've given up focusing on the weight, b/c I've finally realized its not going to come off, until i address the underlying issues. Like you I've been battling this for years (while I'm not at the same weight as you - its been a battle none the less)

There's something freeing about saying F.U.C.K. I.T. with regards to weight. Yeah I said it. I'm looking good in the clothes I have now. and Sure I'd love to loose what's sitting on my stomach - but I've finally come to grips, its not going to happen until I get the underlying things fixed, and apply epipaleo, CT, weights, circadian and seasonal cycles. Got into my 1st CT bath this morning in close to 2 months - I only had time to hop in for 20mins, but its still better than yesterday, or the day before.

we're relearning what's important to our bodies, we've got to let go of the S.H.I.T society has placed on us. Its hard. there are days I struggle. but then I look around me and realize, that while I may not look like the BRTV chicks - in the end I'm WAY healthier b/c of what I'm doing. my journey started well over 12yrs ago... only now am I starting to feel better, but I'm no where near optimal - yesterdays' trip to MIGRAINETOWN w/both nausea and vomiting was a massive sign - my Pg:E2 ratio is majorly effed up.

as for weight lifting /success and body comp changes... my best success in strength has come in lifting when/what I can when time permits. I'm BRTVing 2-3x/week, plus the addition of HIITs in the pool on Sundays after the kids swimming lessons... what's you're HIIT looking like. I've found the biggest changes for me come when I regularly do one HIIT/week. I'm doing TABATTA sprints in the pool and its kicking my @ss.

then again... sometimes you've just got to RAGE. let out the inner T (or in my case E and T!) and go push a boulder around your yard... you might find some serious satisfaction w/that!

### Souldanzer — 2012-10-15T19:58:37+0300

I'm still thinking about the first timer dieting effect. Lot's o'men never dieted before in their life. Their metabolism isn't primed for starvation. And they fix the problem (diet) while at it.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-15T20:26:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58362>

I know your situation and mine are much different (e.g. your bypass history). However, I too, have been frustrated by an accumulation of fat around the belly for many years (dating back at least to my loss of uterus and ovaries at age 35) and subsequent 18 years of synthetic estrogen (no P either). Going cold turkey off the E really messed with my health (crazy hot flashes, waking 6 times per night, wrecked adrenals, hypothyroid). Early this year I started on bioidenticals and in the spring started focusing on adrenal & thyroid health. Finally, at long last, the belly fat is coming off and I am starting to feel "normal." I know not all of this applies to you, but I do suspect that until you get all your hormones more normalized, you may continue to struggle with the weight. Finding a good functional medical doc might be the next step? It's certainly better than the madness of chronic cardio (which I used to do in spades and then some without great success). Hormones are key IMO.

Regarding SS, I think she's on track at least with replacing sex hormones. However, she does use human growth hormone which is not available to us ordinary folks (and maybe we wouldn't want it? I dunno). I know nothing about her diet and I don't see the idea of an old lady of 85 still cycling, but maybe that's a minor point.

That is why we have been at it for so frickin' long. Took a long time to figure it out and I still don't think I have it all figured out.

Cavemam, all in good time. Got to find out first what is really broke. Your thoughts might just be coupled with an off mood day. It is ok to have them. Tomorrow is another day! You are witty, beautiful and inspriring to others. Enjoy yourself for all that you are! You said it yourself about your friend's friend being slim and boring. You are not boring, ever!!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T20:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58377>

OK, I do not have a bypass but your reactions to your friend sound eerily similar. So I'm going out on a limb here ... have you had a full thyroid panel done?

I have bitched for YEARS about my weight gain, my fatigue, my crappy nails, my thinning hair ... but always got the same damned answer. "Your TSH and T3 numbers are normal. You don't have a thyroid issue. Let's talk calories.)

ASSHATS

I have tried every diet out there. I have all of Suzanne's books, and just about every other low carb high fat paleo diet book written. THEY NEVER WORKED FOR ME.

The less I ate the more I gained.

When I topped 200 pounds years ago I actually got so frustrated I beat on my belly until I bruised myself. Boy there's a cringe-worthy confession.

I gained when I started the LR. I couldn't eat 50g of protein for breakfast. I STILL can't.

I have tracked my calories for several weeks and find I average these days (thanks to all the fat) between 1400 and 1800 a day.

WHY?

T3 to RT3 ratio is 3.8 when it should be 10 or greater.

That's it. All along.

I still have those bad reactions to my weight and how I look. I hate both. But now I have to remind myself I have finally tracked down the problem and I AM going to fix it.

Hang in there Cavemam. My fervent hope for you is that you don't have to suffer through 30+ years of self loathing to get to the answer like I have.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-15T23:32:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58402>

Mam,

I think you should hold on to your doubts. You had an insight that the need to know is connected to your self esteem, to be right. But I think you are smart enough (you are WAY smart, trust me) to know that there is no one answer, there is no one who has all the answers, and that there will always be doubts. Doubts are smart.

You are preaching to the converted here and we're all in the same boat. We've all cast our lot with this way of thinking and living, but we all also have doubts. We are all dealing with the what-if's. So when you raise your doubt, most of the responses you get from those who care about you (which is everyone on here commenting) is trying to help you solve your doubts, to figure out your solution.

I think under everyone's doubts are two main things: the fear that we will never get better/are too broken and the worry that this isn't the right path. We all want to be 'fixed.' But there are no guarantees. I do not believe Dr Kruse has all the answers. I believe he has a lot of them. But no one person knows everything and even the smartest theory cannot keep up with all the intricacies of our individual realities.

What if your friend is not wrecking herself? What if she is going to lose weight, keep it off and live a happy life? Does that invalidate this way of thinking? If it does, then we've all got problems because the world is full of people who contradict this theory (some of them living healthy, disease free and long lives without ever sitting in an ice bath or eating fish head smoothies). I think it's important to realise (at least for me) that I will never have proof. I have an n = 1. But I cannot split myself into two and follow myself on two different paths and then compare the results. How does the poem go? Two roads emerged in the woods... and I took the one less travelled by. Something like that. But you can't take both roads.

There is a boatload of epigenetic literature out there that shows correlation between emotional health, relational well being and positive outcomes. There are many roads to soothing what ails us. I happen to believe Dr Kruse gives us an essential (and missing) piece. But there is more than just this, no matter how big this is.

When I face the demons in me like the ones you are facing, it is usually because part of me agrees with the person who is doubting me. As I said before, I think it's like parenting. There are many ways to get a 'well behaved' child. But what is under the surface, in the heart, mind and soul of that child, cannot be seen in behaviour.

Instead of rescuing you with solutions or trying to figure out why, try living with the fact that you will never have the certitude you desire. I am admittedly prone to the woo-woo that you dislike, but certitude is an illusion. You are in control of only so much in life. Boulder may be the promised land, but you cannot protect yourself from every possible bad thing that may befall you, no matter how smart you are. And bad things are rarely just bad.

I believe we all need to learn to live with our worries and fears. It doesn't mean they have to be our guidance system, but to reject them means they pop back up stronger (and bite you in the estrogen-depleted a\*\*). What if you never get below 200 pounds? Can you live with that? Are there certain ways that you could live with it (eg I feel healthy, energetic, happy, etc) or is a deal-breaker to your happiness? If it's a deal-breaker, why? These are your questions and only your answers matter.

### bigknitwit — 2012-10-15T23:33:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58403>

Sorry to butt in here, but I have to say I just love reading your posts!!

I started LR while hypoT, and gradually was increasing my thyroid meds, while staing strict with LR and CT. I lost weight. Gradually over the course of 5 months I dropped off the last 20 pounds of weight, and I was at goal. "Wow!" I said, that LR sure does great things! Never mind I could barely walk because I couldn't handle the top quartile thyroid dose (sure was happy to lie down being thin though lol!). At the beginning of August I had to make a dramatic shift to my meds which meant almost no T3, and then a month later a twinge of T3. In the past 2 months I'm 13 pounds up, and still climbing, while epi-paleo. Actually the past week I've started to transgress. If the weight is going on anyway, might as well eat some of these apples (and applesauce), and other transgressions, because until my hormones are better, it seems like a losing battle...

### indigogirl — 2012-10-16T00:45:15+0300

Also, in the scheme of questioning everything...Jack lost 100+ lbs. because he fine tuned everything based on HIS needs. I think we all need to remember that the Epi-Paleo Rx is a starting point. We each need to figure out what works for us and that can be very hard work. Have you read Nora Gegaudas? IIRC she also thinks calories are important.

> What if your friend is not wrecking herself? What if she is going to lose weight, keep it off and live a happy life? Does that invalidate this way of thinking? If it does, then we've all got problems because the world is full of people who contradict this theory (some of them living healthy, disease free and long lives without ever sitting in an ice bath or eating fish head smoothies). I think it's important to realise (at least for me) that I will never have proof. I have an n = 1. But I cannot split myself into two and follow myself on two different paths and then compare the results. How does the poem go? Two roads emerged in the woods... and I took the one less travelled by. Something like that. But you can't take both roads.
>
>
>
> There is a boatload of epigenetic literature out there that shows correlation between emotional health, relational well being and positive outcomes. There are many roads to soothing what ails us. I happen to believe Dr Kruse gives us an essential (and missing) piece. But there is more than just this, no matter how big this is.
>
>
>
> When I face the demons in me like the ones you are facing, it is usually because part of me agrees with the person who is doubting me. As I said before, I think it's like parenting. There are many ways to get a 'well behaved' child. But what is under the surface, in the heart, mind and soul of that child, cannot be seen in behaviour.
>
>
>
> Instead of rescuing you with solutions or trying to figure out why, try living with the fact that you will never have the certitude you desire. I am admittedly prone to the woo-woo that you dislike, but certitude is an illusion. You are in control of only so much in life. Boulder may be the promised land, but you cannot protect yourself from every possible bad thing that may befall you, no matter how smart you are. And bad things are rarely just bad.
>
>
>
> I believe we all need to learn to live with our worries and fears. It doesn't mean they have to be our guidance system, but to reject them means they pop back up stronger (and bite you in the estrogen-depleted a\*\*). What if you never get below 200 pounds? Can you live with that? Are there certain ways that you could live with it (eg I feel healthy, energetic, happy, etc) or is a deal-breaker to your happiness? If it's a deal-breaker, why? These are your questions and only your answers matter.

After reading all that I think BG 15 might interest you more than I thought.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-58476>

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-20T17:06:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-59334>

208.4, 40.4 - Dissssapointingly high since I've been really tight this week. I did have a consult last night. I'm probably inflamed from that.

ok, nobody tempt me with anything interesting. I won't check. I won't check the internetz. I will work.

So, one thing that I realized ISN'T in fine form is my focus/concentration. My smarts seems to be on the right track (nowhere near as good as pre baby, but what can ya do. I traded smart for love) but I've had a lot of trouble focusing lately. By focus I mean focus on things like work and projects I committed to for other people.

So, I'm behind.

So today I'm locked IN THE OFFICE until my mountains of contracts and real estate paperwork is done well. My office does have a giant picture window and overlooks the mountains, so it's not as dreary as it sounds. I bet there is fancy coating on the windows that block out the good lights.

I really would rather compose emails to some of you, and spend the day cooking and doing optimal things, but I must work. In RE our weeks are backwards. Weekends are for me to work. Hubby doesn't go into work till evening even, suffering the health consequenses of crazy schedules SO I CAN WORK ON THE WEEKENDS.

This is me, working.

Sometimes, my entrepreneurial spirit is on vacation. Did you take it? Probably it's a mental disorder (entrepreneurialism) and all this fish is curing it. What a catch-22.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-30T16:36:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-60927>

Thanks all for reading my Journal!!!! Really, I'm a realist and realize you are feeding some sick narcissism - but it makes me happy so there!

I've gotten several PM's about why I'm not writing much.

I wanted to start my own website, and figure I'll keep my journal on there - along with my essays that will be a book in the future. (hopefully).

The problem is this is a time consuming undertaking, and I'm a hard core INTJ and I have to not only plan the perfect long range plan to take over the world, and every detail and have in mind exactly how perfect it must be and can't let anyone see it until it's a pefect work of brilliance.

As with most of my plans to take over the world, I can't get past the need to have it perfect enough and just have it be good enough so I never get very far with it - because who can spend that kind of time on a vanity project?!

I'm tryin to talk myself into JUST DO IT ALREADY AND START WRITING THERE EVEN IF THE FORMAT ISN'T PERFECT. But alas, I need to show SOME level of professionalism. I am mentally goin to try and strike an uncomfortable balance with that and perhaps find good enough. I'll let ya know when, or I'll start keeping track here again. I learned long ago not to make promises on these things as once an INTJ makes a promise - by god they will make it happen, even if they have to slaughter their family to do it.

### ealachan — 2012-10-30T17:04:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-60935>

eta: also i find it interesting that the website automatically blocks out "s.h.i.t.", even if it's followed by an e. They're watching me. They know what i'm trying to do. WHERE IS MAH TINFOIL HAT?!

### Lyndra — 2012-10-30T17:55:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-60947>

Just do it! If you have an idea for a book (or website), then just get going and write. You'll never know what's down that path until you take it. Tear it up, delete it if you think it's junk but first let it settle for a while before you evaluate. I'm taking an introductory pastel art class and am wavering between having fun and spending all my time telling myself I'm no good at this. Only the rare bird is good at something at first effort; everybody else has to toil away to improve. Just do it!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T17:42:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61301>

I wrote this on another thread - but wanted it here for posterity.

I realized last night, that the long standing pleasure pathways seem to simply lie dormant without use - but they are fully capable of hopping right back on and light up the brain with a passion.

2 cheetoes and a mini reeses cup went in my mouth - it was an experiment really, brought on my smelling that orange pumpkin full of halloween crap. I had sniffed - and BOOM, brain lit up. After having the little amount of the cheetoes and reeses, I thought I was going to have an oxytocin release the pleasure was so deep and strong. It was almost MORE pleasurable to my brain than in the past when I would have that "treat" with regularity.

Thankfully there was no diet coke handy, I'm pretty sure that would have been the final trigger for a full on binge. I love the diet cokes more than anything.

So, this a.m., weight was DOWN, blood sugar was fine, mood was good and I went strait to my Fat only coffee and I have zero hunger and expect to coast through to Noonish before eating anything other than fat.

What will be interesting is seeing if I have the "down" later to go with the "high" from last night.

Ultimately, it wasn't something I would want to repeat, even if harmless - as STOPPING at the few bites was nearly impossible. I literally went to bed to read and made my husband hide the candy etc.

In case you were wondering. . . .Caveboy got to eat 2 items from the booty while hanging with his friends and counting their take, and the rest goes to the candy for soldiers drive.

His choices were Cheetos (thus promptin the above) and skittles. I was shocked he didn't do chocolate!! He said that "I can have chocolate anytime! - we let him have 99%. . .lovely.

### Souldanzer — 2012-11-01T18:51:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61314>

Very interesting your observation. The longer I abstain from "junk" I used to binge on the more "non-food" it tastes to me. There is one exception and that is grain products but then maybe they don't fall under "junk" necessarily. Anything made with pure sugar - ugh. Way too sweet, way too artificial, way too sticky and yuck.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-01T19:04:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61321>

I'm sorry you have that reaction as it must make things really really tough for you. I've never really thought much about it but I guess I just never had a binge problem unless I was hooked on wheat. Which I was for a brief moment.

Nowadays I look at that stuff and just go 'huh' and walk away. I honest-to-god just don't want it. Chocolate? I have about 6 bars of the 90% stuff in my "hoard box" and it's been there for months. I might eat one little square now and then but it takes me ages to finish it. I even forgot I had a bit lying on the table the other night.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-01T19:10:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61326>

> **Anna K.;63056 wrote:** I feel very similar to you, I can bypass all the candies and \*\*\*\*ty chocolates very easily, but a tasty freshly baked bread or a croissant is a different story. If you put infront of me it takes a lot of will power not to eat and most of the time I fail

That's because wheat is LITERALLY addictive like cocaine or heroin. The protein in wheat crosses the blood/brain barrier and attaches at opiate receptor sites. If you eat wheat you most likely will consume, on average, about 400 calories a day more than you would otherwise.

I have banned it from my house for over a year and I don't miss it. There's better things to eat. And I always remind myself of what Jack said (somewhere around here) ... and I'm paraphrasing ... there's eating for entertainment and then there's eating for sustenance.

Eating for entertainment isn't, in and of itself, a bad thing unless you do it all the time. It should be reserved for treat status. Like once in a blue moon. But even on my birthday I don't want cake anymore unless it flourless. And sugarless.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T19:12:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61327>

I would love if more and more people would weigh in on their level of pleasure from old happy foods.

I think the pleasure pathways are as relevant a factor as food, and perhaps moreso significant to some types of obesity, particularly those that become super obese and/or obese from a young age

Those of you that have binge eating issues (interestingly, I don't think I do), what I read isn't that the foods are so pleasurable, that it's a host of other things going on - but I would love to hear from you on this. Bingers comment?

Jack, I know you have no problem with pleasure issues - but then again, you were not super obese for all of life either. . .so you may be wired completely differently.

How many brains have you been in (physically) of someone super fat from youth?

MOAR COMMENTS PLESE

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T19:18:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61329>

I don't think mine is a binge or wheat issue exactly. . .it's like the combo of "cheetos, reeses and diet coke" specifically (and unrelated to micro nutrients etc) have wired to become a deep and profound pleasure trigger.

What I'm wondering is if our brains have a set number of these pleasure things, if all your pleasure mojo is on the cheeto road - does that mean other roads that other people seem to have (sports love) atrophy or simply don't exist?

I know in theory these paths can be changed - but it's interesting as I've probably not given much thought, or ate those foods for 2 years at least, yet I easily could have charged a battery had I been holding one. MY pathway was in no way degraded.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T19:20:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61330>

Also, I didn't make the connection with the other bliss/happy thread until right at this moment.

I've been wondering why I don't feel the bliss like Inger and others seem to report - and now I realize I do. I felt it from eating a 2 cheetos and mini reeses. After not doing it for YEARS.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-01T19:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61332>

Another slightly scary for the general public TMI thing. . .my husband saw all my printouts for the Paramedic program. I've been working on trying to find SOMETHING other than food that makes me happy feeling. I keep making my dream list. . the one thing that pops out that seems like something I could do in reality (lets face it, surgeon isn't gonna happen at this point in my life) is become a paramedic.

So I'm going to start down that program. Don't know if I will finish or where it will take me. I'm doing this for MY PLEASURE. Or the hope it will provide me with some.

My husband looked at me and said, this is pandoras box. . . once you get your hands on someone you are going to turn into some mad scientist experimenting on people in vacant mountain houses.

### ealachan — 2012-11-01T19:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61335>

I'm trying to think if there's any foods I eat that actually give me like super-happy blissed-out feelings, and I keep coming up with stuff like crispy pork fat/skin, crispy chicken skin, fat from a steak that has a good sear to it, hell even salmon skin that's cooked nice and crisp. It seems to be that fat + crispy + salty = bliss for me.

Interestingly, I have problems making myself stop eating ice cream, candy or baked goods if I cave and taste them, but they do NOT give me the OMGHAPPY that fattycrispysalty stuff does. They definitely taste good, but it feels much more like a compulsion, like an addiction. With the fatty crispy stuff, I don't feel out of control. I like it, I eat it, it makes me happy, and then I move on. With sugary stuff, if I eat it, it makes me kinda happy but then I want more, more, more, and I can't move on. Easier to just not eat it.

### Inger — 2012-11-01T19:51:09+0200

Cavemam..lol

Look, I have been addicted to food too. Very much so. Addicted to the taste of the comfort foods. But it all had to go. I knew it did me no good. They might give you a short kind of happiness but it was not the real thing. It was fake and short lived and had painful concequences.

You know what? Happiness is kind of sacred.. It is like a paradox to me. I never look for happiness. It would not work, not for me. I often experienced the greatest happines when I was the most broken. That is about doing the right things. It can be so hard. Excruciating. But just do it.. and the diamond will emerge.. and one feels like..wow.

It was, as they say, my first time. As I cut into the fruit I inhaled the fragrance which was exotic and erotic all at the same time. As I peeled it, my hands became slippery from all the juices dripping across my fingers and down my arm. I took a bite and found myself licking the juice from my fingers and hands. ### KiwiLauren — 2012-11-01T22:12:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61399>

> **Cavemam;63061 wrote:**
>
> How many brains have you been in (physically) of someone super fat from youth?
>
>
>
> MOAR COMMENTS PLESE

Mam, my take on this is that you're talking about pleasure networks that were wired up very early, before verbal ability and certainly before explicit memory (hence, they are stored only as body memories, called implicit memories - these are memories that cannot be recognised as memory by the cognitive brain). There are many studies that have attempted to unravel what is happening in this preverbal/preconscious memory time, both from a relational perspective as well as from a physiological one. In some ways, many of our strongest motivations remain unconscious to us throughout our life. Yes, all pathways can be rewired, but some of the literature suggests that getting access to these pathways (many formed when very early brain wiring was taking place) can be very difficult... and certainly is not accessed via our 'thoughts.'

### granitereno@gmail.com — 2012-11-02T15:23:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61519>

I'll bite.

I guess that I have never really outed myself before as a binger. But that is clearly me. I am good... until I'm not.

I took the wee ones out trick-or-treating the other night and came back with the customary 55 gallon drums full of high-fructose-sweetened-condensed-frosted-franken\*\*\*\*.

And I was an oak.

I didn't touch any of it. (Except to throw some of it out and to start some of the "buy back" negotiations with kids: will swap out candy for lesser evils...)

But the yesterday morning I had to get labs done --fasted.

I used to IF all the time, but I guess that I have grown accustomed to the BAB.

So afterward, I proceeded to snack all day.

I got home and somehow a little twix bar got in my mouth.

and I KNOW it is \*\*\*\*... but it tasted like angel (grassfed angel!)

So I proceeded to unhinge my jaw and shovel fistfulls of this "fun-sized" poison into my face.

super proud of myself...

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-11-04T03:07:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/no-really-im-in-a-tub-of-icewater-and-documenting-on-the-interwebs-by-cavemam.1192/post-61898>

Ate mostly fat most of the day, until I tasted the tomato soup. Then I had a bowl of soup and one of the grilled cheese sammys.

I had 5 oysters left in the fridge, couldn't bring myself to eat them.

I had 3 boys over tonight, and I took them slot car racing and then they played and I fed them tomato soup and "grilled cheese" made with the fat bread. I was so happy, they ate it up yum!

At the end, I was going to make their mom a care-package (she's going to pop any day now with twins) but as I looked at the beautiful Jar of tomato soup, I thought "they are just going to leave this sitting in the fridge because it's not campbells from a can". So I kept it. I did give her the funniest motherhood book I've seen in forever called. . S h i t t y mom. Really some good stuff in there.
